1.intent: Synthesize Zenoh-compatible networking stack from SCXML + SCE Forge as single source of truth; two interoperable backends.
outputs_bullets: AP backend: Rust, std, tokio runtime, Linux x86_64 / aarch64
MCU backend: C11, no_std-equivalent, no heap; SoC TBD until deploy authoring
Wire-interoperability with upstream zenohd for the sec 2 documented subset
caveats_bullets: Not a rewrite of zenoh/zenoh-pico; demonstration that SCE synthesizes industrial wire protocols.

10.intent: Per-binding QoS configuration sourced from deploy.yaml and emitted as runtime structs (AP) or static const tables (MCU).

10/10.1.intent: deploy.yaml QoS — per-binding priority/reliability/express/congestion_control declared in machine binding block; typed config consumed by codegen.

10/10.2.intent: QoS config codegen — AP emits Rust const QosConfig (data section); MCU emits C const struct in .rodata (Flash, no RAM cost).

11.intent: Multi-layer testing strategy: byte-equivalence parity, wire replay, upstream interop, no-alloc guard, drift detection, fuzz.

11/11.1.intent: Cross-backend parity gate — every algorithm/codec with sce:test-vector runs on both backends and is byte-diffed; gate on merge.

11/11.2.intent: Wire replay tests — pcap captures from upstream zenoh peers replayed into generated stack with declared expected event sequence.

11/11.3.intent: Upstream interop CI — dockerized zenohd + AP node + MCU host-mode build (lwIP shim, renode/QEMU later) asserting full handshake + pub/sub roundtrip.

11/11.4.intent: MCU heap prohibition enforced as five layers — direct trap, linker wrap, libc pin, call-graph reachability, exception ban — not runtime trap alone.
rationale_bullets: Layer 1 direct trap: malloc/calloc/realloc/free stubs emit __builtin_trap on call.
Layer 2 linker --wrap: -Wl,--wrap on alloc symbols routes libc-internal indirect calls to traps.
Layer 3 libc pin: newlib-nano or picolibc tiny stdio prevents allocating printf path.
Layer 4 call-graph: post-link analyzer rejects any reach from out/mcu to deny-listed allocator.
Layer 5 exception ban: -fno-exceptions/-fno-rtti/-fno-unwind-tables blocks libstdc++ machinery.

11/11.5.intent: Drift gate: every emitted file carries source-hash + template-hash; sce-build verify recomputes and fails on mismatch; manual edits to out/ forbidden.
rationale_bullets: Per-file header carries source-hash + template-hash (sha256 over inputs and sce-build binary).
sce-build verify recomputes both hashes and fails the gate on mismatch (CI + pre-commit).
Fixed artifact set: out/{ap,mcu}, Cargo.toml, CMakeLists.txt, linker fragments, sce_sourcemap.json.
Manual edits to out/ forbidden: path is RFC or sce:extern; exceptions tracked with expiry date.
sce_sourcemap.json hash mismatch surfaces as traceability/sourcemap-source-hash-mismatch.

11/11.6.intent: Adversarial input fuzz testing on generated codec/parsers; deferred Phase D+ work; complements §11.1 cross-backend parity.

12.intent: Generated AP/MCU code dependency surface: SCE crates, runtime crates, intrinsics, and user-provided platform pieces.

12/12.1.intent: Generated code dependency surface — AP Rust pulls sce_core/forge_runtime/link_runtime_tokio/intrinsics + bytes/tokio/heapless; MCU C pulls forge_runtime_c/link_runtime_lwip/intrinsics + lwIP/HAL.

12/12.2.intent: Runtime crate versioning — SemVer; generated code pins exact minor at build time via Cargo.toml/CMakeLists constraint to avoid SSoT drift between codegen and runtime.

12/12.3.intent: External user-provided surface — MCU consumer provides SoC HAL/lwIP config/master linker script; AP binary ships self-contained.

13.intent: Pre-SCE prose and tooling work: wire spec, pcap corpus, deploy skeletons, FSM sketches, meta-source generator (RFC 5.G workaround).

14.intent: Living-doc maintenance notes: when Phase A lands, what sections to revise and how to track architecture-vs-code drift.

15.intent: Placeholder for observability stack (logging, metrics, tracing); decisions deferred per section 14 living-doc notes.

16.intent: Placeholder for performance targets (OPEN handshake latency on MCU, AP TX throughput); awaits first benchmark numbers.

2.intent: Scope partition: MVP (zenoh-pico parity), Deferred (with migration path), Permanent constraints, Extensibility invariants, Protocol pinning.

2/2.0.intent: Long-term migration vision (every Zenoh feature eventually lands) + MVP criterion (full zenoh-pico parity, peer+client both backends).

2/2.1.intent: MVP scope table — peer+client modes, full message set, 5 transports, QoS dims, bounded KeyExpr matching, fragmentation, both targets, zero-copy posture.

2/2.2.intent: Phase D+ deferred items (router mode, BLE/Raweth, API shim, shared-mem, admin/scripting) each carries an explicit migration path; not permanently excluded.

2/2.3.intent: MCU-class permanent constraints (no router, no aggregation, no heap, no scripting, no unbounded KeyExpr, no dynamic loading); AP unaffected.

2/2.4.intent: Six extensibility invariants: static-first, link-extensible, kinds-additive, library-not-monolith, platform gating, out/ SSoT.
rationale_bullets: Static-first, dynamic-opt-in: every MCU-static feature should admit an AP-dynamic variant.
Link drivers extensible: open set via target plugin, not closed enum.
Kinds additive: ForgeKind set grows; existing kinds remain stable across versions.
Generated code exports as library, not monolith: out/ap and out/mcu are library targets.
Platform gating only when physically necessary; keep pool/link/worker kinds platform-neutral.
out/ is SSoT-downstream: manual edits forbidden; new features land via SCE extensions.

2/2.5.intent: Zenoh 1.x wire format pinned via deploy.yaml protocol block; additive extensions allowed within 1.x, 2.x is a separate scope decision.

3.intent: Four design invariants: zero-runtime abstraction, location transparency, strict SSoT, hardware-aware codegen.

3/3.1.intent: Zero-Runtime Abstraction — no vtables, no dynamic dispatch in the hot path; all bindings resolve at build time to direct calls (SCE AOT philosophy carried through).

3/3.2.intent: Location Transparency — SCXML uses logical identities; physical transport realization and addressing live in deploy.yaml; same SCXML deploys to AP or MCU.

3/3.3.intent: Strict SSoT — wire format / algorithms / FSMs defined once in SCXML+Forge; AP and MCU are descendants; cross-backend drift is a generator bug, not expected.

3/3.4.intent: Hardware-Aware Codegen — MCU code integrates with target SoC capabilities (SRAM placement, DMA pairing, alignment) declared via deploy.yaml; AP/MCU share no memory.

4.intent: Mapping of SCE existing capabilities, RFC-requested extensions, and watching-zenoh authoring scope.

4/4.1.intent: SCE existing surface — SCXML parser/IR/W3C algos, expression transpiler, 11 Forge kinds, multi-language codegen, no_std Rust runtime, diagnostics + composition.

4/4.2.intent: SCE extensions requested via RFC — new kinds (algorithm/link/buffer-pool/worker/bounded-collection), codec DSL, fragment pattern, C11 backend, no_std statechart, sce:extern, deploy fields.

4/4.3.intent: Project authoring scope — SCXML statecharts, Forge codec/algorithm/bounded-collection/link/pool/worker sources, deploy.yaml variants, runtime crates, optional API shim.

5.intent: Cargo workspace structure: out/ap library, crates/watching_zenoh_{api,bin}, plugin extension path.

6.intent: Build stages from SCXML+SCE through codegen, out/, cargo, to AP/MCU artifacts; per-target artifact list.

6/6.1.intent: Build pipeline stages — sce-codegen single-invocation parse/validate/IR/typecheck/const-fold/emit/sidecar driving Cargo + CMake builds.

6/6.2.intent: Per-target artifacts — AP gets src/Cargo.toml/build.rs; MCU gets headers/sources/CMake/linker fragment with linker-flavor matrix (GNU LD default, scatter/ICF/os-managed via target plugin).

7.intent: Runtime layout: AP backend (Rust+tokio), MCU backend (C11+lwIP), shared runtime crates/headers.

7/7.1.intent: AP backend stack — generated code on top of sce_core + sce_link_runtime_tokio + Rust std/bytes/tokio; tokio::spawn workers, mpsc channels, BytesMut pools.

7/7.2.intent: MCU stack — generated code over sce_forge_runtime_c + sce_link_runtime_lwip; cooperative tick scheduler with WCET budget; static pools + DMA descriptors.

7/7.3.intent: Shared runtime crates/headers — sce_core (AP only), sce_forge_runtime (Rust+C variants), sce_link_runtime_{tokio,lwip}, sce_intrinsics_runtime (whitelisted).

8.intent: Wire format layering: scouting/transport/network/zenoh/extensions; FSM, codec, link layers.

8/8.1.intent: Wire format stack layer diagram — Application -> Session FSM -> Network FSM -> Codec -> Link -> OS/HAL; bottom-up data flow on RX, top-down on TX.

8/8.2.intent: Session FSM sketch — Init/Scouting/Opening/Established/Closing path authored as session_unicast/multicast.scxml; full model lives in docs/session-fsm.md.

8/8.3.intent: Codec layer — one SCXML file per message type using RFC §5.B extensions (flags/vle/present-if/len-prefix/TLV); test vectors drive cross-backend parity.

8/8.4.intent: Link layer — kind=link SCXML files (UDP unicast/multicast/TCP/Serial/WS) with framer + RX-pool + backpressure declared; driver and address bound via deploy.yaml.

9.intent: Zero-copy: TX always zero-copy; RX happy path zero-copy; oversized/fragment stage-copy; pool placement; platform-aware link.

9/9.1.intent: TX path zero-copy — Codec encodes directly into pool slot, link send-from-slot, DMA reads via prebuilt descriptor; pool slot in DMA-coherent SRAM.

9/9.2.intent: RX happy path zero-copy — DMA writes pre-programmed RX slot; codec parses in place; event emitted with slot borrow; slot returned to freelist.

9/9.3.intent: Stage-copy RX path for oversized/multi-DMA — one copy DMA-slot to stage; build-time fragmentation analysis; stage_copy_policy warn/error/forbid per machine.

9/9.4.intent: Pool placement across .dtcm/.sram1/.flash regions; per-pool placement declared in kind source; memory regions in deploy.yaml; linker fragment ties them.

9/9.5.intent: Platform-aware link substrate: 5-row matrix (MCU bare_metal, linux+epoll, linux+io_uring, QNX, etc) sharing pool FSM.

architecture--watching-zenoh.intent: Pre-implementation design document for watching-zenoh (Zenoh stack synthesized from SCE), blocked on SCE Phase A.
caveats_bullets: Status: Pre-implementation (2026-04-24); blocked on SCE Phase A landing.
Becomes living reference updated in lockstep with code once SCE Phase A lands.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface.intent: Defines sce_intrinsics_runtime symbol surface (C11/Rust variants) for atomics/fences/cache/IRQ/RNG/HMAC/HW-sem.
inputs_bullets: RFC sec 5.I sce:extern whitelist — atomics, fences, cache maintenance, IRQ control
RFC sec 5.J.2 — statechart no_std HAL trait shape (ticks/wake/irq-save)
RFC sec 5.K target_plugin extension — per-SoC HMAC + HW semaphores out of core whitelist
session-fsm sec 2.7 G-SFM-5 — HMAC + RNG primitive ownership question (OQ-W15)
reassembly-fsm sec 6 — cache maintenance pinning (sec 5.E pool FSM call sites)
ARCHITECTURE sec 9.5 5-row matrix — same symbol set, different bodies per row
outputs_bullets: Categorized symbol table marking core whitelist vs target plugin per category
sec 5.J.2 statechart no_std HAL trait mapped onto these symbols
Cross-reference into RFC sec 5.I diagnostic list for authoring violation traceability
caveats_bullets: OQ-W14 (HW-sem standardization) and OQ-W15 (HMAC+RNG ownership) gate final shape.
Initial proposal: option 2 for HMAC (target plugin), option 1 for RNG (core whitelist).
Non-outputs: symbol body implementations, per-SoC HMAC accelerator binding.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-1-a.intent: Atomics core whitelist — per-width (u8-u64+usize) load/store/cas/fetch_* with explicit memory ordering encoded in the symbol name; no integer ordering parameter.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-2-f.intent: Fences core whitelist — atomic_fence_{acquire/release/acq_rel/seq_cst}, compiler_barrier (no runtime cost), dma_fence (DSB on ARMv7-M+, no-op on linux).

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-3-c.intent: Cache maintenance core whitelist on MCU, no-op on AP linux (kernel-coherent); emitted only on §5.E pool lifecycle FSM edges; direct <sce:call> rejected.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-4-i.intent: IRQ control core whitelist (optional) — irq_save/irq_restore; only emitted for workers with sce:critical-section (RFC §5.D, Phase C+); MVP deploys do not use these.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-5-r.intent: RNG target plugin row (OQ-W15 (a) Q1 ratified Round 11: core whitelist rejected; sce_random_fill consumed by stateless_accept cookie HMAC key gen).
caveats_bullets: Q1 = No ratified Round 11: SCE BASELINE_SYMBOLS boundary is architecture-fixed primitives only.
Impl multiplicity (HW TRNG / ADC+Yarrow / getrandom / arc4random) places RNG in plugin tier.
Fallback validated by SCE c13_beta_antiflood tests (baseline ∪ plugin lookup, 4570a389).
Counter-offer tracked as OQ-W24 (RFC §5.I tier-explicit separation, boundary hardening).
R62 cookie key currently caller-supplied static bytes (no RNG fill yet)
R70b carry: AP-side getrandom integration for SigningKey::new entropy

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-6-h.intent: HMAC target plugin (OQ-W15 (a) Q2 ratified Round 11: per-SoC accelerator selection; sce_hmac_sha256 consumed by listener links with untrusted_source).
caveats_bullets: Q2 = Yes ratified Round 11: proposal target_plugin path confirmed by SCE maintainer.
Per-SoC accelerator surface (STM32H7 CRYP / ESP32 SHA / Cortex-M0+ SW) keeps baseline crypto-free.
SW fallback authorable as algorithm kind; wire output identical across backings.
R62 AP path uses RustCrypto hmac+sha2 (std-coupled, vendored)
R70 SigningKey newtype + zeroize-on-drop wraps the secret key bytes
RFC 4231 TC1+TC2 byte-equiv pinned in wz-runtime-tokio unit tests (R70)
Phase W (MCU) swaps to sce_intrinsics_runtime::hmac_sha256 per RFC §5.I

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-7-h.intent: HW semaphores/mailboxes target plugin (OQ-W14) — sce_hw_sem_*/sce_hw_mbox_*; required at multi-core MCU bring-up (Phase C cross-core); single-core MVP omits.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-sym.intent: Symbol surface partition into 7 categories (atomics/fences/cache/IRQ/RNG/HMAC/HW-sem) marked core or target-plugin; OQ-W15/W14 gate unresolved cells.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/3-whi.intent: Whitelist policy 2x2 matrix — core if every platform has body AND choice is not deploy-meaningful; target plugin if author/vendor needs to pick between non-interchangeable bodies.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/4-dep.intent: deploy.yaml extern_symbols block (RFC §5.K) names runtime crate variant + target plugin; activates 3-tuple per RFC §5.J.3; three concrete deploy states (mcu_target/ap_standalone/ap_mcu_pair).

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/5-1-t.intent: StatechartHal trait (Rust pseudocode) — now_us monotonic tick microseconds, wake cooperative-scheduler hint, irq_save/restore (Phase C+ critical section).

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/5-2-m.intent: HAL method body mapping per platform — now_us via DWT/SysTick on MCU vs Instant::now on AP linux; wake via worker_wake or tokio reactor; irq_save/restore via §2.4 or empty.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/5-3-t.intent: HAL trait belongs in sce_intrinsics_runtime (statechart concern), not sce_link_runtime_* (I/O concern); both consumers share one symbol surface as single dependency.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/5-no-.intent: RFC §5.J.2 statechart no_std HAL trait — provided by sce_intrinsics_runtime, bottoms out on §2 symbols; trait shape MCU-class per RFC §5.J.4.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/6-sel.intent: Self-review against ARCHITECTURE §2.4 six invariants — every row PASS; symbol surface identical across platforms with body-elided per platform-class (same shape, different body).

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/7-nex.intent: Next-step scaffolding when Phase A SCE codegen lands — sce_intrinsics_runtime_c skeleton, _rust skeleton, target_extensions_stm32h747.yaml; OQ-W23 deferral noted.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/8-cha.intent: Change log — 2026-05-01 initial draft (third of three runtime-crate sibling docs; symbol surface partitioned into 7 categories; OQ-W15 (a) initial proposal locked in).

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Two binary ratification decisions on sce_intrinsics_runtime_* whitelist membership for RNG and HMAC primitives, individually decidable but coupled by shared consumer.
inputs_bullets: Shared consumer: stateless_accept: cookie_hmac_sha256 (RFC §5.K hard error if absent)
Decisions are individually decidable — RNG location does not constrain HMAC location
outputs_bullets: Decision 1: sce_random_fill(buf, len) -> int → core whitelist (yes/no)
Decision 2: sce_hmac_sha256(key, key_len, msg, msg_len, out) → target plugin (yes/no)

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Phase A entry of public-Internet-facing MCU listeners is blocked until both stateless_accept primitives have ratified placement.
rationale_bullets: RFC §5.K stateless_accept required when domain_attrs.untrusted_source: true (build refusal)
stateless_accept body uses sce_hmac_sha256 (cookie HMAC) and sce_random_fill (key generation)
Both symbols must be placed (whitelist or plugin) before SCXML can author stateless_accept
inputs_bullets: Exposure class: domain_attrs.untrusted_source flag on listener link
deploy/stateless-accept-required-on-untrusted-source — hard error if missing
outputs_bullets: Public-Internet-facing MCU listener Phase A blocked pending placement decision
Private-LAN MCU listener Phase A not gated (caps alone suffice)

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Watching-zenoh proposes RNG to core whitelist (universal entropy primitive) and HMAC to target plugin (per-SoC accelerator selection).
rationale_bullets: RNG universal — every platform has some entropy source (HW RNG / ADC noise / OS getrandom)
RNG no SoC-specific accelerator selection — bundled with HAL, single canonical symbol
RNG WCET cleanly handled by canonical symbol with measured bound, no per-deploy redeclaration
HMAC HW-accelerator selection genuinely per-SoC (CRYP/SHA/SW fallback)
HMAC SW fallback authorable as algorithm kind (option 3 fallback path)
HMAC bounded blast radius — keeps whitelist small, contains crypto precedent
inputs_bullets: Decision questions from §1 of this doc (RNG location / HMAC location)

alternatives_rejected.reason: loses universality, every public-Internet deploy ships plugin
not viable, entropy generation is platform-coupled
adds 32-byte crypto, sets precedent for more crypto primitives
loses HW accelerator, ~10x CPU on STM32H7, tight WCET headroom

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Detailed counter-option matrix for the two decisions, expanding the trade-offs that §3 cites in summary form.

alternatives_rejected.reason: every deploy ships entropy plugin even when platform is canonical
not viable; entropy is irreducibly platform-coupled
HW dispatch survives but precedent for more crypto additions set
zero plugin dep but HW unused, ~10x CPU, tighter burst headroom

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Forward-compatibility implications across the 2x2 matrix of RNG and HMAC placement decisions.
rationale_bullets: RNG core (proposal): RNG features land free; missing entropy → build-time refusal
HMAC plugin (proposal): future crypto land by precedent; whitelist stays small
HMAC plugin (proposal): SoC-vendor crypto IP changes absorbed without RFC churn
RNG plugin alt: duplicated authoring per canonical deploy; each new RNG re-states it
HMAC core alt: faster Phase A on listener MCUs; precedent for crypto whitelist adds
caveats_bullets: R62-R70 AP-side HMAC land (RustCrypto std-coupled) ratifies OQ-W15(a) Q2 plugin path
SigningKey newtype + zeroize: Phase W MCU swap MUST preserve drop-time wipe semantics

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Ratification ask: yes/no on each of the two proposal decisions, with optional counter-direction and audit artifact in the OQ-W15 entry.
inputs_bullets: Initial proposal (this doc) as ratification subject
Counter-option matrix as alternatives if proposal rejected
outputs_bullets: Ratify or reject proposal (yes/no on each of two decisions)
If reject: counter-direction from matrix or proposed independently
Decision artifact: short note in rfc-open-questions-log.md OQ-W15 entry
caveats_bullets: OQ-W15 (b) MCU/AP defaults are out of scope here; settle post-Phase-A via HIL measurement
Decision Outcome (Round 11, 2026-05-14): Q1 = No, Q2 = Yes ratified by SCE maintainer.
Q1 rationale: BASELINE_SYMBOLS = architecture-fixed primitives only; RNG → plugin tier.
Q2 rationale: per-SoC accelerator selection genuinely plugin-meaningful (proposal aligned).
Counter-offer (tier separation) tracked as OQ-W24; spec restructure deferred to next round.
Fallback path validated (SCE C13-β tests, 4570a389); deploys unblock via 4-line plugin row.

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Citation index linking this artifact to its source material in the watching-zenoh internal docs.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/1-framing-o.intent: Reassembly FSM between codec (Fragment) and network FSMs; chain key=(peer_zid, reliability) — two buffers per peer per upstream wire shape.
inputs_bullets: Chain key (peer_zid 16B, reliability 1bit); no priority, no sn_base in key
Wire: Fragment {sn:u64 VLE, payload:ZSlice, more bit, reliability bit}
FRAGMENT_FIRST/DROP exts patch-1+; legacy patch-0 infers first via empty+consecutive SN
Fragment classification: First / Continue / Final / Drop / unmatched-continue dropped at Router

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/10-next-ste.intent: Reassembly FSM unblocks Phase A SCXML authoring of sources/network/reassembly.scxml; deploy.yaml reassembly_pool block fully named; OQ-W22 closed.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-1-hierarc.intent: ReassemblyDispatcher = Router region + N parallel Slot regions (N=slot_count); one-active-per-key invariant; codegen N is build-time const.
inputs_bullets: Router: chain-key→slot-index map (bounded-collection<SlotEntry, N>), per-peer quota check
Slot[0..N-1]: parallel sub-region, four-state machine each
Upstream divergence: shared bounded pool decoupled from peer count (vs upstream inline 2×peers)
Parity invariant: slot_count ≥ 2 × peer_table.capacity reproduces upstream no-exhaustion
MCU slot_count=4, AP=32 (OQ-W8); over-commits trade strict parity for memory cost

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-2-slot-st.intent: Slot four-state FSM: Empty → Receiving → Complete / TimedOut / Aborted (three terminals funnel back to Empty via slot release).
inputs_bullets: Empty: slot free, awaiting allocation from Router
Receiving: chain in progress; consecutive Continue allowed
Complete: Final received with consecutive SN
TimedOut: reassembly_timeout_ms expired
Aborted: 7 reasons (OOO/Drop/duplicate-First/oldest-wins/codec-error/unmatched/peer-drop)
Slot release transitions buffer-pool slot cpu-mut/cpu-ref → free (lifecycle FSM funnel)

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-3-router-.intent: Router region routes Fragment.* events to Slot regions by chain key; enforces per-peer quota; refuses second First on already-open key (duplicate-first abort).

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-4-transit.intent: Eight detailed transitions across Slot states: Empty→Receiving (First), Receiving→Receiving (Continue), Final→Complete, plus 5 abort variants and Terminal→Empty release funnel.
outputs_bullets: §2.4.1 Empty→Receiving: Fragment.First admitted by Router (key not-active, quota OK, slot OK)
§2.4.2 Receiving→Receiving: Fragment.Continue with consecutive SN appends payload
§2.4.3 Receiving→Complete: Fragment.Final consecutive SN, emit message-complete event
§2.4.4 Receiving→Aborted: out-of-order Continue (non-consecutive SN, strict in-order)
§2.4.5 Receiving→TimedOut: reassembly_timeout_ms expired
§2.4.6 Receiving→Aborted: oldest-wins eviction by Router under pool pressure
§2.4.7 Receiving→Aborted: codec error on payload decode (§2.4.7a peer-drop via DROP ext)
§2.4.8 Terminal→Empty: slot release funnel; lifecycle FSM cpu-mut→free

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-5-out-of-.intent: Strict in-order policy (OQ-W21 closed): non-consecutive Continue aborts the chain (option 2 ratified); upstream consecutive-SN parity preserved.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-6-mapping.intent: Mapping prose four-state Slot FSM (Empty/Receiving/Complete/TimedOut/Aborted) to RFC §5.M three-state high-level sketch (Idle/Active/Done) with terminal split.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/3-tx-fragme.intent: TX fragmentation sibling FSM — chunks outbound NetworkMessage exceeding link MTU into Fragment.{First, Continue, Final} with consecutive SN.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/4-timer-quo.intent: Reassembly timer / per-peer quota / pool config from deploy.yaml — reassembly_timeout_ms, per_peer_quota, slot_count, slot_size live under buffer_pools.reassembly_pool.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/5-trust-cla.intent: Trust-class interaction — reassembly forbidden on untrusted/session_arming links; established_session sibling instance receives reassembly bindings (OQ-W22 close).

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/6-buffer-po.intent: Buffer-pool lifecycle FSM (§5.E) interaction — reassembly slot acquire = pool slot cpu-mut; terminal release = cpu-mut/cpu-ref → free.
caveats_bullets: R51-R52 RxFrame { bytes: Vec u8 } owned — pool-slot zero-copy not yet wired
Pool-slot Rx borrows carry: wz-runtime-tokio §4.1 io_uring fixed-buffer opt-in

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/7-1-reassem.intent: Build-time reassembly capacity check — max_fragments × per_peer_quota × peer_table.capacity sufficiency for slot_count; hard error if violated.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/7-2-stage-c.intent: Build-time stage-copy rate warning — expected_p99_bytes / mtu_bytes ratio against pool slot reuse policy; warning if ≥25%.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/7-3-reassem.intent: Build-time reassembly slot_size recommendation (informational) — slot_size ≥ max_fragments × mtu_bytes for full chain absorption.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/7-4-stage-c.intent: Build-time stage-copy WCET hard error — expected_p99_bytes × memcpy_cycles / clock_freq ≤ worker_slot_budget_us; rejects M0+ deploys with oversized stage-copy.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/8-1-authori.intent: Reassembly authoring-contract gaps (G-RFM-1..N) — most resolved via OQ-W21/W22 closure; residuals tracked for Phase D+ TX fragmentation work.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/8-2-new-ope.intent: OQ-W21 (out-of-order continue policy) closed via strict in-order; OQ-W22 (listener-link trust-class lifecycle) closed via codegen sibling split.

rfc--sce-forge-extensions-for-wire-proto.intent: New ForgeKind::Algorithm — pure synchronous functions with bounded loops, mutable locals, six-backend codegen, MCU-viable WCET.
rationale_bullets: No existing kind expresses pure synchronous functions with bounded loops and mutable locals
Procedure forces per-byte event dispatch — absurd performance for CRC/VLE
Transform is single-expression only — cannot host accumulator state
Bounded loops + no recursion guarantee termination, MCU-viable
WCET annotation extends termination guarantee to fit cooperative scheduler slot budget

RFC sec 5.C link kind + sec 5.J.3 backend 3-tuple naming + sec 5.J.4 MCU-class matrix
ARCHITECTURE sec 9.5 row 2 (linux+epoll) and row 3 (linux+io_uring fixed buffers)
OQ-W20 — keep trait surface small enough for future custom QNX-native runtime
outputs_bullets: LinkDriver trait + LinkEvent enum compile-time bindable from session_unicast/multicast SCXML
deploy.yaml links.<name>.driver enum (tokio_udp / tokio_tcp / optional tokio_uring)
io_uring fixed-buffer opt-in path reusing sec 5.E pool slot lifecycle FSM unchanged
caveats_bullets: Pre-implementation prose, design-only during Phase A-C (MCU-first invariant per RFC sec 7).
Non-outputs: Rust source, QNX runtime, SCXML authoring (Phase D.1+ blocks on Phase A).

runtime-crate--sce_link_runtime_tokio/2-.intent: Four-method LinkDriver trait — open/send/close/poll_event; OQ-W20 satisfied (no epoll/mio types exposed); QNX-native re-impl maps to dispatch_block/MsgSend.
LinkEvent enum — 6 inbound (Ready/Lost/Rx/TxDrained/Backpressure/FramingError) lifting session-fsm §6 1:1; opaque FramingDetail; LostCause variants.
RxFrame borrows pool slot per RFC §5.E Sample lifetime — no copy default, take() opt-in for owned, Rust borrow checker enforces lifetime across await; (rust,*) only kind.
Trust-class propagates to compile-time trait selection — three flavors (untrusted/session_arming/established_session); listener two-instance emission per OQ-W22.
Rust LinkDriver + LinkEvent contract. R57 INSTALLED OnceLock enforces single-FSM-per-process; multi-peer requires fork or R63 session-scoped Lua binding (deferred).
rationale_bullets: LinkDriver trait (R51): async open / send / close / poll_event — 4-method baseline
LinkEvent enum (R51): Ready / Rx(RxFrame) / Lost{cause: LostCause}
LostCause: PeerClosed / Timeout / OsError
Reliability hint: Reliable / BestEffort (per-message; UDP enforces BestEffort)
TcpDriver (R51) + UdpDriver (R52) impls; from_stream / from_socket constructors
R57 INSTALLED OnceLock guards single-FSM-per-process; 2nd install → SessionActionsAlreadyInstalled
Multi-peer FSM forks per peer until R63 session-scoped Lua binding lands
TokioLinkDriverAdapter::new asserts runtime_flavor == MultiThread (block_on deadlock guard)
R60 real-TCP integration test verifies the chain end-to-end (Engine → Lua → driver → socket)
Trust-class gating + io_uring opt-in + pool-slot Rx borrows deferred to later rounds
caveats_bullets: R60 SessionInitParams::default removed (silent-misuse risk); for_test gated _test_support
R62 cookie_signing_key Vec u8 plain memory (R70 carry: SigningKey newtype + zeroize)

runtime-crate--sce_link_runtime_tokio/3-.intent: deploy.yaml links.<name>.driver mapping — three values (tokio_udp/tokio_tcp/tokio_uring) for linux runtime; codegen wires per-driver impl; tokio_uring opt-in (kernel 5.10+).

runtime-crate--sce_link_runtime_tokio/4-.intent: Same six-state pool lifecycle FSM applies — per-edge action table maps MCU-bare-metal vs linux+epoll vs linux+io_uring; codegen emits same state-machine code.
io_uring fixed buffers must be pre-registered; build invariant Σ pool.slot_count ≤ io_uring.max_registered_buffers; diagnostic on overflow (hard error).
Neither §4.1 nor §4.2 changes the trait surface of §2 — same SCXML compiles unchanged across epoll and io_uring rows; ARCHITECTURE §9.5 invariant maintained.
io_uring fixed-buffer Phase D.1 opt-in — trait surface §2 unchanged; only edge actions on §5.E pool lifecycle FSM transitions differ; mechanical mapping documented.

runtime-crate--sce_link_runtime_tokio/5-.intent: Self-review against ARCHITECTURE §2.4 six invariants — every row PASS; LinkDriver trait extensible, driver selection deploy-driven, no runtime-driver swap.

runtime-crate--sce_link_runtime_tokio/6-.intent: Phase Z R51-R55b landed: TcpDriver + UdpDriver + session_glue (sce-rust-lua + 17 native fns + Engine-driving). Next: R55c codec wiring + multi-step FSM tick.

runtime-crate--sce_link_runtime_tokio/7-.intent: Change log — 2026-05-01 initial draft (KICKOFF #2); trait surface to four methods per OQ-W20; LinkEvent locked to 6 variants; trust-class compile-time gating; io_uring opt-in trait-invariant.

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/1-1-position-.intent: Scouting is pre-session — turns abstract "join Zenoh mesh" intent into concrete unicast endpoint feeding session FSM Init→LinkOpening.
inputs_bullets: deploy.scouting.{mode, timeout_ms, hello_max_peers}
links.udp_scout (trust_class=untrusted)
connect=/listen= deploy fields (used by static to bypass scouting)
Local node zid (16B ZenohID) and whatami (peer/client) compile-time consts
outputs_bullets: scout.hello.received(zid, whatami, locators[]) — feeds session FSM outbound Init→LinkOpening
scout.timeout — terminal 'no peers found within scouting.timeout_ms'
scout.hello.peer_table_full — diagnostic, HelloPeerTable hit hello_max_peers cap

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/1-2-scout-hel.intent: Scout (version/what/zid) and Hello (version/whatami/zid/locators[]) wire messages on UDP multicast 224.0.0.224:7446.
inputs_bullets: Scout: version u8 (0x09) / what u8 bitmask (router=1, peer=2, client=4, default 3) / zid 16B
Hello: version u8 / whatami u8 (single value) / zid 16B / locators len-prefixed array
Default UDP multicast 224.0.0.224:7446; SCOUT_BUFFER_SIZE=32B; default timeout 1000ms
scout_rx_pool.slot_size 256B (>> Scout encoded ~19B upper bound)

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/1-3-scouting-.intent: Scouting link is separate link instance from session links even on shared UDP multicast group/port; trust_class differs (untrusted vs session_arming).
caveats_bullets: R53 wz authoring: sources/links/lwip_udp_scout.scxml (retained R63 lwip revert)
SCE B6 link kind C11 emitter validated as link_emitter_b6 Layer 3 test (R63)

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/1-4-three-mod.intent: Three modes — active (zenoh-pico parity Included) / passive (Phase D+ deferred via OQ-W23 close) / static (config bypass parity).
inputs_bullets: active: _z_scout_inner one-shot (exit_on_first true|false) — zenoh-pico parity
passive: no zenoh-pico equivalent — deferred Phase D+, OQ-W23 closed
static: scouting bypass via connect=/listen= deploy fields — parity, scouting FSM never instantiated
MVP mode enum = {active, static}; passive lands at Phase D customer-driven need

alternatives_rejected.reason: weakens zenoh-pico parity contract (no upstream daemon)
reversibility asymmetry, removing harder than adding (forward-namespace-0)
schema simpler, but loses uniform mode enum

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/10-next-step-.intent: Scouting FSM unblocks Phase A SCXML authoring (active + static modes); declare_fsm authoring contract listed; passive deferred Phase D+.
outputs_bullets: Phase A SCXML authoring of sources/session/scouting.scxml for {active, static}
declare_fsm.scxml authoring contract documented (Mech1+Mech2 transport-class guard)
deploy.yaml scouting block stable (mode/timeout_ms/hello_max_peers)
Passive mode body + 3 deploy fields preserved in OQ-W23 entry for Phase D+ revival

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-1-hierarchy.intent: ScoutingDispatcher composes ModeSelector + Active body region (instantiated iff mode∈{active,passive}) + HelloPeerTable; codegen elides regions deploy-mode doesn't require.
inputs_bullets: ModeSelector: deploy.scouting.mode read at codegen time, compile-time const not runtime branch
Active region: states Idle/Sending/AwaitingHello/Cooldown (only when mode∈{active,passive})
HelloPeerTable: bounded-collection<HelloEntry, hello_max_peers>, keyed by zid, per-dispatcher
static mode emits near-empty stub; matches Z_FEATURE_SCOUTING compile-time gate discipline

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-2-states-un.intent: Five states union across modes: Idle / Sending / AwaitingHello / Cooldown (passive) / Bypassed (static); per-mode usage is a subset.
inputs_bullets: Idle: waiting for session-open trigger (active) or next period (passive)
Sending: encoding + transmitting one Scout frame on scouting link
AwaitingHello: polling RX path until scouting.timeout_ms; loops if exit_on_first=false
Cooldown: passive-only inter-period dwell; cooldown_timer = scout_retry_interval - timeout
Bypassed: static-only marker; emits synthetic scout.hello.received from deploy connect[] at startup

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-3-transitio.intent: Active mode transitions Idle→Sending→AwaitingHello with hello.received guards (capacity/dedup); Cooldown for passive; Bypassed terminal for static synthesizes events.
inputs_bullets: Idle→Sending on session_open.requested (active) or passive_period.elapsed (passive)
Sending: encode+link.send; immediate→AwaitingHello; tx_failed→Idle (diag scout/tx-failed)
AwaitingHello: scout_timer; hello.received guards (capacity, zid-dedup, exit_on_first)
Cooldown (passive only): cooldown_timer = scout_retry_interval_ms - timeout_ms
Bypassed (static): synth scout.hello.received per deploy.connect[] entry at startup; terminal

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-4-1-active.intent: Active body lifts zenoh-pico __z_scout_loop (scout.c:29-140) into states; per-line mapping to Sending entry / AwaitingHello self-loop / hello.received.
inputs_bullets: scout.c:57 link_send_wbuf → Sending entry action
scout.c:60-62 RX setup + clock → AwaitingHello entry, arm scout_timer
scout.c:63 elapsed loop → AwaitingHello self-loop until scout_timer.elapsed
scout.c:73-78 message decode → codec kind on scouting link, decode fail = drop+diag
scout.c:121-123 exit_on_first → guard on AwaitingHello→Idle transition
MVP active = implicit-scout (one Hello, take it); scouting.exhaustive=true deferred

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-4-2-passive.intent: Passive deferred Phase D+ (OQ-W23 closed 2026-05-01); MVP enum={active,static}; deploy/scouting-mode-unknown rejects passive value pre-Phase-D.

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-4-3-static.intent: Static = scouting-absent; one synthetic scout.hello.received per deploy.connect[] entry at startup; saves ~1.5KB SRAM via codegen elision on MCU.
inputs_bullets: deploy.connect[] locator list verbatim; matches zenoh-pico _z_locators_by_config
Codegen elides scouting link, scout codec, scout buffer pool (~1.5KB SRAM saved on MCU)
udp_session link still authored; only scouting setup omitted under static
Trust-class clarity: locators trusted-by-construction (deploy file) vs untrusted (wire)
Distinct mode value gives distinct diagnostics on session-open failure

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-5-timeouts-.intent: Two timers — scouting.timeout_ms (1000ms zenoh-pico parity) + link.scouting.open_timeout_ms (0ms always-on); passive timers deferred Phase D+.

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/2-6-bounded-c.intent: HelloPeerTable cap = hello_max_peers; two invariants — cap ≤ peer_table+multicast cap (hard error), slot_size ≥ Hello upper bound (informational).
inputs_bullets: Invariant 1 (hard error): hello_max_peers ≤ peer_table.capacity + multicast_peer_table.capacity
Invariant 2 (informational): scout_rx_pool.slot_size ≥ HelloUpperBoundBytes(hello_max_peers)
Diagnostic scouting/hello-max-peers-exceeds-peer-tables (hard error)
Diagnostic scouting/hello-slot-size-recommendation (informational)

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/3-2-oq-w3-ans.intent: OQ-W3 closed: Interest is not router-only in 1.x — three transport-class mechanisms (unicast acceptor push / multicast Interest pull / client-router push).
outputs_bullets: Mechanism 1 (unicast peer-peer): acceptor pushes ALL local declares + DeclareFinal at handshake
Mechanism 2 (multicast peer mesh): joining peer Interest{CURRENT,KEYEXPRS} → each remote replies
Mechanism 1 (client unicast to router): router-side same as peer-peer
Client unicast no router: not supported (Mech1 absent, Mech2 absent, inbound Interest = no-op)
MCU multicast peer must implement both directions; unicast-only MCU does only Mech1 acceptor push

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/3-3-implicati.intent: Scouting FSM doesn't participate in Interest itself, but mode choice determines which Interest mechanism downstream session-establishment fires.
outputs_bullets: mode=active → unicast session-open → Mechanism 1 (acceptor push at handshake)
mode=passive → multicast peer join → Mechanism 2 (Interest pull + multicast reply)
mode=static + connect[] → Mechanism 1 (same as active for resulting unicast handshake)
Scouting is the trigger chain; declare_fsm authors consult here for Interest-trigger boundary

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/6-1-per-link-.intent: Trust-class table extension: untrusted required on scouting links / forbidden elsewhere; three-layer mechanical defense (wire / session-fsm / reassembly).
inputs_bullets: untrusted: required on scouting / forbidden on session+reassembly
session_arming: forbidden on scouting / required on listener / forbidden on reassembly
established_session: forbidden on scouting / required on data-plane / required on reassembly
Layer 1 (wire): trust_class deploy field validated by RFC §5.K
Layer 2 (session-fsm): no Accepting.* on untrusted (session-fsm §2.6 row 1)
Layer 3 (reassembly): forbids reassembly pool binding on untrusted (reassembly-fsm §5)

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/6-4-oq-w9-clo.intent: OQ-W9 closed: zenoh-pico clients refuse multicast sessions (UNSUPPORTED) but participate in multicast scouting; codegen rejects whatami=client + multicast session link.
inputs_bullets: whatami=peer + multicast scouting + multicast session: OK (_z_multicast_open_peer)
whatami=client + multicast scouting: OK (scouting layer is whatami-agnostic)
whatami=client + multicast session link: REFUSED (_Z_ERR_CONFIG_UNSUPPORTED_CLIENT_MULTICAST)
Build diag: deploy/client-multicast-session-unsupported (hard error)
session-fsm §3.4 amend OQ-W9 status open→answered, see scouting-fsm §6.4

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/8-1-authoring.intent: Three gaps (G-SCT-1 resolved via OQ-W23 defer; G-SCT-2 unsolicited Hello broadcaster speculative; G-SCT-3 Hello slot-size worst-case sizing).
inputs_bullets: G-SCT-1 resolved: passive mode deferred Phase D+ (OQ-W23 close, MVP={active,static})
G-SCT-2 speculative: unsolicited Hello broadcaster (no OQ filed; await deploy scenario)
G-SCT-3 informational: scout_rx_pool slot_size vs Hello worst-case (multi-homed AP, 8 locators)
Recommendation: scouting/hello-slot-size-recommendation diagnostic (informational, not blocker)

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/8-2-new-open-.intent: OQ-W23 / OQ-W3 / OQ-W9 — all three closed in this document; cross-ref pointers added to rfc-open-questions-log entries.
inputs_bullets: OQ-W23 answered/deferred: passive mode Phase D+; (b) defaults preserved in OQ entry
OQ-W3 closed in §3.2: not router-only, three transport-class mechanisms documented
OQ-W9 closed in §6.4: client refuses multicast session, participates in scouting

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/10-next-.intent: R54-R55b: R29 script-action placeholders wired to wz-runtime-tokio LinkDriver via sce-rust-lua + Engine::process_event drives Init→LinkOpening verified.
outputs_bullets: deploy/*.yaml skeleton authorable — timer / lease / handshake / closing fully named
Runtime crate API stubs — link event contract no longer hand-waved
scouting-fsm.md sibling sketch (active/passive/static)
reassembly-fsm.md sibling sketch (RxDispatch + Fragment row inputs)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-1-unic.intent: Unicast session FSM has eight states plus Closed terminal; splits Opening (initiator) and Accepting (acceptor) explicitly.
inputs_bullets: Init: created, link not yet open; entry from outbound or inbound
LinkOpening: outbound link driver attempting open (TCP connect / WS upgrade)
Scouting: active scout loop pre-target (mode=active deploys only)
Opening: initiator driving 4-way Init/Open handshake
Accepting: acceptor responding to remote-driven handshake
Established: handshake done, RX/TX regions enabled (sub-region container)
Closing: graceful close in progress; sends Close with reason
Closed: terminal, link released, pool slots freed

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-2-unic.intent: Unicast transitions — outbound (LinkOpening → Opening.SentInitSyn → GotInitAck → Established) + inbound (Accepting.AwaitingInitSyn → SentInitAck → SentOpenAck → Established).
outputs_bullets: Outbound: Init → LinkOpening → Opening.SentInitSyn → GotInitAck → Established
Inbound: Init → Accepting.AwaitingInitSyn → SentInitAck → SentOpenAck → Established
Inbound entry guarded by half_open_cap_available + accept_rate_token (silent drop if denied)
Cookie validation guard: Accepting.SentInitAck → SentOpenAck rejects invalid/expired cookie
stateless_accept mode: AwaitingInitSyn→SentInitAck does not allocate FSM instance (cookie-only)
Timeouts: init_ack/open_ack/init_syn/open_syn → Closing(GENERIC) or Closed(inbound-stall)
Framing errors → Closing(reason=INVALID); peer Close → Closed(peer-rejected)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-3-esta.intent: Established is parallel SCXML region with 4 concurrent sub-machines: RxDispatch / TxSchedule / Keepalive / LeaseMonitor.
inputs_bullets: RxDispatch: Frame→NetworkMessage / Fragment→reassembly / KeepAlive→reset / Close→parent / OAM→diag
TxSchedule: Idle / Batching / Flushing per (reliability, priority) band
Keepalive: Armed / Sending; timer = lease × keepalive_ratio (default 1/3)
LeaseMonitor: Healthy / Warning / Expired; warning at lease×2/3

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-4-clos.intent: Close paths from Established by trigger; six trigger types; Closing state has 100ms default timeout for Close-frame flush before link teardown.
outputs_bullets: session.close() → Closing(GENERIC) → Closed
LeaseMonitor.Expired → Closing(EXPIRED) → Closed
RxDispatch peer Close → Closed (no reciprocal Close)
Framing error → Closing(INVALID|UNSUPPORTED) → Closed
link.lost → Closed (no Close frame)
TX congestion budget exhaust on RELIABLE batch → Closing(UNRESPONSIVE) → Closed

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-5-time.intent: 12 named timers (link / handshake / scout / lease / keepalive / closing / batch / reassembly / accept-rate / cookie). R61b: Hal time-mock for delayed-send coverage carry.
inputs_bullets: link.open_timeout (5s) / init_ack_timeout / open_ack_timeout (2s) / scout_timeout (1s)
lease (10s, negotiated) / keepalive_interval (lease/3) / closing_timeout (100ms)
batch_timer (1ms AP / 5ms MCU) / reassembly_timeout (500ms per peer-priority-reliability-sn_base)
accept_rate_window (1s) / accepting_inactivity_timeout (1s)
cookie_hmac_lifetime (30s) / cookie_hmac_key_rotation (1h, old key honored one extra lifetime)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-6-trus.intent: Trust-class on each link gates hardening rules; session_arming requires quota + accept_rate; established_session is sibling for post-handshake traffic.
inputs_bullets: untrusted: Scout/Hello-only links, never spawns Accepting
session_arming: hosts Accepting cluster; quota + accept_rate required (hard error)
established_session: post-handshake Frame/Fragment, no Accepting attachment
untrusted_source=true on session_arming → stateless_accept required (not just recommended)
Listener with session_arming emits sibling established_session instance (OQ-W22 resolution)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-7-acce.intent: 3-cap hardening (half-open / accept-rate / cookie HMAC) + silent-drop on denial. R62: cookie HMAC-SHA256 helper landed; R63b inbound parser populates Initiator-side cookie from peer InitAck.
inputs_bullets: Half-open capacity: session_arming_quota bounds concurrent Accepting.* instances per listener
Per-source token bucket: accept_rate_per_sec / accept_rate_burst per (link, src_addr)
Stateless accept (optional): cookie_hmac_sha256 defers FSM allocation until cookie validated
Three caps chain in series; silent drop on cap denial (no Close frame, anti-amplification)
deploy.yaml emits all three as bounded-collection checks + token-bucket guard at Init→Accepting

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/3-1-mult.intent: Multicast session-level FSM: Idle → LinkOpening → Running (parallel: JoinEmit / RxDispatch / PeerSweep) → Stopped.
inputs_bullets: JoinEmit: periodic Join (default 2500ms upstream interval); state Idle/Sending
RxDispatch: classify Join/Frame/Fragment/KeepAlive/Close/OAM on TransportMessageLowLatency
PeerSweep: timer lease/3, evict PeerTable entries with last_seen > lease
Idle→LinkOpening on create(multicast_link); LinkOpening→Running on link.ready

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/3-2-mult.intent: Per-peer FSM (one per zid in multicast_peer_table bounded-collection): Discovered → Active → Expired.
inputs_bullets: Discovered: first Join validated (version/resolution/batch/qos); init RX seq table
Active: any msg refresh last_seen; Close{zid} or lease expiry → Expired
Expired: emit peer-lost event; PeerTable removes at next sweep
Rejection: version/max_sessions/SN-res/QoS mismatch → ignored + diag (not a transition)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/3-3-no-c.intent: Multicast has no Closing state — peers time out via lease monitors; symmetric to upstream which has no multicast/close.rs.

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/3-4-clie.intent: Client whatami refuses multicast session (zenoh-pico parity); participates in scouting only. OQ-W9 closed via deploy/client-multicast-session-unsupported.

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/4-scouti.intent: Scouting is a sibling SCXML; produces Hello events that drive Init→LinkOpening→Opening. Three modes: active / passive / static.
inputs_bullets: active: Listening → Sending → WaitHello → Connect; clients + peer initial bring-up
passive: Listening only; peers after mesh stable, receives unsolicited Hello
static: no scouting; deploy.yaml endpoints, session.create(outbound,endpoint) at boot
Scouting link distinct from session links even on shared UDP multicast group/port (different framer)
Hello from already-established peer = no-op (matched by zid; idempotent with session creation)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/5-3-q13-.intent: Q13 closed: two SCXML files split by transport class (unicast / multicast), NOT by mode (peer / client) — peer/client wire-identical at session layer.
outputs_bullets: sources/session/session_unicast.scxml — handles both peer and client unicast
sources/session/session_multicast.scxml — instantiated only on machines with multicast session link
whatami read from deploy at compile time, embedded in InitSyn, not branched on
Earlier session_peer.scxml + session_client.scxml framing retracted across ARCH §5 / RFC §7 C8
Parametric across unicast/multicast rejected — handshake vs no-handshake structurally different

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/6-link-l.intent: 6 inbound link events + 3 outbound invocations. R52.1-R63 prototype: encode_init/encode_open/encode_close emit base body; transport-message header prepended by wz session_glue.
rationale_bullets: Inbound 6: ready / lost(cause) / rx(bytes) / tx_drained / backpressure(on|off) / framing_error
Outbound 3: link.open() / link.send(bytes, reliability) / link.close()
FSM never touches raw bytes — wz-codecs::init_body / open_body / close emit per-message body
Transport header prepended by session_glue::encode_init/open/close: (flags | T_MID-low-5-bits)
InitSyn flags=T_INIT_S (0x40); InitAck adds T_INIT_A (0x20); T_MID_INIT (0x01)
OpenSyn flags=T_OPEN_T per lease unit; OpenAck adds T_OPEN_A (0x20); T_MID_OPEN (0x02)
Close flags=T_CLOSE_S (graceful session close, 0x20); T_MID_CLOSE (0x03)
R57 byte-equiv: wz body matches zenoh-pico _z_init_encode / _z_open_encode / _z_close_encode
R62 cookie HMAC-SHA256(key, peer_zid)[..16] flows through SessionInitParams.cookie field
Reliability::Reliable in R57 baseline; BestEffort path lives in R52 UdpDriver
inputs_bullets: link.ready: TCP/WS open → LinkOpening→Opening.SentInitSyn or attach Accepting
link.lost(cause): underlying link broken → Closed(diag: link-lost)
link.rx(bytes): pass to codec, codec emits recv(MsgType) events
link.tx_drained: TX pool slot freed → wake TxSchedule
link.backpressure(on|off): TX queue watermark crossed → TxSchedule pause/resume
link.framing_error: codec decode failed → Closing(INVALID)
outputs_bullets: link.open(): initiate connect (outbound only)
link.send(bytes, reliability): ship encoded TransportMessage
link.close(): tear down byte channel after Close flushed
caveats_bullets: link.rx parser pass (R63b carry): peer InitAck → SessionInitParams.cookie populate
Reliability::BestEffort path lands when wz UdpDriver session FSM wiring closes (R52)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/7-extens.intent: 5 extension classes (QoS/QoSLink/Auth/MultiLink/LowLatency) negotiated on Init/Open/Join. R63a: wz SCXMLs not yet authored against §7 sce:tlv-chain — multi-session carry.
inputs_bullets: ext::QoS / ext::QoSLink — InitSyn/InitAck; QoS Close(UNSUPPORTED) on incompat
ext::Auth — multi-step in InitSyn/InitAck/OpenSyn/OpenAck; OQ-W2/W10
ext::MultiLink — graceful downgrade to single-link on negotiation fail
ext::LowLatency — session refused on link-class mismatch
ext::Shm — always advertised absent in MVP; Close(UNSUPPORTED) on peer-critical
ext::Compression — accept-and-ignore (deploy extensions_ignored); OQ-W4 if peer-critical
ext::PatchType — Close(UNSUPPORTED) on peer patch > ours; OQ-W7
caveats_bullets: R67: ext_envelope.scxml landed (SCE 6-backend body-equiv). init/open/join body 보존. R63a → close.
R67b: ext_envelope round-trip oracle pinned in cargo test (8-byte wire decode + encode byte-equiv).
R67c: Layer 3 wire-interop pinned (wz byte-equiv pico chain). R67 full close.

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/8-1-auth.intent: Five authoring contract gaps (G-SFM-1..5) blocked on §5.B codec IR / §5.N policy / §5.I extern primitive ownership.
inputs_bullets: G-SFM-1: cookie continuity datamodel across Accepting.SentInitAck→SentOpenAck (blocked on codec IR)
G-SFM-2: Established.TxSchedule × multi-link policy (RFC clarification or deploy attribute needed)
G-SFM-3: session FSM emit / codec write boundary (blocked on codec IR)

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/1-p.intent: 5-layer stack — Scouting/Transport/Network/Payload/Extensions; every byte belongs to exactly one layer; per-layer SCE kind unambiguous.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/10-.intent: Open questions OQ-W1..W8 — zenoh-pico release pin, Auth baseline, Interest no-router, ext::Compression critical bit, OAM drop semantics, batch defaults, PatchType, bounded defaults.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/11-.intent: Self-review checklist against ARCHITECTURE §2.4 six invariants — every row checkable true; failing during Phase B is design bug, file as RFC OQ or doc revision.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/12-.intent: Change log — 2026-04-24 initial draft (zenoh-protocol 1.5.0 / VERSION 0x09 grounded); OQ-W1..W8 filed; classification totals to re-verify against zenoh-pico.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/2-c.intent: Three-bucket classification legend (Included/Deferred/Permanent) aligned with ARCHITECTURE §2.1/2.2/2.3; Backing-kind column points at RFC §5 source.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/3-s.intent: Scouting layer — Scout/Hello messages (codec RFC §5.B); link kind with framer datagram; UDP multicast 224.0.0.224:7446 by convention.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/4-1.intent: Transport control messages — InitSyn/InitAck/OpenSyn/OpenAck/Close/KeepAlive (4-way handshake + lease); all Included; codec + statechart.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/4-2.intent: Data-carrying framing — Frame (priority/SN), Fragment (RFC §5.M reassembly), Join (multicast), OAM (decode-only); chain key (peer_zid, reliability) 2-tuple.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/4-3.intent: TransportMessageLowLatency variant for UDP unicast/multicast — union of Close/KeepAlive/NetworkMessage; selected statically per-link in deploy.yaml.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/4-4.intent: Init/Open/Join extension chain (6 classes). R63a: wz SCXMLs not yet authored against §7 sce:tlv-chain — Phase Z prototype emits base body only.
rationale_bullets: QoS / QoSLink — Included (priority + reliability negotiation per Init/Open/Join)
Auth — Included-negotiate-only; full method catalog (USRPWD, pubkey) Phase D
MultiLink — Included; backs RFC §5.N concurrent-link-per-session codegen
LowLatency — Included; selects §4.3 framing variant on UDP unicast/multicast
PatchType — Included; wire-format patch level negotiated + honored
Shm — Permanent on MCU / Deferred on AP (not in zenoh-pico baseline)
Compression — Deferred; ARCHITECTURE §2.5 pins under extensions_ignored
R57 wire-format land emits init_body / open_body / close codec body only; no ext chain
R62 cookie HMAC-SHA256 carried inside InitBody.cookie field, not via ext::Auth
Real zenoh-pico peer interop requires R63a — SCXMLs against §7 sce:tlv-chain primitive
caveats_bullets: R52.1-R63 prototype emits NO ext chain on wire (session_glue base body only)
Layer 3 byte-equiv references also lack ext::* inputs — passing test ≠ ext chain emit

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/4-t.intent: Transport (session) layer — per-peer session state authored as session_unicast/multicast.scxml; split by transport class; peer/client share unicast FSM (Q13).

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/5-1.intent: Declare sub-variants — KeyExpr/Subscriber/Queryable/Token (declare+undeclare) + DeclareFinal; all Included; bounded-collection (RFC §5.L) backed.
caveats_bullets: R110 added wz codecs for all 9 DECLARE sub-MIDs (kexpr/sub/queryable/token + undecl + final).

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/5-2.intent: Permanent-on-MCU network features — wildcard Interest aggregation across N peers, forwarding-table maintenance, global key-space indexing; deferred on AP.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/5-n.intent: Network (routing) layer carried inside Frame/Fragment — Push/Request/Response/ResponseFinal/Interest/Declare/OAM; Interest bounded form; aggregation router-only.
caveats_bullets: R107 INTEREST M-flag M=0 default kept (R-gate semantic differs from push/response/request).

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/6-1.intent: PushBody variants — Put (publish value with timestamp/encoding/sinfo/attachment/payload) and Del (delete key); both Included; codec-backed.
caveats_bullets: wz Push::default() bakes 0x5D (MID 0x1D|M); M=1 = LOCAL wireexpr (R106/R102 Layer 3).

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/6-2.intent: RequestBody variant — Query (parameters/consolidation/sinfo/body/attachment); Included; codec + statechart query FSM.
caveats_bullets: wz Request::default() bakes 0x5C (MID 0x1C|M); R108a fixed missing mid value=, R108b Layer 3.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/6-3.intent: ResponseBody variants — Reply (timestamp/encoding/sinfo/payload) and Err (encoding/sinfo/payload, reply-channel error); Included; codec + statechart.
caveats_bullets: wz Response::default() bakes 0x5B (MID 0x1B|M); LOCAL wireexpr default (R106/R105 Layer 3).

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/6-z.intent: Zenoh payload layer — innermost user payload carried inside Push/Request/Response; PushBody/RequestBody/ResponseBody variants enumerated in §6.1-§6.3.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/7-1.intent: Common app-visible extensions — Timestamp/Attachment/NodeId/SourceInfo/Consolidation/Encoding/QoS Included; SHM permanent on MCU / deferred on AP.
caveats_bullets: ext_entry variant requires explicit default="true" arm per SCE 022621a3 (R112 vendor bump).

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/7-2.intent: Unknown extension policy — ignorable bit set: decode envelope discard body; critical bit unset: framing error → Close{unsupported_extension}.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/7-e.intent: Extension chain TLV with more-bit per RFC §5.B; per-extension budgets in deploy.yaml; decode-time enforcement with diagnostic on overflow.
caveats_bullets: R67: wz ext_envelope codec landed (RFC §5.B B5-ε wrapper; SCE upstream body-equiv 6/6).

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/8-t.intent: Transport/link matrix — message-class × link-class table (TCP/UDP-uni/UDP-multi/Serial/WS); per-link framer in deploy.yaml; new classes via target plugin.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/9-1.intent: Per-layer Included/Deferred/Permanent counts — Scouting 2/0/0, Transport 10/0/0, Network 16/0/3, Payload 5/0/0, Extensions 7/1/0.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/9-2.intent: RFC §5 kind cross-reference table — §5.A/B/C/D/E/F/I/J/K/L/M/N each used by; every MVP message covered by ≥1 kind; gaps file as RFC OQ.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/9-s.intent: Scope classification summary — message-type counts per layer + Cross-reference to RFC §5 kinds; every Included row covered by ≥1 kind.

Round 100.decision_summary: R100 promotes SubscriberRegistry from literal-only to zenoh chunk-wildcard matching (`*` single-chunk, `**` zero-or-more chunks) — production-realistic keyexpr semantics that closes R98 carry #3 and matches what real zenoh-pico subscribers use (`home/**`, `sensors/*/temp`).
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs: `Subscriber.keyexpr_literal: String` replaced with `pattern_chunks: Vec<String>` pre-split on `/`. Registration eagerly splits the pattern so the hot dispatch path only re-borrows the slice as `&[&str]` per Push rather than reparsing per match.

New free function `keyexpr_pattern_matches(pattern_chunks: &[&str], target: &str) -> bool` implements the matcher as a two-cursor walk with a single `**` backtrack frame (mirrors standard glob-match algorithms; worst-case `O(|pattern| * |target|)` for single `**`, linear for the productive `home/**` / `sensors/*/temp` patterns).

`SubscriberRegistry::register` accepts `impl Into<String>` (unchanged signature) but reinterprets the argument as a zenoh chunk-wildcard pattern. Backward compatibility: a literal keyexpr like `"topic/a"` still matches itself because all chunks are literals — the existing R98/R99 tests continue to pass without modification.

`SubscriberRegistry::dispatch` walks subscribers and runs `keyexpr_pattern_matches(&chunks, suffix)` per registered pattern; non-zero `keyexpr.id` filtering preserved (DECLARE mapping still deferred).

Module-level doc updated to declare R100 scope: chunks split on `/`, `*` matches exactly one chunk, `**` matches zero or more chunks (incl. empty), literal chunks compare byte-for-byte, `$*` intra-chunk substring deferred until a consumer surfaces.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib pubsub: 12 passed — 6 pre-R100 tests stay green (literal patterns still match themselves) + 6 new wildcard tests cover literal-equality, single-chunk `*` (including the negative case where `*` does NOT collapse to zero), and `**` at prefix / middle / trailing positions plus the zero-chunks case.
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --test session_fsm_drive_session r99_subscriber: still 1 passed — R99 integration test uses a literal pattern `"demo/topic"` so it stays unchanged.

scripts/run-ci.sh all 5 lanes pass: Layer A (validate-workspace), Layer B (verify-codegen), Layer C1 (cargo test), Layer C2 (cargo clippy -D warnings), Layer D (deploy yaml schema). Clippy clean first try.
Workspace tests: 134 → 140 (+6 = new wildcard matcher unit tests).
atomic ledger entries: 112 → 113 (this Round 100 entry).
T1=0 / RT 12/12 / GENERATED.md=sync preserved.
carry_forward_bullets: AP MVP demo binary — R98+R99+R100 cover the publisher → subscriber registry path with zenoh-realistic keyexpr matching. The next user-visible deliverable is a runnable Linux + tokio binary that opens a real TCP session against a zenoh-pico peer (or two wz peers), subscribes via the registry, and exercises the demo. Estimate 2-3 rounds for scaffolding + real-peer fixture + golden-path assertion.

`$*` intra-chunk substring wildcard — zenoh full keyexpr language includes patterns like `home/temp$*` matching `home/temperature`. Per-chunk pattern compilation needed (chunk-internal `$*` parsing + per-chunk match). Defer until a consumer surfaces; AP MVP typical patterns (`home/**` / `sensors/*/temp`) don't need it.

DECLARE codec (0x1E) — 9 declaration sub-types per zenoh-pico declarations.c, multi-round chain (~3 rounds like RESPONSE). Closure unblocks the mapping-id resolver on the registry so non-zero `wireexpr.id` Pushes route through resolved-base + suffix concatenation.

Symmetric dispatch for Reply / Err / Response — dispatch is Push-only. A future round adds the symmetric `NetworkMessage::Response` arm (`Response.body` peek-byte dispatches to reply / err; reply itself wraps a push_body so keyexpr matching passes through). Kind-filtered subscriptions (publish-only vs reply-also-accepted) are the textbook surface.

Layer 3 wire-interop tests — R90 / R91 carry. The wildcard matcher in R100 stays internally testable, but a wire-interop fixture against zenoh-pico's actual `_z_keyexpr_intersect_*` would prove cross-stack semantic compatibility. Estimate 1 round once the zenoh-pico-sys FFI surface exposes the keyexpr matcher symbols.
Round 100.publishable_decision_summary: R100 promotes SubscriberRegistry from literal-only to zenoh chunk-wildcard matching (`*` single-chunk, `**` zero-or-more chunks) — production-realistic keyexpr semantics that closes R98 carry #3 and matches what real zenoh-pico subscribers use (`home/**`, `sensors/*/temp`).
publishable_changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs: `Subscriber.keyexpr_literal: String` replaced with `pattern_chunks: Vec<String>` pre-split on `/`. Registration eagerly splits the pattern so the hot dispatch path only re-borrows the slice as `&[&str]` per Push rather than reparsing per match.

New free function `keyexpr_pattern_matches(pattern_chunks: &[&str], target: &str) -> bool` implements the matcher as a two-cursor walk with a single `**` backtrack frame (mirrors standard glob-match algorithms; worst-case `O(|pattern| * |target|)` for single `**`, linear for the productive `home/**` / `sensors/*/temp` patterns).

`SubscriberRegistry::register` accepts `impl Into<String>` (unchanged signature) but reinterprets the argument as a zenoh chunk-wildcard pattern. Backward compatibility: a literal keyexpr like `"topic/a"` still matches itself because all chunks are literals — the existing R98/R99 tests continue to pass without modification.

`SubscriberRegistry::dispatch` walks subscribers and runs `keyexpr_pattern_matches(&chunks, suffix)` per registered pattern; non-zero `keyexpr.id` filtering preserved (DECLARE mapping still deferred).

Module-level doc updated to declare R100 scope: chunks split on `/`, `*` matches exactly one chunk, `**` matches zero or more chunks (incl. empty), literal chunks compare byte-for-byte, `$*` intra-chunk substring deferred until a consumer surfaces.

Round 101.decision_summary: R101 lands the first Layer 3 wire-interop test for the post-R90 codec catalog — ResponseFinal::default().encode() now proven byte-equivalent to zenoh-pico's `_z_response_final_encode`; closes the smallest carry of the R90/R91/R92/R93-R94/R95-R97 wire-interop debt accumulation and seeds the rollout pattern for the rest of the catalog.
changes_bullets: crates/zenoh-pico-sys/wrapper.h: `#include "zenoh-pico/protocol/codec/network.h"` added alongside the existing transport/message/ext includes so bindgen can see the N-level (network-MID) encoders — same expansion pattern as previous Layer 3 rounds (the umbrella public header does not transitively pull in the internal codec headers; Layer 3 needs them explicitly).

crates/zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist: `_z_response_final_encode` + `_z_n_msg_response_final_t` added with an inline comment explaining why ResponseFinal was chosen as the first contact (smallest envelope post-R90: header + zsize request_id only).

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_response_final.rs new — 3 byte-equivalence tests: (1) default ResponseFinal (request_id=0) emits the canonical [0x1A, 0x00] 2-byte wire, (2) single-byte VLE rid 0x42 emits [0x1A, 0x42], (3) multi-byte VLE rid 200 crosses the 7-bit boundary and emits [0x1A, 0xC8, 0x01]. Each test asserts wz_codecs::response_final::ResponseFinal encode() bytes match zenoh-pico's `_z_response_final_encode` output. Test layout mirrors the existing layer3_keep_alive.rs pattern (wbuf alloc → encode → wbuf_to_zbuf → raw slice copy → wbuf_clear).

Helper `zenoh_pico_encode_response_final(request_id: u64) -> Vec<u8>` widens the test's u64 input to the bindgen-surfaced `usize` zint_t via `as _` so the call site keeps the natural numeric type — zenoh-pico's `_z_zint_t` resolves to `size_t` on x86_64-linux which bindgen produces as `usize`. Comment explains the widen for future Layer 3 authors hitting the same surface.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test layer3_response_final: 3 passed (default + single-byte VLE + multi-byte VLE).
Byte-by-byte equality confirmed against the live `_z_response_final_encode` call — wz codec output is not merely self-consistent but actually matches zenoh-pico's encoder for the modeled state space.

Wire-interop for the inner-body sub-codecs (interest_body R94, reply R95, err R96) is gated on whether zenoh-pico exposes the inner encoder as a separately callable function. interest_body is invoked via `_z_interest_encode` (the outer envelope), reply/err via `_z_response_encode`. Direct sub-codec byte-equivalence may require synthetic fixtures that target the wire-form sub-range; defer until the outer envelope Layer 3 tests reveal whether per-sub-codec coverage adds confidence beyond the envelope test alone.

PUSH (R90) Layer 3 will exercise the wireexpr embed + ext-chain + peek-byte variant body — first Layer 3 test to span an embedded codec AND a tlv-chain AND a variant primitive in a single fixture. Expect minor allowlist additions (`_z_push_encode`, `_z_n_msg_push_t`, `_z_wireexpr_t`, `_z_msg_put_t` ext slot helpers).

OAM (R92) Layer 3 will exercise the self-flag-dispatch variant (header.enc) with three default-state arms (UNIT / ZINT / ZBUF). Should resolve the ext_entry self-flag-dispatch audit (R88 carry #1) cleanly because UNIT is the declared default arm and `_z_oam_encode` defaults the enc field to 00 = UNIT, matching the wz codec's variant-default-uniformity behavior.

RESPONSE (R97) Layer 3 is the most involved post-R90 codec (envelope + wireexpr embed + ext-chain + peek-byte variant with reply/err arm bodies). Likely a 2-round split: layer3_response.rs (envelope default state with empty reply body) + layer3_response_with_inner_body.rs (R88 default arm chain: response → reply → msg_put each carrying their baked MID).

Round 102.decision_summary: R102 lands Layer 3 byte-equivalence for PUSH (R90) — first composite-shape post-R90 codec validated against zenoh-pico's `_z_push_encode`; surfaces a real M-flag semantic divergence (wz M=0 default vs zenoh-pico's auto-M=1 for local mapping) that the test fixture documents and pins as carry.
changes_bullets: crates/zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist: `_z_n_msg_push_t` + `_z_push_encode` added with inline rationale citing the composite shape (header + wireexpr + Z-gated ext-chain + put/del peek-byte variant) — first Layer 3 fixture to span all those primitives at once.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_push.rs new — 1 byte-equivalence test asserting `wz_codecs::push::Push::default().encode() == _z_push_encode(default _z_n_msg_push_t)`. Wire bytes: `[0x1D, 0x00, 0x01, 0x00]` (push header, wireexpr.id VLE 0, msg_put header MID 0x01, payload_len VLE 0).

The zenoh-pico fixture patches three fields off zero-init to reach the wz default state: (1) `_qos._val = 5` matches `_Z_N_QOS_DEFAULT._val` so `has_qos_ext` evaluates false; (2) `_body._is_put = true` routes the union to the PUT branch (zero-init would pick DEL via `_is_put=false`); (3) `_key._mapping = 1` (non-LOCAL) prevents zenoh-pico's encoder from auto-setting the M flag.

Each patch carries a comment naming the upstream source location (`definitions/network.c L22` for qos default, `protocol/core.h:151` for LOCAL constant) so future Layer 3 authors can audit the divergence list without re-discovering them.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test layer3_push: 1 passed.
Wire bytes byte-for-byte equal between wz Push::default().encode() and the zenoh-pico encoder with the documented field patches applied.

First-pass test FAILED with `wz=[1d,00,01,00] pico=[5d,00,01,00]` — the diagnostic byte-diff (0x5D = 0x1D | M_flag 0x40) located the M-flag divergence in seconds. Textbook value of byte-equivalence testing: a semantic mismatch that R90's wz-only round-trip never surfaced is now caught at the wire-interop boundary.
carry_forward_bullets: M-flag semantic divergence — wz push.scxml declares `<sce:flag name="M" bit="6"/>` without a `value=` default, so `Push::default().header = 0x1D` (M=0). zenoh-pico's encoder auto-sets M when `_z_wireexpr_is_local(&_key)` returns true, which is the DEFAULT for a zero-init wireexpr (mapping=0=LOCAL). For freshly-constructed Pushes without a prior DECLARE the LOCAL semantic is the right one ("the mapping I'm using is local to me"). Investigation: should wz push.scxml bake M=1 by default? Same applies to request.scxml / response.scxml (same flag layout). 1-round audit + decision.

Layer 3 rollout to OAM (R92) is next — self-flag-dispatch variant (header.enc) with three default-state arms (UNIT / ZINT / ZBUF). Adds `_z_oam_encode` + `_z_n_msg_oam_t` to the allowlist. Should resolve the ext_entry self-flag-dispatch audit (R88 carry #1) since UNIT is the declared default arm and `_z_oam_encode` defaults the enc field to 00 = UNIT.

Layer 3 rollout to INTEREST envelope + inner body (R93+R94) — `_z_n_interest_encode` exercises the disjunction present-if landing R94 brought in. Will likely surface a parallel divergence with the wireexpr's mapping default (interest_body's wireexpr embed is parent-flagged off interest_body.header.N, not the outer interest.header).

Layer 3 rollout to RESPONSE (R97) — most involved post-R90 codec (wireexpr embed + ext-chain + reply/err peek-byte variant). Likely 2-round split per R101 carry: envelope-default state first, then non-trivial inner-body state space.

AP MVP demo binary is still the user-visible critical path — R98-R100 internal infrastructure + R101-R102 wire-interop confidence growing. Real Linux + tokio demo binary remains 2-3 rounds out (TCP scaffolding + zenoh-pico peer fixture).

Layer 3 interest fixture is is_final form (no body emitted) so neither path affects R104's test; the decision affects only future non-final coverage.

Round 107.decision_summary: R107 closes the INTEREST M-flag carry from R104/R106 with a decision-only outcome: interest_body keeps M=0 default (no baking), because the M-flag in INTEREST is R-gate (RESTRICTED bit) dependent and wz default (R=0) would produce wire M=1 against zenoh-pico's wire M=0 if the M=1-baking pattern from R106 were applied uniformly.
changes_bullets: No code change. interest_body.scxml:74 (`<sce:flag name="M" bit="6"/>` without `value=`) is the correct shape for INTEREST and is preserved verbatim.

Audit performed by direct read of zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/interest.c:41-69: _z_interest_encode branches on `_Z_INTEREST_FLAG_RESTRICTED` (bit 4). When R=1 it sets `_Z_INTEREST_CODEC_FLAG_M = 0x40` from `_z_wireexpr_is_local(&_keyexpr)`; when R=0 it emits `interest->flags` unchanged (flags byte initialized to 0 by `_z_interest_null()`).

wz interest_body default is R=0 (the entire wireexpr embed is `sce:present-if="header.R"`-gated). M=1 baking under that default would emit wire M=1 while zenoh-pico's natural default emits wire M=0 — direct wire-byte regression. The R106 baking pattern only fits codecs whose wireexpr is unconditionally present (push/response/request); INTEREST is structurally different.

R104 layer3_interest fixture asserts the is_final form (`[0x19, 0x00]`) where the outer envelope's `is_final` branch in `_z_n_interest_encode` skips the flags byte entirely. R107 decision therefore does not touch R104's wire assertion. Verified by reading crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_interest.rs:26-45.

Round 107.publishable_decision_summary: R107 closes the INTEREST M-flag carry from R104/R106 with a decision-only outcome: interest_body keeps M=0 default (no baking), because the M-flag in INTEREST is R-gate (RESTRICTED bit) dependent and wz default (R=0) would produce wire M=1 against zenoh-pico's wire M=0 if the M=1-baking pattern from R106 were applied uniformly.

Audit performed by direct read of zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/interest.c:41-69: _z_interest_encode branches on `_Z_INTEREST_FLAG_RESTRICTED` (bit 4). When R=1 it sets `_Z_INTEREST_CODEC_FLAG_M = 0x40` from `_z_wireexpr_is_local(&_keyexpr)`; when R=0 it emits `interest->flags` unchanged (flags byte initialized to 0 by `_z_interest_null()`).

wz interest_body default is R=0 (the entire wireexpr embed is `sce:present-if="header.R"`-gated). M=1 baking under that default would emit wire M=1 while zenoh-pico's natural default emits wire M=0 — direct wire-byte regression. The R106 baking pattern only fits codecs whose wireexpr is unconditionally present (push/response/request); INTEREST is structurally different.

Round 108a.decision_summary: R108a fixes a latent wire-correctness defect in request.scxml: the `mid` flag was declared without `value=` so wz Request::default()'s wire header byte was emitted as 0x40 (M flag only, no MID nibble) instead of the spec-required 0x5C (= 0x1C MID | M flag); discovered during R108 REQUEST Layer 3 wire-interop planning before the Layer 3 fixture was authored, so no published test ever consumed the broken bytes.
changes_bullets: sources/codecs/request.scxml:91 — `<sce:flag name="mid" bit="0" width="5"/>` → `value="0x1C"` added, matching zenoh-pico's `_Z_MID_N_REQUEST = 0x1c` (definitions/network.h:36).

Defect provenance: R90 wz-side request authoring imported the SCXML structure from push.scxml / response.scxml but missed copying the mid `value=` annotation. push (0x1D) and response (0x1B) had it; request did not. No upstream test caught this because no Layer 3 wire-interop fixture for REQUEST existed yet — a wire-spec self-consistency gap the R102/R104/R105 paired-land pattern only retroactively flagged through R108 planning.

Defect detection path: planning the REQUEST Layer 3 fixture surfaced the question "what is the expected wire first byte?" — cross-checking codegen emit ($OUT_DIR/wz-codecs-*/out/request.rs:63 `header: 0x40u8`) against zenoh-pico's `_z_request_encode` (network.c:116 `header = _Z_MID_N_REQUEST | ...`) made the divergence immediate. Documented as the proper R94-style empirical verify path before relying on the bake.

Build emit re-inspected after the SCXML change: request.rs `impl Default::default()` now bakes `header: 0x5cu8` (= 0x1C | 0x40). R88 RFC variant-default-uniformity for the inner RequestVariant (declared default arm = Query) still picks `CodecZenohQuery(Query::default())` unchanged.

No fixture / test code touched in R108a — the Layer 3 wire-compare itself (zenoh-pico-sys allowlist expansion, fixture for the 5 ext fields, inner Query body fixture) is carry-split into R108b under feedback_partial_apply_carry.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-codecs: clean rebuild; emit inspected directly at target/debug/build/wz-codecs-*/out/request.rs:63 — header literal `0x5cu8`.

cargo test --workspace: 147 tests pass (same count as post-R106; no test currently asserts wire bytes for REQUEST so no test status changes — the silent-bug-fix profile of R108a is documented honestly in this entry rather than performing a fake test-count delta).
scripts/run-ci.sh: all 5 lanes (A/B/C1/C2/D) pass.
validate_workspace: docs=12/12, T1=0, RT 12/12, ledger entries 120 → 121, sections=214, GENERATED.md=sync.

carry_forward_bullets: R108b REQUEST Layer 3 wire-interop — zenoh-pico-sys allowlist expansion (`_z_n_msg_request_t` + `_z_request_encode`); fixture for the 5 default-state-emitted ext fields (`_z_n_msg_request_needed_exts` evaluates `ext_qos = (val != 5)` true by default — the same `_ext_qos._val = 5` patch as PUSH/RESPONSE handles it); inner-body fixture for `_Z_REQUEST_QUERY` tag (= 0, first enum variant) so the encoder reaches `_z_query_encode(wbf, &msg->_body._query)`; Query inner-body byte-equivalence against wz Query::default() (separate Query codec, additional analysis surface). 1-round closure with the layered fixture pattern from R102/R105.

Latent spec-correctness audit — R108a's defect (mid `value=` missing on REQUEST despite it being present on PUSH / RESPONSE) suggests a textbook gate: every wz envelope codec's `<sce:flag name="mid">` declaration should carry an explicit `value=` matching the upstream MID, and a unit-level check (or SCE-side T-rule) should reject mid-without-value at parse time. Out of scope for R108a / R108b; recorded here for future hardening.
