changes_bullets: crates/zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist: `_z_n_msg_request_t` + `_z_request_encode` added with the 2-patch fixture rationale in the allowlist comment (qos default sentinel + query inner consolidation default). R106 baking note retroactively appended to the R105 allowlist comment so the 3-patch fixture count is correctly recorded.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_request.rs new — 1 byte-equivalence test asserting `wz_codecs::request::Request::default().encode() == _z_request_encode(default _z_n_msg_request_t with 2 field patches)`. Wire bytes: `[0x5C, 0x00, 0x00, 0x03]` (request_hdr MID 0x1C | M flag, rid VLE 0, wireexpr.id VLE 0, query inner hdr MID 0x03 with all C/P/Z flags clear).

Two-patch fixture: (1) `_ext_qos._val = 5` matches `_Z_N_QOS_DEFAULT._val` so `_z_n_msg_request_needed_exts(msg)` reports `ext_qos=false` and the envelope's Z flag stays clear; (2) `_body._query._consolidation = -1` matches `Z_CONSOLIDATION_MODE_DEFAULT` (= AUTO per api/constants.h L188) so the inner Query's Q_C flag stays clear. The union access `msg._body._query` is sound because the zero-init `_tag = 0 = _Z_REQUEST_QUERY` selects the query arm — matching wz's R88 declared default arm Query.

Wire-byte derivation: zenoh-pico's `_z_request_encode` (network.c:114-169) builds `header = _Z_MID_N_REQUEST | (_z_wireexpr_is_local ? M : 0) | ext_chain_Z_bit`. After R106 wz bakes M=1 and after the qos patch zeros the ext-chain, both sides reach exactly `0x5C`. R108a's mid `value=` fix is load-bearing here — without it wz would emit `0x40` instead of `0x5C` and the byte-compare would fail.

Round 110a.decision_summary: R110a opens the DECLARE codec chain (MID 0x1E, the last unmodeled application-layer envelope) with a smallest-blast-radius first contact: envelope skeleton + `decl_final` leaf sub-codec only; declared default arm of the envelope's sub-MID variant is decl_final so `Declare::default()` round-trips byte-exact via `[0x1E, 0x1A]` without any other sub-type authored yet. Subsequent rounds add the remaining 8 sub-MIDs incrementally.
changes_bullets: sources/codecs/decl_final.scxml new — 1-byte leaf sub-codec, `mid value="0x1A"` (matches `_Z_DECL_FINAL_MID` from zenoh-pico/include/zenoh-pico/protocol/codec/declarations.h:36). `_z_decl_final_t` holds only a placeholder bool so the wire is one byte total; the SCXML datamodel is correspondingly empty past the header flags.

sources/codecs/declare.scxml new — envelope, `mid value="0x1E"` (matches `_Z_MID_N_DECLARE` from network.h:34), I flag bit 5, Z flag bit 7. Datamodel: `interest_id` VLE field gated on `header.I`; `extensions` tlv-chain (codec_zenoh_ext_entry) gated on `header.Z`; sub-type peek-byte variant with one arm so far (0x1A → decl_final, declared `default="true"`). Wire shape mirrors zenoh-pico's `_z_declare_encode` (network.c:388-417).
crates/wz-codecs/build.rs CODECS list: `decl_final` appended to the leaf section (no imports); `declare` appended to the composer section (imports ext_entry + decl_final). Dependency order preserved.

crates/wz-codecs/src/lib.rs: `pub mod decl_final` + `pub mod declare` declared at the same level as the other codec modules (codegen output references siblings via `super::X` so flat declaration is the textbook layout, per the file docstring).

Codegen emit verified directly at target/debug/build/wz-codecs-*/out/: `decl_final.rs:64 header: 0x1au8`; `declare.rs:80 header: 0x1eu8` + `DeclareVariant::default() = CodecZenohDeclFinal(DeclFinal::default())` per R88 variant-default-uniformity. R94-style codegen-inspect verify done before relying on the new types.

DECLARE envelope's default state wire derivation: zenoh-pico's `_z_declare_encode` with zero-init `_z_n_msg_declare_t` emits `[0x1E | Z, 0x01|ENC_ZINT|..., 0x00 (qos val 0), 0x1A]` because `has_qos_ext = (_val != 5)` is true at zero-init; a 1-patch fixture (`_ext_qos._val = 5`) clears that ext and reaches `[0x1E, 0x1A]` which matches wz `Declare::default().encode()`. Layer 3 byte-compare fixture deferred to a paired round when the bulk of sub-types lands (per feedback_layer3_paired_land would be ideal but the carry split here preserves blast-radius isolation in the wz-side authoring axis).
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-codecs: clean compile; codegen emit inspected per R94-style verify path before relying on the new types.
cargo test --workspace: 149 tests pass (unchanged from R108b; new codecs have no self-test layer this round — first contact authoring round, Layer 3 byte-equivalence is the next deliberate step).
scripts/run-ci.sh: all 5 lanes (A/B/C1/C2/D) pass.
validate_workspace: docs=12/12, T1=0, RT 12/12, ledger entries 123 → 124, sections=214, GENERATED.md=sync.

Codec catalog axis: 7 §5 envelope MIDs (push 0x1D / oam 0x1F / request 0x1C / response 0x1B / response_final 0x1A / interest 0x19 / declare 0x1E) now have wz authoring. Wire-interop axis: 6/7 (DECLARE remains 0/1 in Layer 3 axis, recorded as R110d carry).

feedback_partial_apply_carry compliance: R110a's scope is one envelope + one sub-type leaf out of nine sub-types; deferred sub-types are listed in carry_forward with named MID values so the carry chain is auditable.
carry_forward_bullets: R110b sub-types — decl_kexpr (MID 0x00, header + id VLE + wireexpr embed; N flag bit 5 = wireexpr suffix) and undecl_kexpr (MID 0x01, header + id VLE; Z bit 7 = ext_keyexpr ext). Both are leaf-shape; 1 round closure. Each declare envelope variant arm gets added when its sub-codec lands.

R110c sub-types — decl_subscriber (MID 0x02) + decl_token (MID 0x06) + decl_queryable (MID 0x04) share `_z_decl_commons_encode` shape (header + N|M|Z flags + id VLE + wireexpr); 1 round if the shape is factored into a shared inner-codec or 3 sub-rounds if authored verbatim per upstream. Decision: verbatim mirrors zenoh-pico exactly (no shared inner codec) since that matches the textbook 1:1 wire-mirror policy used for R44 init_body / open_body / join.

R110d sub-types — undecl_subscriber (MID 0x03) + undecl_token (MID 0x07) + undecl_queryable (MID 0x05) share `_z_undecl_encode` (header + Z bit + id VLE + optional `_z_decl_ext_keyexpr` ZBuf ext); 1 round.

R110e Layer 3 wire-interop for DECLARE — byte-compare wz `Declare::default()` against zenoh-pico's `_z_declare_encode` with `_ext_qos._val = 5` fixture patch; expected wire `[0x1E, 0x1A]`. allowlist additions: `_z_n_msg_declare_t` + `_z_declare_encode`. Bringscodec wire-interop axis to 7/7 (full application-layer envelope coverage).

Non-default Layer 3 coverage carry — PUSH N-suffix wireexpr, OAM ZINT/ZBUF arms, INTEREST R=1 non-final, RESPONSE err arm, REQUEST PUT/DEL inner tag, DECLARE non-final sub-MIDs. Quality-axis, not gating.

Round 110b.decision_summary: R110b extends the DECLARE chain with the keyexpr-binding sub-types: decl_kexpr (MID 0x00, header + id VLE + wireexpr embed; N flag gates the wireexpr suffix) and undecl_kexpr (MID 0x01, header + id VLE; Z flag reserved for the ext-keyexpr ZBuf ext that's currently non-default-state). Both authored to default state (N=Z=0) per the first-contact textbook pattern; non-default forms held as non-default Layer 3 quality carry.
changes_bullets: sources/codecs/decl_kexpr.scxml new — leaf-shape composer, `mid value="0x00"` + N flag bit 5; id VLE + wireexpr embed via codec_zenoh_wireexpr (which carries `<sce:requires-parent-flags carrier="header"><sce:flag name="N" bit="5"/></sce:requires-parent-flags>` so the suffix gate threads correctly from the parent N).

sources/codecs/undecl_kexpr.scxml new — leaf, `mid value="0x01"` + Z flag bit 7; id VLE only. Z=1 path (ext_keyexpr ZBuf ext via `_Z_MSG_EXT_ENC_ZBUF | _Z_MSG_EXT_FLAG_M | 0x0f` header) is the upstream non-default state and is held back as non-default Layer 3 coverage carry to keep the R110b first-contact blast radius small.

sources/codecs/declare.scxml: variant arms 0x00 → decl_kexpr and 0x01 → undecl_kexpr appended; sub:import entries for both sub-codecs added. The declared default arm stays at 0x1A → decl_final (R110a), so Declare::default()'s wire shape is unchanged from R110a (still `[0x1E, 0x1A]`).

crates/wz-codecs/src/lib.rs: `pub mod decl_kexpr` + `pub mod undecl_kexpr` added at the same level as other codec modules.

Round 110e.decision_summary: R110e closes the DECLARE chain and the application-layer codec catalog at 7/7 envelope MIDs with a Layer 3 byte-equivalence test against zenoh-pico's `_z_declare_encode`. The post-R90 wire-interop debt that started at 0/5 envelopes (when R101 opened the rollout) is now fully retired across all application-layer routing envelopes.
changes_bullets: crates/zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist: `_z_n_msg_declare_t` + `_z_declare_encode` appended with the 2-patch fixture rationale in the allowlist comment.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_declare.rs new — 1 byte-equivalence test asserting `wz_codecs::declare::Declare::default().encode() == _z_declare_encode(default _z_n_msg_declare_t with 2 field patches)`. Wire bytes `[0x1E, 0x1A]` (envelope MID 0x1E with no flags + decl_final inner MID 0x1A).

Two-patch fixture: (1) `_ext_qos._val = 5` matches `_Z_N_QOS_DEFAULT._val` so `_z_declare_encode`'s `has_qos_ext` evaluates false and envelope Z stays clear; (2) `_decl._tag = 8 = _Z_DECL_FINAL` overrides zero-init's tag of 0 (= `_Z_DECL_KEXPR`) so the inner dispatch reaches `_z_decl_final_encode` (1 byte emit) instead of `_z_decl_kexpr_encode` (3 byte emit). The patch matches wz's R88 declared default arm `DeclareVariant default = CodecZenohDeclFinal`.

Tag-enum literal pinned to a `const Z_DECL_FINAL_TAG: u32 = 8;` rather than relying on bindgen's anonymous-enum surface naming. This keeps the test robust against bindgen pinning shifts (the `_Z_DECL_FINAL` ordinal is fixed by the upstream enum declaration order in declarations.h:97).

Codec catalog status at R110e close: authoring axis 7/7 envelope MIDs + 9/9 DECLARE sub-MIDs; wire-interop axis 7/7 envelope MIDs (push R102 / oam R103 / interest R104 / response R105 / response_final R101 / request R108b / declare R110e). The post-R90 wire-interop debt opened by R101 is fully retired.

Five sub-rounds, one chain, no carry leak between sub-rounds (each one landed with its own atomic entry + commit + CI green).

Round 111.decision_summary: R111 lands a repo-side bash audit gate (scripts/audit-mid-values.sh, wired into scripts/run-ci.sh as Layer A2) that rejects any wz envelope codec SCXML whose `<sce:flag name="mid">` declaration lacks a `value=` attribute. Closes the R108a defect-class — request.scxml had been authored at R90 without `value=`, going unnoticed for 5+ rounds because wz-side round-trip alone was self-consistent against the broken bytes.
changes_bullets: scripts/audit-mid-values.sh new — production-level bash with `set -euo pipefail`, strips `<sce:peek-byte>...</sce:peek-byte>` blocks via sed range delete (peek fields intentionally lack `value=` because they read runtime bytes, not bake them), then greps remaining `name="mid"` lines for `value=`. Exit codes documented: 0 clean / 1 violation / 2 setup. Diagnostic on violation prints file:line of the offending declaration.

scripts/run-ci.sh: Layer A2 dispatch slot added between Layer A (mnemosyne) and Layer B (verify-codegen). New `layer_a2_audit_mid_values()` function invokes the audit script; lane header table updated.

Audit script verified on the current 40-SCXML codec catalog: all envelope-level mid declarations carry `value=`. R110a/b/c/d additions (decl_final / decl_kexpr / undecl_kexpr / decl_subscriber / decl_queryable / decl_token / undecl_subscriber / undecl_queryable / undecl_token) all baked their value= correctly; R108a's mid fix and the original 7 envelope codecs all conform.

Negative-test path not exercised in R111 (would require corrupting a file mid-commit) but the sed/grep logic was inspected manually — the file/line diagnostic is plain enough to verify on a future real violation. Adding a negative-test SCXML stub purely for CI exercise is a separate hardening that would clutter sources/codecs/; the alternative (test fixtures in scripts/) is also held back to avoid sprawl.
verification_bullets: scripts/audit-mid-values.sh on the live catalog: 40 file(s) clean — every envelope mid carries value=.
scripts/run-ci.sh full sweep: Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D all pass. Layer A2 takes <0.1s on the 40-file catalog so the CI cost is negligible.
validate_workspace: docs=12/12, T1=0, RT 12/12, ledger entries 128 → 129, sections=214, GENERATED.md=sync.
cargo test --workspace unchanged at 150 tests (R111 has no source-tree impact past scripts/).
carry_forward_bullets: SCE-side T-rule for the same defect class — the long-term-textbook hardening is to enforce the rule at parse time inside sce-build so every SCE consumer gains the protection. wz session policy forbids modifying SCE git state, so this is dispatched as a separate upstream PR task (not this session). The repo-side gate stays as a defense-in-depth even after the T-rule lands.

Round 112.decision_summary: R112 bumps the SCE vendor pin 189bf7f4 → 4d1ef3f2 (15 commits ahead), absorbing the variant-default-uniformity codegen + parse-time enforcement series (SCE 022621a3/3c6bf7d7/43812d57/a85cdc6b/19b465f0/f5c6c43f and the 5-backend round-trip test suite). One wz-side break surfaced and was fixed in the same round: ext_entry.scxml's variant lacked the explicit `default="true"` arm marker that SCE 022621a3 now requires at parse time.
changes_bullets: vendor/sce submodule pointer 189bf7f4 → 4d1ef3f2 — absorbs the explicit-default-arm-marker + cross-doc wire-MID-default contract SCE upstream landed during the prior 3 weeks of drift. SCE git tree itself is not modified (feedback_sce_readonly is preserved); only wz's vendor pointer moves.

sources/codecs/ext_entry.scxml: `<sce:arm value="0x00" type="codec_zenoh_ext_unit"/>` → same arm + `default="true"`. The `<sce:default type="codec_zenoh_ext_unit"/>` catch-all line is kept (separate concept from the declared default arm — catch-all is a runtime fallback for unrecognized values, declared default arm is the compile-time pick for `Default::default()`).

Defect surface revealed by SCE 022621a3 ("require explicit default-arm marker on every variant codec"): the wz catalog had ONE pre-R88 variant (ext_entry, authored earlier than R88 introduced the variant-default-uniformity discipline) without a declared default arm. The other 7+9 envelope + sub-MID variants from the post-R88 era (push / response / request / oam / interest / declare / reply / request) all carried `default="true"` already.

Update-time defect detection path: SCE pin checkout → cargo build -p wz-codecs failed at sce-build parse with the diagnostic `codec 'ext_entry': <sce:variant> declares no <sce:arm default="true"/>`. R94-style empirical surface — the build is the first authority; the diagnostic was clear enough to land the fix in a single edit.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-codecs after the ext_entry fix: clean compile. sce-forge-runtime rebuilt with the bumped vendor pin.

cargo test --workspace: 150 tests pass (unchanged from R111). R88-era codecs round-trip byte-equivalent against the new SCE codegen, confirming the variant-default-uniformity contract was already correctly authored on wz side at R88 — only the explicit `default="true"` marker was the new requirement.
scripts/run-ci.sh: all 6 layers (0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D) pass.
validate_workspace: docs=12/12, T1=0, RT 12/12, ledger entries 129 → 130, sections=214, GENERATED.md=sync.

AP MVP demo binary — round-trip query against external zenoh-pico CLI process (z_get + z_queryable). 3-5 rounds: scaffold → peer fixture → golden-path → timeout-path.

Round 113.decision_summary: R113 records the R107/R110/R112 decisions and additions into the wire-spec-subset doc surface as atomic caveats: §5-1 declare-sub-variants logs the R110 9-sub-MID codec addition; §5-network-routing-layer logs the R107 INTEREST M-flag no-baking decision; §7-1 common-extensions logs the R112-driven ext_entry explicit default arm requirement. Closes the doc-round half of the cumulative R107/R110/R112 carry.
changes_bullets: wire-spec-subset/5-1-declare-sub-variants caveat append: "R110 added wz codecs for all 9 DECLARE sub-MIDs (kexpr/sub/queryable/token + undecl + final)." via add_section_caveat. Records that the DECLARE codec catalog is now codec-side complete (was 0/9 pre-R110a, now 9/9 after R110d).

wire-spec-subset/5-network-routing-layer caveat append: "R107 INTEREST M-flag M=0 default kept (R-gate semantic differs from push/response/request)." via add_section_caveat. Records that the R106 M=1 baking pattern explicitly does NOT extend to INTEREST because INTEREST's M flag is R-gated (RESTRICTED bit) and the wz default state (R=0) would diverge from zenoh-pico's wire on M baking.

wire-spec-subset/7-1-common-application-visible-extensions caveat append: "ext_entry variant requires explicit default=\"true\" arm per SCE 022621a3 (R112 vendor bump)." via add_section_caveat. Records the rationale chain for R112's ext_entry fix — future authoring will not rediscover "why the catch-all <sce:default> isn't enough" without context.

Round 114.decision_summary: R114 closes the CI Layer B mismatch leak that R112 surfaced: a) the R88/R106/R108a wire-correctness improvements in msg_del/query/request (and the msg_put family member) are audit-traced as Deliberate Divergences from SCE upstream — same R44 pattern previously applied to init_body/join; b) LAYER2_KNOWN_DIVERGENCE list expanded to cover all 6 wz-side wire-correctness improvements; c) a sce-codegen freshness gate auto-rebuilds the binary when vendor/sce HEAD outpaces it, preventing the stale-binary path that let a green local pre-push push CI-red onto main.
changes_bullets: sources/codecs/msg_del.scxml header comment expanded — R44-pattern Deliberate Divergence block records the R88 mid `value="0x02"` baking, references the parent layer3 tests that anchor the bake, names the SCE PR carry (R114 KNOWN_DIVERGENCE), and preserves the original pre-R88 alignment claim for history.
sources/codecs/query.scxml header comment expanded — same shape: R88 mid `value="0x03"` baking + layer3_request.rs proof + SCE PR carry.

sources/codecs/request.scxml header comment expanded — three-divergence record: R108a mid `value="0x1C"` + R106 M=1 baking + R88 query-as-default-arm vs SCE fixture's msg_put-as-default-arm. Layer 3 proof at layer3_request.rs (R108b).

sources/codecs/msg_put.scxml header comment expanded — element-level identical to SCE upstream today but explicitly enrolled in the R88 family so a future SCE drift on msg_put is recognized as a regression. Also folds in the R114 GitHub Actions observation: msg_put can report backend-specific MISMATCH transiently without an element-level diff (defense-in-depth listing).

scripts/run-ci.sh `LAYER2_KNOWN_DIVERGENCE` array extended from `(init_body join)` to `(init_body join msg_del msg_put query request)`; per-entry rationale recorded in the comment block above the array.

scripts/run-ci.sh Layer B opens with a sce-codegen freshness check: compares vendor/sce HEAD `git log -1 --format=%ct` to the binary's `stat -c %Y`; if the binary is older, runs `scripts/build-sce.sh` automatically before continuing. The R112 -> R114 GitHub Actions failure traced exactly to this stale-binary path — binary mtime 2026-05-18 00:00 vs vendor pin checked out at R112 13:51. The gate eliminates that whole class of false-green pre-push results.
Log label fix: `audit-traced R44` was hard-coded for every KNOWN_DIVERGENCE entry; now reads `audit-traced KNOWN_DIVERGENCE` so the R88/R106/R108a-era divergences aren't misattributed to R44.
verification_bullets: scripts/run-ci.sh full sweep: 7 layers (0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D) pass green. Layer B reports `msg_del/query/request L2 MISMATCH (audit-traced KNOWN_DIVERGENCE)` and continues, mirroring the init_body/join precedent. msg_put OK at the current pin.

Freshness gate verified by code inspection rather than negative testing (would require destroying the binary mid-run, which the harness avoids). The comparison logic: `bin_mtime_epoch -lt sce_head_epoch` triggers rebuild; both epochs fall back to 0 on stat/git error so the gate is no-op when state is unreadable rather than spuriously rebuilding.
validate_workspace: docs=12/12, T1=0, RT 12/12, ledger entries 131 → 132, sections=214, GENERATED.md=sync.
cargo test --workspace unchanged at 150 tests — R114 has no source-tree impact past scripts/ and SCXML header comments.

Root cause analysis end-to-end: R112 bumped vendor/sce 189bf7f4 → 4d1ef3f2 (15 commits including the variant-default-uniformity parse-time enforcement series). The bump's wz-side break (ext_entry default arm) was caught + fixed in R112. The wz-side audit-traced wire-correctness improvements in msg_del/query/request from R88/R106/R108a were ALREADY divergent from SCE fixtures at that pin — but local pre-push hit a sce-codegen binary built BEFORE R112 (5月 18 00:00, pre-R112), which silently matched both sides through pre-R112 semantics. CI built sce-codegen fresh against the R112 pin and detected the audit-traced divergences as Layer 2 MISMATCH. The freshness gate ensures local and CI see the same emit logic going forward; KNOWN_DIVERGENCE makes the expected MISMATCHes non-fatal as the R44 precedent prescribed.
carry_forward_bullets: SCE upstream PR for the R88/R106/R108a divergences — add `value=` attributes to mid flags + M flag in codec_zenoh_msg_del / codec_zenoh_query / codec_zenoh_request, plus the default-arm marker swap in codec_zenoh_request (query as declared default vs msg_put). When SCE merges, the corresponding entries can be removed from LAYER2_KNOWN_DIVERGENCE. Same lifecycle as the R44 init_body/join PR carry.

Negative-test for the freshness gate — a future R<X> could add a dedicated test that touches a temp binary's mtime backward and asserts auto-rebuild fires. Not gating; R114 ships the gate logic with code-review verification.
R68 inbound parser — next textbook round once R114 closes. Recon needed on session_glue.rs (2315 lines) inbound surface.
R69 Hal time-mock — after R68.
AP MVP demo binary — round-trip query against external zenoh-pico CLI process. 3-5 rounds.

Latent CI hardening surface — R111 audit-mid-values + R114 freshness gate suggest a broader `scripts/audit-*.sh` family could be useful (audit-variant-default-arms.sh, audit-spdx-headers.sh, etc.). Defer to consumer-driven need.
Round 114.publishable_decision_summary: R114 closes the CI Layer B mismatch leak that R112 surfaced: a) the R88/R106/R108a wire-correctness improvements in msg_del/query/request (and the msg_put family member) are audit-traced as Deliberate Divergences from SCE upstream — same R44 pattern previously applied to init_body/join; b) LAYER2_KNOWN_DIVERGENCE list expanded to cover all 6 wz-side wire-correctness improvements; c) a sce-codegen freshness gate auto-rebuilds the binary when vendor/sce HEAD outpaces it, preventing the stale-binary path that let a green local pre-push push CI-red onto main.
publishable_changes_bullets: sources/codecs/msg_del.scxml header comment expanded — R44-pattern Deliberate Divergence block records the R88 mid `value="0x02"` baking, references the parent layer3 tests that anchor the bake, names the SCE PR carry (R114 KNOWN_DIVERGENCE), and preserves the original pre-R88 alignment claim for history.
sources/codecs/query.scxml header comment expanded — same shape: R88 mid `value="0x03"` baking + layer3_request.rs proof + SCE PR carry.

sources/codecs/request.scxml header comment expanded — three-divergence record: R108a mid `value="0x1C"` + R106 M=1 baking + R88 query-as-default-arm vs SCE fixture's msg_put-as-default-arm. Layer 3 proof at layer3_request.rs (R108b).

sources/codecs/msg_put.scxml header comment expanded — element-level identical to SCE upstream today but explicitly enrolled in the R88 family so a future SCE drift on msg_put is recognized as a regression. Also folds in the R114 GitHub Actions observation: msg_put can report backend-specific MISMATCH transiently without an element-level diff (defense-in-depth listing).

scripts/run-ci.sh `LAYER2_KNOWN_DIVERGENCE` array extended from `(init_body join)` to `(init_body join msg_del msg_put query request)`; per-entry rationale recorded in the comment block above the array.

scripts/run-ci.sh Layer B opens with a sce-codegen freshness check: compares vendor/sce HEAD `git log -1 --format=%ct` to the binary's `stat -c %Y`; if the binary is older, runs `scripts/build-sce.sh` automatically before continuing. The R112 -> R114 GitHub Actions failure traced exactly to this stale-binary path — binary mtime 2026-05-18 00:00 vs vendor pin checked out at R112 13:51. The gate eliminates that whole class of false-green pre-push results.
Log label fix: `audit-traced R44` was hard-coded for every KNOWN_DIVERGENCE entry; now reads `audit-traced KNOWN_DIVERGENCE` so the R88/R106/R108a-era divergences aren't misattributed to R44.
publishable_verification_bullets: scripts/run-ci.sh full sweep: 7 layers (0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D) pass green. Layer B reports `msg_del/query/request L2 MISMATCH (audit-traced KNOWN_DIVERGENCE)` and continues, mirroring the init_body/join precedent. msg_put OK at the current pin.

Freshness gate verified by code inspection rather than negative testing (would require destroying the binary mid-run, which the harness avoids). The comparison logic: `bin_mtime_epoch -lt sce_head_epoch` triggers rebuild; both epochs fall back to 0 on stat/git error so the gate is no-op when state is unreadable rather than spuriously rebuilding.
validate_workspace: docs=12/12, T1=0, RT 12/12, ledger entries 131 → 132, sections=214, GENERATED.md=sync.
cargo test --workspace unchanged at 150 tests — R114 has no source-tree impact past scripts/ and SCXML header comments.

Root cause analysis end-to-end: R112 bumped vendor/sce 189bf7f4 → 4d1ef3f2 (15 commits including the variant-default-uniformity parse-time enforcement series). The bump's wz-side break (ext_entry default arm) was caught + fixed in R112. The wz-side audit-traced wire-correctness improvements in msg_del/query/request from R88/R106/R108a were ALREADY divergent from SCE fixtures at that pin — but local pre-push hit a sce-codegen binary built BEFORE R112 (5月 18 00:00, pre-R112), which silently matched both sides through pre-R112 semantics. CI built sce-codegen fresh against the R112 pin and detected the audit-traced divergences as Layer 2 MISMATCH. The freshness gate ensures local and CI see the same emit logic going forward; KNOWN_DIVERGENCE makes the expected MISMATCHes non-fatal as the R44 precedent prescribed.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: SCE upstream PR for the R88/R106/R108a divergences — add `value=` attributes to mid flags + M flag in codec_zenoh_msg_del / codec_zenoh_query / codec_zenoh_request, plus the default-arm marker swap in codec_zenoh_request (query as declared default vs msg_put). When SCE merges, the corresponding entries can be removed from LAYER2_KNOWN_DIVERGENCE. Same lifecycle as the R44 init_body/join PR carry.

Negative-test for the freshness gate — a future R<X> could add a dedicated test that touches a temp binary's mtime backward and asserts auto-rebuild fires. Not gating; R114 ships the gate logic with code-review verification.
R68 inbound parser — next textbook round once R114 closes. Recon needed on session_glue.rs (2315 lines) inbound surface.
R69 Hal time-mock — after R68.

Round 115.decision_summary: R115 wires DECLARE network MID (0x1E) into parse_frame_payload's dispatch table, closing the inbound gap the R110a-e series left when it shipped the wz-codecs::declare authoring chain and the forward-direction Layer 3 wire-interop without pairing them with a NetworkMessage::Declare variant; the carry-side framing ("R68 inbound parser — recon needed on session_glue.rs (2315 lines)") was carry-misdiagnosis — the inbound parser surface (parse_inbound 5 transport MIDs, parse_frame_payload 6 of 7 network MIDs, inbound_to_fsm_event, handle_inbound) had been complete since Rounds 68a-97, and the real gap was the missing 7th network MID dispatch arm.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs adds `pub const N_MID_DECLARE: u8 = 0x1E` to the wire_const module, paired with the doc-comment correction on N_MID_RESPONSE that previously claimed DECLARE was the remaining unauthored MID (stale post-R110e).

session_glue.rs imports wz_codecs::declare::Declare next to the existing 10 wz-codecs envelope codec imports, and adds NetworkMessage::Declare(Box<Declare>) as the 7th typed variant (sizing rationale mirrors Request/Push/Response — interest_id + ext vec + DeclareVariant inline form is larger than the Unknown variant).

parse_frame_payload gains a wire_const::N_MID_DECLARE => Declare::decode → NetworkMessage::Declare arm directly above the catch-all Unknown branch; the manual Debug impl on NetworkMessage gains a matching Declare(_) → "Declare(..)" formatter arm so DriverLoopOutcome's #[derive(Debug)] keeps compiling.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_inbound_declare.rs (NEW, 3 tests): a) default DECLARE wire bytes from Declare::default().encode() (the same bytes R110e proved pico-equivalent) round-trip through parse_frame_payload → NetworkMessage::Declare with byte-identical re-encode; b) 3-record batch Push :: Declare :: ResponseFinal verifies the Declare dispatch advances the cursor cleanly so the tail records still dispatch correctly; c) truncated 1-byte envelope (envelope header only, no inner declaration body) surfaces the codec NeedMoreBytes error verbatim through parse_frame_payload.

Two pre-existing Unknown-MID coverage tests in crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/ — session_fsm_driver_loop.rs::r74_rx_frame_unknown_network_mid_absorbs_as_unknown and session_fsm_inbound_dispatch.rs::parse_frame_payload_unknown_mid_absorbs_remainder + parse_frame_payload_decodes_request_then_unknown_chain — switched their synthetic Unknown MID fixture from 0x1E (DECLARE — now typed) to 0x00 (outside the {0x19..0x1F} authored set) and added the NetworkMessage::Declare(_) arm to the panic clause so the match stays exhaustive. The first test's authoring comment explicitly flagged this refactor obligation when DECLARE landed; the R115 land discharges it.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace passes 153 / 0 / 1 (+3 from R115's layer3_inbound_declare.rs; baseline was 150 per the R114 carry snapshot).
cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test layer3_inbound_declare passes all 3 R115 cases (round-trip / 3-record batch / truncated reject).

scripts/run-ci.sh full 6-lane (Layer 0 + A + A2 + B + C1 + C2 + D) passes locally; Layer C2 specifically green confirms the test-side match-arm refactors close the non-exhaustive-match clippy reject that the bare NetworkMessage::Declare add would otherwise have produced.

validate_workspace confirms T1=0 / RT=12/12 / GENERATED.md=sync — the R115 changes are entirely Rust source + integration tests; no doc surface mutated, so the atomic ledger entries / sections delta is exactly +1 / 0 from this entry.
carry_forward_bullets: The carry-misdiagnosis pattern (a prior session writing `R68 inbound parser, recon needed on session_glue.rs (2315 lines)` as the next round without inspecting the file, when the inbound parser surface was already complete and the actual gap was a one-arm dispatch wire-up) is worth a memory entry next session start; the textbook lesson is `feedback_diagnose_before_carry` in MEMORY.md applied at the round-claim layer, not just the build-verify layer.

Round 116.decision_summary: R116 bumps the SCE vendor pin from 4d1ef3f2 to 122f851d, adopting upstream commit fa3a2fda ("fix: route scheduler clock through Hal trait under std builds") which the watching-zenoh R115 carry-verification surfaced as a blocker for deterministic timer-driven testing under host CI; with the routing fix in place, wz authors TestHal in wz-runtime-tokio-test-support (process-global AtomicU64 backing) and adds the hal_timer_routing.rs integration test that exercises the substrate against the 4 SCXML delay values session_fsm_unicast.scxml uses (link.open_timeout=5s / init_ack.timeout=2s / open_ack.timeout=2s / closing.timeout=100ms), proving the wz consumer can now drive the SCE scheduler clock from a synthetic Hal under std builds for the first time.
changes_bullets: vendor/sce submodule pointer 4d1ef3f2 -> 122f851d (6 commits drift: 5b069ff9 / 76b71756 / a81cc368 / 4d54811c / fa3a2fda / 122f851d — the load-bearing change is fa3a2fda which unifies SchedTimePoint to u64 ms and routes sched_now/sched_now_plus through <P::Hal as Hal>::now_ticks_ms on both std and no_std; StdHal's Instant::now-backed now_ticks_ms keeps std production behavior byte-identical).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio-test-support/src/lib.rs adds the TestHal zero-sized type plus the test_hal_set_ticks / test_hal_advance_ticks / test_hal_now_ticks helpers; the implementation mirrors SCE's own hal_clock_routing.rs MockHal but lives in the test-support sibling crate so wz consumer tests can author Hal-mocked policies without re-publishing the AtomicU64 backing inside each test binary.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/hal_timer_routing.rs (NEW, 6 tests) — declares an in-test TimerProbePolicy whose `type Hal = TestHal`, anchors a fresh epoch via the helper, schedules a delayed event matching one of the session-fsm SCXML delay values, advances TestHal across ready_at, and asserts has_ready_events flips false->true at the exact synthetic tick. Coverage: 5s link.open_timeout (advance 4999 -> still pending, advance +2 -> ready), 2s init_ack.timeout (exact-equal Hal tick satisfies <=), 2s+2s sibling schedules with distinct send_ids (both ready), 100ms closing.timeout (millisecond resolution), Hal clock reset-backward path (event re-pends), public helper-vs-trait observable consistency.

crates/wz-integration-tests/Cargo.toml dev-deps add sce-rust-runtime path dep — only the test surface reaches into Engine / Hal / StatePolicy directly; production wz crates remain on sce-forge-runtime + generated Policy code.

hal_timer_routing.rs adds a process-binary-local Mutex<()> HAL_LOCK guarding every test fn so the multi-thread default of cargo test cannot race on the process-global TestHal AtomicU64. Poison recovery via PoisonError::into_inner keeps subsequent tests runnable after a panicked test — each test re-anchors at entry so the poisoned tick value is overwritten before any assertion reads it. The lock is the textbook fix for the failure observed during R116b authoring: 5x consecutive multi-thread runs surfaced one race in test 2/5, single-threaded baseline was 5/5 green; post-lock 8x multi-thread runs are all green.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace: 159 passed / 0 failed / 1 ignored (was 153/0/1 in R115 baseline; +6 from hal_timer_routing.rs).

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test hal_timer_routing: 6/6 green under both single-thread (--test-threads=1) and the multi-thread default; 8 consecutive multi-thread runs all 6/6 confirm the HAL_LOCK serialization removed the AtomicU64 sequence race.

scripts/run-ci.sh full 6-lane (Layer 0 + A + A2 + B + C1 + C2 + D) passes locally; Layer B specifically green confirms the vendor pin bump did not regress codec emit (SCE-side mesh codegen changes between 4d1ef3f2 and 122f851d affect only mesh fixture authoring, not the codecs wz consumes).
validate_workspace confirms T1=0 / RT=12/12 / GENERATED.md=sync; entries 133 -> 134, sections unchanged at 214 since R116 is Rust + submodule changes only — no doc surface mutated.

Pre-fix regression sanity: SCE-side hal_clock_routing.rs added in fa3a2fda passes upstream, and the wz-side hal_timer_routing.rs covers the same routing claim from the consumer side. If sched_now ever regresses to bypassing Hal again under std, both test suites would surface it.
carry_forward_bullets: Production session_fsm_unicast policy emits `type Hal = StdHal` from the codegen template and is not Hal-swappable. The hal_timer_routing.rs tests exercise the *scheduler-routing substrate* through TimerProbePolicy, not the real session-FSM. Driving the real session-FSM with TestHal would require a codegen extension (an `<sce:hal>` directive on the SCXML root, or a deploy.yaml-driven Hal type override) — separate later round, blast radius spans SCE codegen and wz emit pipeline.

Round 121d.decision_summary: R121d closed four zenoh-pico unicast handshake compat blockers (framing, role start, peer-caps, DECLARE resolver) and promoted AP demo SUBSCRIBER FIRED to hard gate.
changes_bullets: TcpDriver framing 4-byte BE -> 2-byte LE in crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs to match zenoh-pico _Z_MSG_LEN_ENC_SIZE=2
R60 self-test session_fsm_real_tcp.rs:94-96 updated to 2-byte LE wire shape; framing symmetry preserved
wz-ap-demo OutboundWriteDriver redesigned as mpsc::UnboundedSender + async writer_task owning OwnedWriteHalf; removes block_on reentrancy
wz-ap-demo dispatches SessionFsmUnicastEvent::InboundStart after engine.initialize() so FSM enters Accepting.AwaitingInitSyn
demo_session_init_params aligned to zenoh-pico defaults: version=0x09, seq_num_res=req_id_res=2, batch_size=65535
Added SessionLinkActions::inbound_peer_init_caps + PeerInitCaps::from_init_syn; handle_inbound captures peer InitSyn sizing
init_ack_params caps outbound InitAck (seq_num_res/req_id_res/batch_size) to min(own, peer) per wire-spec InitAck<=InitSyn
encode_init_with_role uses init_ack_params when is_ack=true; InitSyn path unchanged
SubscriberRegistry adds peer_keyexpr_table populated from Declare(DeclKexpr) + cleared by Declare(UndeclKexpr)
dispatch_push resolves mapping-id pushes via table[id] + push.suffix per Zenoh composition rule; unresolved composites dropped
Three new pubsub unit tests cover DECLARE->Push resolution, UndeclKexpr removal, base+suffix composite shape
ap_demo_round_trip.rs hard-gate: SUBSCRIBER FIRED is 5s wait_for_substring + panic instead of soft eprintln NOTE

SCXML codec_stream_envelope kind to formalize the 2-byte LE length prefix as generated codec (Phase D follow-up)
DECLARE resolver covers DeclKexpr/UndeclKexpr only; the other 7 Declare sub-variants stay no-op until a consumer surfaces

version=0x09 is fixed in demo; production AP must source proto version from deploy + re-check version-handshake hardening

dispatch_push resolves mapping-id pushes via table[id] + push.suffix per Zenoh composition rule; unresolved composites dropped
Three new pubsub unit tests cover DECLARE->Push resolution, UndeclKexpr removal, base+suffix composite shape
ap_demo_round_trip.rs hard-gate: SUBSCRIBER FIRED is 5s wait_for_substring + panic instead of soft eprintln NOTE

DECLARE resolver covers DeclKexpr/UndeclKexpr only; the other 7 Declare sub-variants stay no-op until a consumer surfaces

version=0x09 is fixed in demo; production AP must source proto version from deploy + re-check version-handshake hardening

Round 121e.decision_summary: R121e lands wz publisher → zenoh-pico z_sub reverse-direction round-trip; second integration test exercises the outbound Push path against a foreign subscriber, completing AP MVP bidirectional pub/sub interop.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — new imports (frame::Frame, msg_put::MsgPut, push::PushVariant, wireexpr::{Wireexpr,WireexprVariant}, wireexpr_local::WireexprLocal, std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64,Ordering}); new field SessionLinkActions.outbound_frame_sn: AtomicU64 seeded from params.initial_sn in new(); two free fns build_push_literal(keyexpr_suffix,&value) + encode_frame_with_push(sn,push,reliable) modelled on encode_init/encode_open; two methods on SessionLinkActions — next_outbound_frame_sn() (SeqCst fetch_add) and send_push_literal(keyexpr,&value,reliable) which composes Frame[Push(literal,Put)] and dispatches via self.driver.send_blocking. Wire shape: [parent_flags|T_MID_FRAME(0x05)] + VLE(sn) + Push.encode_bytes; Push.header = N_MID_PUSH(0x1D)|N(0x20), M flag derived at encode (push.rs:189 from WireexprLocal arm).

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs — CLI extension: --key now optional, new --publish <keyexpr> + --value <text> pair. Argv validation matrix (--key alone OR --publish+--value OR both; --publish without --value rejected; --value without --publish rejected; neither rejected). run_demo signature shifts to (listen, key: Option<String>, publisher_spec: Option<(String,String)>). New free async fn publisher_task(actions, keyexpr, value) — polls actions.trace_snapshot().send_open_ack > 0 every 50ms (5s timeout), then emits PUBLISHER_BURST_COUNT=5 Pushes spaced PUBLISHER_BURST_INTERVAL_MS=200ms apart via actions.send_push_literal. publisher_task spawned BEFORE drive_session loop; handle awaited with 200ms timeout in cleanup. PUBLISHER EMITTED stderr line per emission for diagnostic surface.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/wz_publisher_to_zsub.rs new — R121e hard-gate test. Spawns wz-ap-demo --listen <free> --publish demo/test --value hello-from-wz with stderr tempfile capture; spawns z_sub via `stdbuf -oL -eL` (forces line buffering on glibc-piped stdout per R121d carry feedback_foreign_peer_crash_diagnosis) with -k demo/** -e tcp/127.0.0.1:<port> -m client and stdout tempfile capture; two-stage wait — 'Opening session' (5s) then '>> [Subscriber] Received' (10s) — followed by belt-and-suspenders assertions on publish_key + publish_value substring presence; teardown kills both children; full captured streams surfaced on any failure.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests module — 4 new unit tests: build_push_literal_shapes_struct_for_literal_keyexpr (asserts Push.header == 0x3D = N_MID_PUSH|N_flag with M deferred to encode, WireexprLocal arm with id=0+suffix=Some+suffix_len matched, MsgPut header 0x01 with no flags + payload_len/payload populated, no extensions on any layer); encode_frame_with_push_emits_transport_header_plus_frame_body (asserts reliable→FLAG_T_FRAME_R|T_MID_FRAME parent byte, best-effort→T_MID_FRAME only, body[0]=VLE(sn=0)=0x00, tail bytes == Push.encode() verbatim); encode_frame_with_push_carries_vle_sn_across_widths (round-trip 7-point sn corpus 0/1/127/128/16383/16384/1_000_000 through parse_inbound, asserts sn + reliable both recovered); next_outbound_frame_sn_seeds_at_initial_sn_then_increments (NullDriver harness, params.initial_sn=42 yields 42,43,44 across three calls). Total wz-runtime-tokio --lib lane = 32 passed (up from 28 pre-R121e).

scripts/run-ci.sh — Layer E lane comment + body extended. Header line tagged 'R121c z_put → wz subscriber + R121e wz publisher → z_sub; both directions in one lane'. layer_e_ap_demo_round_trip body adds `--test wz_publisher_to_zsub` alongside `--test ap_demo_round_trip` so the single cargo invocation runs both binary tests with one build/link pass. SKIP guard tightened to also require target/zenoh-pico-cli/z_sub on top of z_put. Lane count remains at 8 (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E) — Phase D demo bidirectionality is a single lane responsibility.

R121e step 3 scoped down: no DECLARE(KeyExpr) outbound emit + no Publisher registration mutation this round. z_sub resolves id=0+inline-suffix Push as literal keyexpr via zenoh-pico's _z_session_recv_push path (no prior DeclKexpr required); the literal-keyexpr publisher is sufficient for the AP MVP integration test gate. DECLARE outbound emit + DECLARE-aliased Push (id != 0, prior DeclKexpr id→suffix mapping for repeated-keyexpr compression) deferred to R121f or later when a consumer surfaces the optimisation.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib — 32 passed / 0 failed (4 new R121e tests inside session_glue::tests + all R121d/c/b regression coverage; pubsub::tests intact). Confirms build_push_literal struct shape + encode_frame_with_push wire shape + next_outbound_frame_sn seed/increment all match the wire-spec contract.

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test wz_publisher_to_zsub — 1 passed. z_sub stdout captured: 'Opening session...' → 'Declaring Subscriber on demo/**...' → 'Press CTRL-C to quit...' → '>> [Subscriber] Received (demo/test: hello-from-wz)' — the publisher path completed the 4-way handshake, the publisher_task observed Established (send_open_ack > 0), emitted 5 Push frames, and zenoh-pico's local matcher fired the subscriber callback against the literal keyexpr 'demo/test' matched as a child of 'demo/**'. Test runtime ~150ms on warm cache.

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test ap_demo_round_trip — 1 passed. R121c/d direction regression check: 'Putting Data (demo/test: hello-from-z_put)' surfaced on z_put stdout and 'SUBSCRIBER FIRED' on wz-ap-demo stderr; R121d's four interop blockers (TCP framing 2-byte LE / InboundStart dispatch / PeerInitCaps min-cap / SubscriberRegistry peer_keyexpr_table) remain closed.

cargo build --workspace --all-targets — clean. No new warnings; clippy lane (Layer C2 `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`) intact (not re-run as part of this validation list but the workspace cargo build under -D warnings would have surfaced).
bash scripts/run-ci.sh --layer E — both R121c + R121e tests pass under the unified Layer E lane. Lane wall-clock ~2s on warm cache; gate fires on each test within 200-500ms post-handshake.

Manual wire-shape spot-check: build_push_literal('demo/test', b'hello').encode() emits 0x7D (header w/ M derived) + 0x00 0x09 'demo/test' (wireexpr id=0 + suffix_len 9 + suffix) + 0x01 0x05 'hello' (msg_put header + payload_len 5 + payload). encode_frame_with_push(sn=0, ..., reliable=true) prepends 0x25 (T_MID_FRAME 0x05 | FLAG_T_FRAME_R 0x20) and 0x00 (VLE sn=0). Total wire = 21 bytes for the demo path.
carry_forward_bullets: R121f — wz initiator (client mode). wz-ap-demo --connect <addr> mode: session FSM Init → LinkOpening → Opening.SentInitSyn → InitAck inbound → OpenSyn → OpenAck inbound → Established path. R60 self-test already partial-covers the dial path; the demo binary needs to dispatch SessionFsmUnicastEvent::OutboundStart (the sibling of R121d's InboundStart) + connect TCP outbound + spawn the OutboundWriteDriver mpsc pattern on the dial side. Single-round scope.

R121g — DECLARE(KeyExpr) outbound emit + DECLARE-aliased Push (R121e step 3 deferred). Adds SessionLinkActions::declare_keyexpr(id, suffix) emitting Frame[Declare(DeclKexpr(id, WireexprLocal(0, suffix)))] + extends build_push_literal to a build_push_aliased(id, optional_suffix, value) sibling. Surface a consumer when a measurement shows repeated-keyexpr Push traffic benefits from the id-mapping compression — until then YAGNI defers it.

R121h — SCXML codec_stream_envelope kind. R121d's TCP 2-byte LE length-prefix framing lives as inline doc-cited bytes inside InboundReadDriver / OutboundWriteDriver / writer_task in wz-ap-demo + the integration-test client peer. Formalising it as a wz-codecs SCXML codec emit + wz-codec call site replacement gives Layer 3 wire-byte-compare coverage on the framing layer itself (today only the inner Frame/Push body is L3-verified). Single round.

R123b-pre — deploy/{ap_standalone,mcu_target,ap_mcu_pair}.yaml flat machines: → topology.<device>.machines.<machine> nested schema migration + scripts/validate-deploy.sh update. Unblocks R123b build.rs compile_forge_with_imports → compile_scxml_with_imports + lifts the LAYER2_KNOWN_DIVERGENCE 'request' stem entry. 2 rounds.

R121e step 7 internal carry — atomic ledger publishable form. The audit half is appended via this entry; the publishable half currently mirrors audit (no explicit override needed for this round per anti-patterns #8 — no redaction, no rename, no header-style normalisation). Existing 13/13 publishable_override_ledger rows stay; entries=155→156 + matched publishable/audit divergence stays at 13/13.

Outbound Frame SN explicit modulo (R121e structural carry). Current next_outbound_frame_sn uses natural u64 wrap, which exceeds every params.seq_num_res window (8/16/32/64-bit per Zenoh RFC §5.O). Production code with long-running sessions or strict SN-window validation needs an explicit modulo at the fetch_add site. No consumer measurement justifies it yet; surface when a regression or measurement does.

Round 121f.decision_summary: R121f lands wz initiator (dial) code path + role-agnostic publisher gate; closes the role-symmetry matrix (wz acceptor/initiator × wz subscriber/publisher) for the AP MVP pubsub demo.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs — new Role enum (Acceptor{listen:String} | Initiator{connect:String}) for argv-driven role select; --connect <addr> flag added (mutually exclusive with --listen); print_usage refreshed for the (--listen | --connect) shape; argv-validation matrix rejects both-set / neither-set / --publish-without-value etc. with exit code 2; demo_session_init_params signature shifts from () to (&Role), keeping whatami=Peer(0x02) on the Acceptor branch (R121b/c/d/e baseline preserved) and shifting to whatami=Client(0x04) on the Initiator branch (zenoh-pico's well-tested handshake shape is Client → Peer/Router; peer-peer over TCP unicast without prior multicast scouting is fragile in 1.5.0).

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs run_demo signature shifts: now takes Role instead of String listen. Step 1 TCP setup is role-conditional (TcpListener::bind+accept for Acceptor, TcpStream::connect for Initiator) but lands at the same TcpStream value. Step 4b FSM role-start is role-conditional: Acceptor dispatches InboundStart (R121d baseline); Initiator dispatches OutboundStart + LinkOpened in sequence (mirrors R60 session_fsm_real_tcp.rs::r60_fsm_drives_real_tcp_loopback pattern) so the FSM lands in SentInitSyn with send_init_syn already fired. tokio::net::TcpStream import added.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs publisher_task gate refactor — polls actions.trace_snapshot().record_established_at > 0 instead of send_open_ack > 0. record_established_at is the role-agnostic Established.onentry counter (fires on both acceptor send_open_ack → Established AND initiator OpenAckReceived → Established transitions). The R121e gate was acceptor-only by accident; R121f generalizes without role-aware branching. Doc comment + diagnostic log message updated.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs InboundReadDriver.poll_event — hex-dump diagnostic log at log::debug! level (`inbound frame len={N} bytes={hex array}`). Fires per inbound frame; visible only with RUST_LOG=debug. R121f authoring needed it to diagnose the wz-initiator → zenoh-pico peer-mode-listener failure mode (no inbound bytes ever reach wz — zenoh-pico closes the TCP connection silently); kept at debug so RUST_LOG=info production runs stay quiet.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor.rs new — R121f hard-gate test. Spawns two wz-ap-demo instances: first as --listen <addr> --key demo/** (acceptor + subscriber), then as --connect <addr> --publish demo/test --value hello-from-wz-initiator (initiator + publisher). Three-stage wait: (i) acceptor 'listening on' — bind succeeded; (ii) initiator 'connected to' — dial succeeded; (iii) acceptor 'SUBSCRIBER FIRED' — full handshake + publisher_task emit + pubsub resolver fired. Belt-and-suspenders assertions on keyexpr='demo/test' + wireexpr_id=0 substrings so a regression on either wire path or DECLARE-alias drift localises. Foreign-interop wz initiator → zenoh-pico peer-mode test deferred (carry) — file header documents the rationale (zenoh-pico 1.5.0 peer-listen does not reply to Client-whatami InitSyn; verified empirically during R121f authoring via debug hex dump showing zero inbound bytes in a 10s window).

scripts/run-ci.sh Layer E lane — header comment + body extended for R121f. Three integration tests now run in one cargo invocation: ap_demo_round_trip (R121c/d), wz_publisher_to_zsub (R121e), wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor (R121f). Single build/link step keeps the lane timing predictable; lane count remains at 8.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor — 1 passed. acceptor stderr: 'listening on 127.0.0.1:<port>' → 'accepted peer' → 'driving session FSM' → 'SUBSCRIBER FIRED key=demo/** wireexpr_id=0 suffix=Some(demo/test)'. initiator stderr: 'connected to <port>' → 'driving session FSM' → 'publisher_task observed Established; emitting 5 Pushes' → 'PUBLISHER EMITTED keyexpr=demo/test value=hello-from-wz-initiator idx=0'. Test runtime ~200ms warm cache.

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test ap_demo_round_trip — 1 passed. Wire-trace evidence (R121f initiator → zenoh-pico peer-listen failure mode — known carry): RUST_LOG=debug run shows 'connected to 127.0.0.1:<port>' (TCP dial succeeds), 'driving session FSM' (engine starts), then ActionTrace { link_driver_open: 1, send_init_syn: 1, send_open_syn: 0, send_close_frame_with_reason: 1, close_reason: Invalid, record_established_at: 0 }. NO 'inbound frame' debug line surfaced in 10s — zenoh-pico's peer-mode listen accepts the TCP socket but never sends back any session-handshake bytes. Foreign-interop on the initiator side needs a Zenoh router binary or zenoh-pico CLI patch; carried for R121f1 / R121g.

Manual whatami sanity: demo_session_init_params(&Role::Acceptor{..}).whatami == 0x02 (Peer, R121b/c/d/e); demo_session_init_params(&Role::Initiator{..}).whatami == 0x04 (Client). The wire-form whatami (init_cbyte's (api >> 1) & 0x03) maps 0x02 → 0x01 (wire Peer) and 0x04 → 0x02 (wire Client) per zenoh-pico transport.c:31-37.
carry_forward_bullets: R121f1 / R121g — wz initiator → zenoh-pico foreign-interop. Two paths to validate: (a) Zenoh router binary (zenohd) accepts wz dial-in; needs Rust Zenoh router build instead of zenoh-pico CLI — different vendor dependency. (b) zenoh-pico CLI patch adding `-m router` or fixing peer-listen acceptance for Client-whatami InitSyn. (a) is closer to production realism; (b) is closer to the existing CLI inventory. Surface a consumer measurement / production deployment shape before picking.

R121g — DECLARE(KeyExpr) outbound emit + DECLARE-aliased Push (R121e step 3 deferred). Adds SessionLinkActions::declare_keyexpr(id, suffix) emitting Frame[Declare(DeclKexpr(id, WireexprLocal(0, suffix)))] + extends build_push_literal to a build_push_aliased(id, optional_suffix, value) sibling. Surface a consumer when a measurement shows repeated-keyexpr Push traffic benefits from the id-mapping compression — until then YAGNI defers it.

R121h — SCXML codec_stream_envelope kind. R121d's TCP 2-byte LE length-prefix framing lives as inline doc-cited bytes inside InboundReadDriver / OutboundWriteDriver / writer_task in wz-ap-demo + the integration-test client peer. Formalising it as a wz-codecs SCXML codec emit + wz-codec call site replacement gives Layer 3 wire-byte-compare coverage on the framing layer itself (today only the inner Frame/Push body is L3-verified). Single round.

publishable_changes_bullets: crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs — new Role enum (Acceptor{listen:String} | Initiator{connect:String}) for argv-driven role select; --connect <addr> flag added (mutually exclusive with --listen); print_usage refreshed for the (--listen | --connect) shape; argv-validation matrix rejects both-set / neither-set / --publish-without-value etc. with exit code 2; demo_session_init_params signature shifts from () to (&Role), keeping whatami=Peer(0x02) on the Acceptor branch (R121b/c/d/e baseline preserved) and shifting to whatami=Client(0x04) on the Initiator branch (zenoh-pico's well-tested handshake shape is Client → Peer/Router; peer-peer over TCP unicast without prior multicast scouting is fragile in 1.5.0).

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs run_demo signature shifts: now takes Role instead of String listen. Step 1 TCP setup is role-conditional (TcpListener::bind+accept for Acceptor, TcpStream::connect for Initiator) but lands at the same TcpStream value. Step 4b FSM role-start is role-conditional: Acceptor dispatches InboundStart (R121d baseline); Initiator dispatches OutboundStart + LinkOpened in sequence (mirrors R60 session_fsm_real_tcp.rs::r60_fsm_drives_real_tcp_loopback pattern) so the FSM lands in SentInitSyn with send_init_syn already fired. tokio::net::TcpStream import added.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs publisher_task gate refactor — polls actions.trace_snapshot().record_established_at > 0 instead of send_open_ack > 0. record_established_at is the role-agnostic Established.onentry counter (fires on both acceptor send_open_ack → Established AND initiator OpenAckReceived → Established transitions). The R121e gate was acceptor-only by accident; R121f generalizes without role-aware branching. Doc comment + diagnostic log message updated.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs InboundReadDriver.poll_event — hex-dump diagnostic log at log::debug! level (`inbound frame len={N} bytes={hex array}`). Fires per inbound frame; visible only with RUST_LOG=debug. R121f authoring needed it to diagnose the wz-initiator → zenoh-pico peer-mode-listener failure mode (no inbound bytes ever reach wz — zenoh-pico closes the TCP connection silently); kept at debug so RUST_LOG=info production runs stay quiet.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor.rs new — R121f hard-gate test. Spawns two wz-ap-demo instances: first as --listen <addr> --key demo/** (acceptor + subscriber), then as --connect <addr> --publish demo/test --value hello-from-wz-initiator (initiator + publisher). Three-stage wait: (i) acceptor 'listening on' — bind succeeded; (ii) initiator 'connected to' — dial succeeded; (iii) acceptor 'SUBSCRIBER FIRED' — full handshake + publisher_task emit + pubsub resolver fired. Belt-and-suspenders assertions on keyexpr='demo/test' + wireexpr_id=0 substrings so a regression on either wire path or DECLARE-alias drift localises. Foreign-interop wz initiator → zenoh-pico peer-mode test deferred (carry) — file header documents the rationale (zenoh-pico 1.5.0 peer-listen does not reply to Client-whatami InitSyn; verified empirically during R121f authoring via debug hex dump showing zero inbound bytes in a 10s window).

Root-cause line-pinpoint via strace -e trace=sendto,recvfrom -xx on z_sub: pre-fix zenoh-pico emitted exactly 25 bytes (LE length prefix 0x17 0x00 + 23-byte payload) with FLAG_T_Z=1 in the InitAck header but no trailing ext bytes; the size negotiation in vendor/zenoh-pico/src/transport/unicast/transport.c:237-241 capped iam._patch to _Z_NO_PATCH (0) after _z_t_msg_make_init_ack had already set the Z bit (transport.c:165-193). The wz parser correctly read Z=1 and reported NeedMoreBytes.

changes_bullets: session_glue.rs: add build_push_aliased(mapping_id, optional_suffix, value) — pure-aliased or composite Push with non-zero WireexprLocal id; panics on id=0.

session_glue.rs: add build_declare_kexpr(mapping_id, suffix) — Declare envelope carrying a DeclKexpr that registers id to literal keyexpr; header explicitly sets _Z_DECL_KEXPR_FLAG_N (0x20), uses WireexprNonlocal arm to suppress the codec's spurious 0x40 derive bit (zenoh-pico's DeclKexpr has no flag at bit 6).
session_glue.rs: add encode_frame_with_declare(sn, declare, reliable) mirroring encode_frame_with_push for the Frame[Declare] wrap.

Codec hazard line-pinpoints via strace recvfrom -xx capture: (1) WireexprLocal arm's B5-ν derived 0x40 bit corrupted DeclKexpr.header (zenoh-pico's _z_decl_kexpr_encode at vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/declarations.c:52-58 does not define a flag at bit 6); (2) wz codec does not auto-derive _Z_DECL_KEXPR_FLAG_N from suffix presence — author must set it. Both pinned in build_declare_kexpr doc + tests.

wz-codecs B5-ν derive-bit propagation: file a follow-up note in the wz-codecs / sce-codegen workflow that the B5-ν derived 0x40 bit for WireexprLocal is only correct when the parent codec uses bit 6 as a flag carrier (Push, Request, Response). For parents that do not (DeclKexpr, possibly Interest), the codec author must either use WireexprNonlocal as a derive-suppression workaround OR the codegen needs a per-parent opt-out for the derive.

Layer E manual reproduction: wz-ap-demo --listen ADDR --publish demo/test --value hi-r121g --declare-id 7 paired with z_sub -e tcp/ADDR -m client -k 'demo/**' produces five Subscriber Received ('demo/test': 'hi-r121g') lines on z_sub's stdout; pre-fix produced Unknown message type received: 9 + Connection compromised due to message processing error: -119 (offset-shifted decode from the missing N flag).

Codec hazard line-pinpoints via strace recvfrom -xx capture: (1) WireexprLocal arm's B5-ν derived 0x40 bit corrupted DeclKexpr.header (zenoh-pico's _z_decl_kexpr_encode at vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/declarations.c:52-58 does not define a flag at bit 6); (2) wz codec does not auto-derive _Z_DECL_KEXPR_FLAG_N from suffix presence — author must set it. Both pinned in build_declare_kexpr doc + tests.

bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E); Layer C2 clippy initially flagged a calls-to-push-immediately-after-creation lint on the new wire-byte fixture, refactored to a vec! initializer.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: R121h: SCXML codec_stream_envelope kind — replace the four inline u16-LE length-prefix sites (TcpDriver send/poll + InboundReadDriver + OutboundWriteDriver + writer_task) with a generated codec call once sce-codegen supports a length-prefix-only stream framer.

R121i: 6 remaining DECLARE sub-variants (DeclSub / DeclToken / DeclQbl + Undecl* + DeclFinal). The DeclKexpr codec hazard surfaced here (B5-ν derive bit collision + author-set N flag) likely repeats for DeclSubscriber / DeclQueryable headers — pin via byte-compare unit tests on each.
R121j: Query/Reply chain (wz-side encoder + dispatch). AP MVP's last large area; 4-6 rounds estimated.
R123b-pre: deploy yaml flat to nested topology schema migration; resumes R123b once that lands.
R114: freshness gate negative test for scripts/run-ci.sh layer_b (sce-codegen mtime backward to auto-rebuild verification). Low-priority defensive carry.

wz-codecs B5-ν derive-bit propagation: file a follow-up note in the wz-codecs / sce-codegen workflow that the B5-ν derived 0x40 bit for WireexprLocal is only correct when the parent codec uses bit 6 as a flag carrier (Push, Request, Response). For parents that do not (DeclKexpr, possibly Interest), the codec author must either use WireexprNonlocal as a derive-suppression workaround OR the codegen needs a per-parent opt-out for the derive.

Round 121h.decision_summary: R121h replaces the hand-rolled u16 LE length-prefix framing in TcpDriver::send and TcpDriver::poll_event with calls into the generated wz_codecs::stream_envelope::StreamEnvelope encoder/decoder, lands a 5-vector byte-compare regression test against the zenoh-pico _z_link_send_t_msg reference (including the 256-byte multi-byte-length critical case), and removes the last hand-rolled wire-framing site from the AP tokio runtime.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs imports: add `sce_forge_runtime::codec::SceCursor` and `wz_codecs::stream_envelope::StreamEnvelope`.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs TcpDriver doc comment rewritten: removes the R121d-era `framing remains hand-rolled` deferral note and replaces it with the R121h SSOT statement plus a rationale paragraph for the 2-byte prefix sniff at the start of poll_event (until SCE exposes a codec MIN_FRAME_BYTES associated const, the wire-shape requires this read before the structural decode can run).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs TcpDriver::send (lines 160-181): replaces the `len.to_le_bytes()` + two `write_all` pair with a single StreamEnvelope construction + encode + write_all. Behaviour is byte-identical to the prior path (verified by R121h byte-compare test); the codec catalog now owns the wire-shape.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs TcpDriver::poll_event (lines 191-224): two-step read pattern. First reads the 2-byte prefix raw (sized by the codec's min_frame_bytes), then sizes a frame buffer to `2 + payload_len` from the LE-decoded prefix, reads the payload tail into `frame[2..]`, and decodes the full frame through `StreamEnvelope::decode` for SSOT byte interpretation. The codec validates the structural decode; the manual prefix sniff is a bounded duplication of `min_frame_bytes=2` documented in the call-site comment.

crates/wz-codecs/tests/smoke.rs: new `stream_envelope_wire_bytes_match_zenoh_pico_reference` test exercising 5 vectors (empty payload, 3-byte payload, 255-byte boundary, 256-byte boundary, 300-byte payload). The 256-byte case locks the multi-byte length regression that the pre-R121h-pre2 SCE simple-path decode silently truncated to `raw[0] = 0x00`; the post-let-binding decode now reconstructs the full u16 through the named local and the test catches any future regression. Three small expected-wire helpers `expected_wire_255`/`256`/`300` build the reference byte sequences inline.
verification_bullets: cargo build --workspace: clean rebuild including wz-runtime-tokio with the codec wire-up; no rustc warnings introduced.

cargo test -p wz-codecs --test smoke stream_envelope: the new byte-compare test passes with all 5 vectors green; the 256-byte case explicitly verifies that StreamEnvelope::decode reconstructs payload_len from both prefix bytes (not the low byte alone), defending against any future SCE simple-path regression.

cargo test --workspace: all suites green. wz-runtime-tokio lib tests + Layer E 5 integration tests (ap_demo_round_trip / wz_publisher_to_zsub / wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor / wz_initiator_to_zsub / wz_publisher_aliased_to_zsub) all pass — confirms the codec-routed send/poll_event remains wire-compatible with both wz↔wz and wz↔zenoh-pico 1.5.0 interop scenarios.

bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). Layer E foreign interop validated end-to-end including the AP demo z_put → wz subscriber path that crosses both encode (wz publisher) and decode (wz subscriber) on the codec.
validate_workspace baseline: docs=12/12, T1 orphan=0 (no new), round-trip mandatory 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync; atomic ledger entries advance 161 -> 162 with this entry, sections=214 unchanged.
carry_forward_bullets: R121i: 6 DECLARE sub-variants (DeclSub / DeclToken / DeclQbl + Undecl x3 + DeclFinal). Each variant's parent codec opts in/out of M-flag derive at the import site per the R121h-pre B5-nu invert; each new outbound gets a per-variant byte-compare unit test mirroring the stream_envelope_wire_bytes test pattern. Estimate 2-3 rounds.
R121j: Query/Reply chain (wz-side encoder + dispatch). AP MVP's last large area. Estimate 4-6 rounds.
R123b-pre: deploy yaml flat to nested topology schema migration; resumes R123b once that lands.
R114: freshness gate negative test for scripts/run-ci.sh layer_b (sce-codegen mtime backward auto-rebuild verification). Low-priority defensive carry.

SCE feature proposal (optional): expose codec model's min_frame_bytes (and possibly max_frame_bytes) as a generated associated const on each codec struct, e.g. `pub const MIN_FRAME_BYTES: usize = 2;`. Removes the last hard-coded wire-shape number from runtime consumers like wz-runtime-tokio TcpDriver::poll_event's 2-byte prefix sniff. Per user directive (this session) any SCE non-textbook surface or improvement proposal is reported back rather than worked around in wz; this carry is the report. Not a blocker for R121i/j — just a future polish item if SCE accepts the proposal.

Round 121h-perf-bump-1-study.decision_summary: R121h-perf-bump-1 reduced to a study round: empirical impact survey shows the prior session's 3-phase carry is infeasible because partial SCXML migration breaks the build at every intermediate state, so the carry is revised from 3-phase to 2-phase (atomic bump+migrate, then TcpDriver hot path).
changes_bullets: No wz src or vendor changes this round. Study-only entry; the audit trail captures the revised migration plan so the next session can execute against a verified scope rather than the prior session's misdiagnosed 3-phase split.

Empirical scope: 12 wz SCXML files use the legacy `<sce:requires-parent-flags>` element and/or `parent.X` predicate (prior session estimate was 11). The 12: init_body, open_body, hello, join, push, request, interest_body, timestamp, decl_kexpr, decl_subscriber, decl_queryable, decl_token, wireexpr, wireexpr_local, wireexpr_nonlocal (correction: actually 15 files surface in grep but decl_kexpr / decl_token / wireexpr / wireexpr_local / wireexpr_nonlocal grouping needs per-file checks during the migration round; the 12 = files with non-zero RPF blocks OR non-comment parent.X predicates).

Partial migration is infeasible. SCE Phase D2+D3+D4 (commit 19b0fcf0) deletes the legacy RPF mechanism + `<sce:requires-parent-flags>` XSD element entirely. The new grammar (`<sce:flag-inputs>` / `<sce:flag-bind>` from Phase A 36c0750e + Phase B 63daab97 codegen wire-up) is rejected by the current vendor pin 54d513af. There is no overlap window where both grammars parse; the bump and the 12-file migration must land in one commit.

Migration pattern reference (SCE Phase C bf41498c migrated 19 conformance fixtures with this exact pattern). Old form on a leaf consumer: `<sce:requires-parent-flags carrier="header"><sce:flag name="S" bit="6"/></sce:requires-parent-flags>` + `sce:present-if="parent.S"` on gated fields. New form: `<sce:flag-inputs><sce:flag-input name="S" width="1"/></sce:flag-inputs>` + `sce:present-if="S"` (no `parent.` prefix; SCE Phase B snake_cases the identifier for the typed encode/decode parameter so uppercase leaf flag names retain their literal form). Parent importers add `<sce:flag-bind input="S" source="S"/>` as a child of the `<sce:import>` element to wire each declared input to a source flag (dotted carrier.flag form or chain-forwarder bare-name form).

init_body.scxml-shaped example (the smallest migration in the 12): drop the RPF block declaring `S`(bit=6) and `A`(bit=5) on carrier=header; add `<sce:flag-inputs>` with `<sce:flag-input name="S" width="1"/>` and `<sce:flag-input name="A" width="1"/>`; rewrite the 4 `parent.S` predicates to `S` and the 2 `parent.A` predicates to `A`. init_body is NOT imported by any wz SCXML (wz constructs the transport envelope in Rust runtime), so no parent flag-bind is needed.

Variant-dispatcher cases (wireexpr.scxml + wireexpr_local.scxml + wireexpr_nonlocal.scxml): Phase D1 b8f86b46 added variant arm flag-bind threading codegen — the dispatcher's `<sce:import>` of each arm body now needs the same `<sce:flag-bind>` wire-up as a regular consumer. wireexpr.scxml has the variant-dispatch + RPF combination; the migration needs care to preserve the B5-ν chain-forwarding (parent.N gates the suffix in each arm body, threaded through the dispatcher's flag-bind to the arm's flag-inputs).

Encoder API impact after vendor bump (separate from SCXML grammar). SCE B1-α series (cb1b40b7 / 4eafcc7b / e01aac02 / 35998fd7 / 100a2ca0 / de5d5e9a) introduced `SceSink` trait + `SliceSink<&mut [u8]>` + `VecSink<&mut Vec<u8>>` + an `encode_to_vec()` feature-gated wrapper. The `*.encode()` zero-arg method that wz callers rely on is replaced. Mechanical drop-in: rename to `*.encode_to_vec()` (alloc feature on by default for std builds). Hot-path sink-based migration (TcpDriver) is a separate concern. ~10+ wz call-sites need the rename.
verification_bullets: Impact count gathered via `grep -l 'requires-parent-flags|parent\.[A-Za-z_]' sources/codecs/*.scxml` (15 files surfaced) and a follow-up per-file count of non-zero RPF blocks + non-comment `parent.X` predicates (12 files actually need migration; the 3 comment-only ones are not at risk).

SCE Phase C migration pattern verified by reading the diff at SCE commit bf41498c (tests/forge/resources/codec_init_syn_body.scxml + codec_zenoh_decl_subscriber.scxml). Both files showed the same RPF → flag-inputs / parent.X → X / import flag-bind addition pattern.

Coexistence-window check: SCE Phase A (36c0750e) commit body states `legacy RPF + parent.X predicate paths coexist during Phase A pending later atomic`. SCE Phase D2+D3+D4 (19b0fcf0) commit body states `Removes RequiresParentFlags struct + CodecModel field + PresentIfScope::Parent variant + ... XSD drops <sce:requires-parent-flags> element`. The current SCE main HEAD is post-D2 (de5d5e9a), so the coexistence window has closed. No vendor pin between A and D2 is reachable because SCE main is now past D2; the only safe target is HEAD (de5d5e9a).

No wz src changes → no test surface to verify. The next round's verification surface is `bash scripts/run-ci.sh` 8 lanes (especially Layer B verify-codegen.sh and Layer E ap_demo_round_trip 5-test bundle).
carry_forward_bullets: Revised R121h-perf-bump scope (replaces the 3-phase carry from R121h-perf-bump-attempt). Round 121h-perf-bump-2 (atomic): vendor pin 54d513af -> de5d5e9a + all 12 SCXML migrate (init_body + open_body + hello + join + push + request + interest_body + timestamp + decl_subscriber + decl_queryable + decl_token + wireexpr trio) + `*.encode()` -> `*.encode_to_vec()` mass rename + 8-lane CI pass + 14 byte-compare invariants preserved. Round 121h-perf-bump-3 (refinement): TcpDriver / hot path sink-based migration to consume `SliceSink<&mut [u8]>` directly, retire the `.to_vec()` + extend pattern flagged at R121g.

Migration order recommendation for R121h-perf-bump-2: start with the simplest leaves (init_body, open_body, timestamp — single-flag RPFs with no variant-dispatch), validate each via `cargo check` at the new vendor pin, then handle the variant-dispatcher cluster (wireexpr.scxml + wireexpr_local + wireexpr_nonlocal) which needs flag-bind threading. Decl_* family last (they import wireexpr and inherit the wire-up from the dispatcher fix).

Inventory hazard for R121h-perf-bump-2: per-leaf input narrowing per SCE Phase C convention (`declared inputs narrowed to actually-used flags`). E.g. decl_subscriber's RPF declared N/M/Z but only N is actually consumed by the leaf body; M became codegen-derived (B5-ν variant dispatch), Z is unused. Each wz SCXML migration needs the same narrowing audit — trust grep-confirmed predicate usage as the authoritative input list, not the RPF block's flag count.

Round 121h-perf-bump-2.decision_summary: SCE vendor pin 54d513af to 27accb35 atomic bump + Axis-1 inversion grammar migration on 11 SCXML files + Axis-3 session-role declaration + alloc feature wiring + .encode() to .encode_to_vec() rename across wz codebase, gated by SCE-side fix 07afcc45 for the embed-dispatcher arg-order bug surfaced in this round.
changes_bullets: vendor/sce 54d513af to 27accb35 (33 commits incl Axis-1 Phase A-D2 RPF deletion + Axis-3 Phase A-D listener role + B1-alpha sink-based encode + 07afcc45 embed-dispatcher arg-order fix + 9a8a869e cross-backend substring guard + 27accb35 callee-side validator)
7 RPF leaves migrated to Axis-1 grammar (hello, init_body, join, open_body, wireexpr, wireexpr_local, wireexpr_nonlocal): requires-parent-flags to flag-inputs + parent.X to X predicate
8 wireexpr importers extended with flag-bind input=N source=header.N child on the wireexpr import (push, request, response, decl_subscriber, decl_queryable, decl_token, decl_kexpr, interest_body)

Round 121h-perf-bump-3.decision_summary: Five hot-path encode_frame_with_* helpers in session_glue.rs collapsed from 3 alloc/emit to 1 alloc/emit via a sink-based envelope helper leveraging the R121h-perf-bump-2 B1-alpha sink encode.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: new private encode_frame_envelope (FnOnce closure-receiving) helper pre-reserves wire Vec with capacity = 1 (parent_flags|T_MID_FRAME byte) + 10 (worst-case VLE(sn) for u64) + inner codec MAX_ENCODED_BYTES, wraps the Vec in a VecSink, writes VLE(sn) bit-identically to Frame.encode's sn block, delegates payload encode to the closure

5 hot-path helpers (encode_frame_with_push, _declare, _request, _response, _response_final) rewritten as 3-line delegations: parent_flags compute + encode_frame_envelope(sn, parent_flags, Inner.MAX_ENCODED_BYTES, |sink| inner.encode(sink)). Bodies shrunk from 11 lines to 3 each

imports: +SceSink + VecSink to sce_forge_runtime::codec use list (SceSink trait required in scope so VecSink's write_u8 method-call resolves); -wz_codecs::frame::Frame removed (no longer constructed in this file)

Alloc count per outbound hot-path emit: 3 to 1. Pre-reserved capacity for a typical Push emit: 1219 bytes (1 header + 10 VLE + 1208 Push.MAX_ENCODED_BYTES) replaces the prior Frame.MAX_ENCODED_BYTES = 65546 (64 KB) over-allocation; compound savings on AP MVP pub/sub where one Push emit per published sample

Flake pattern matches the prior feedback_ci_yaml_local_verify learning (TCP localhost concurrency env-flake)

Byte-identical guarantee: VLE(sn) is the wire format itself (zenoh-pico VLE base-128, not consumer-tunable) and the inner codec sink-based encode writes the same bytes regardless of whether the sink wraps a new empty Vec (prior code) or the wire Vec at offset 1+VLE(sn) (new code). Layer 3 wire-byte-compare tests pin this invariant
carry_forward_bullets: Four secondary encoder sites in session_glue.rs still use the inner-Vec + extend_from_slice pattern: line 1543 (InitBody encode with S/A unpacking), 1596 (OpenBody encode with A unpacking), 1620 (DeclFinal batch entry), 1645 (KeepAlive). Not on the AP MVP pub/sub hot loop (handshake + control only, one-off per session); future R121h-perf-bump-4 can apply the same envelope-style shape. Lower priority than R121j-5 inbound parser on the weighted axis

SliceSink-based zero-alloc hot path (caller-owned reusable buffer) is the next refinement axis. Requires plumbing a per-link reusable buffer through LinkDriver -> TxFrame -> tcp_write; the current TcpDriver tx path consumes Vec<u8> via TxFrame.bytes. Defer to a runtime API design round -- wire bytes computation is now optimal; remaining alloc cost is LinkDriver enqueue layer

Round 121h-perf-bump-4.decision_summary: Three secondary encoders (encode_init / encode_open / encode_close) in session_glue.rs migrated from inner-Vec + extend_from_slice to sink-based encode, saving one allocation per call. Layer-3 byte-compare regression-zero.
changes_bullets: encode_init at crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs:1539-1547: pre-reserve Vec capacity = 1 (parent_flags | T_MID_INIT) + InitBody::MAX_ENCODED_BYTES + ext_bytes.len() instead of an ad-hoc body.zid.len() + cookie.len() + 12 estimate; wrap the wire Vec in VecSink after pushing the header byte and call body.encode(&mut sink, s, a) directly, eliminating the intermediate body.encode_to_vec(s, a) allocation. ext_bytes still extended at the end (encode_ext_chain is a separate concern not touched this round)

encode_open at session_glue.rs:1593-1600: same sink-based shape applied. Pre-reserve = 1 + OpenBody::MAX_ENCODED_BYTES + ext_bytes.len(); body.encode(&mut sink, a) replaces body.encode_to_vec(a) + extend_from_slice. Same ext_bytes extension at end

encode_close at session_glue.rs:1641-1648: pre-reserve = 1 + Close::MAX_ENCODED_BYTES (2 byte total worst-case); Close { reason }.encode(&mut sink) replaces the encode_to_vec + extend_from_slice pair. Most dramatic per-call ratio (2-byte wire was 2 allocs; now 1)

encode_ext_chain at session_glue.rs:1614-1633 (the 4th secondary site flagged in the R121h-perf-bump-3 carry) intentionally NOT touched this round -- the per-entry Z-bit byte-patching at index 0 of entry.encode_to_vec() output requires a deeper refactor (either expose a sink with positional rewind, or restructure the ext encode to emit the Z bit conditionally via a flag arg). Defer to a future R121h-perf-bump-5 carry
