Refactor options: (a) extend ExtEntry::encode to accept a chain_continuation: bool arg so the Z bit is emitted natively in the right position; (b) add a sink method position() that lets callers rewind by 1 byte after entry.encode() to patch the Z bit. Option (a) is cleaner if SCE codegen can express it; option (b) is purely consumer-side. Defer pending SCE Phase X decision

DeclFinal batch entry encode + KeepAlive encode at session_glue.rs were originally flagged in the closure but on closer reading those are NOT direct encoder sites -- the closure mistakenly cited those line numbers. Round 121h-perf-bump-attempt.decision_summary: R121h-perf-bump-attempt investigated SCE drift +15 commits (vendor pin 54d513af -> SCE main de5d5e9a) and discovered the SCE B1-α series (sink-based encode + encode_to_vec wrapper) already lands the exact zero-alloc fix our R121i-c carry parked as an SCE upstream proposal; deferred the actual vendor bump to a multi-round R121h-perf-bump phase because the Axis-1 inversion that ships in the same drift window strengthens the present-if predicate grammar and 11 wz codec SCXMLs need migration before the bump can land cleanly.
changes_bullets: vendor/sce submodule fetched SCE main; observed +15 commits since wz vendor pin 54d513af: 6 B1-α (sink-based encode chain) + 8 Axis-1 / Axis-2 / Axis-3 / Axis-5 inversion + refactor + fmt commits. The B1-α series alone is exactly the perf-debt repayment proposed in R121i-c carry; no SCE upstream PR needed.

Read sce-forge-runtime/rust/src/codec.rs:180-330 in the bumped tree. SCE B1-α API surface confirmed: SceSink trait (object-safe, write_bytes / position / per-width helpers as default methods); SliceSink<'a> over &mut [u8] (raises BufferOverflow at capacity, no_std + no_alloc compatible — the natural MCU sink); VecSink<'a> over &mut Vec<u8> (alloc-feature-gated, infallible grow — the std hot path); encode_to_vec() preserved as a feature-gated wrapper for source-compat. Wire format byte-identical — wz's 14 byte-compare invariants would be preserved across the bump.

Attempted cargo check at the bumped pin -> wz-codecs build.rs panicked: 'codec init_body: field sn_res has sce:present-if="parent.…" but parent is not declared earlier in this codec — present-if predicates must reference a flags-bearing carrier that the streaming decoder has already consumed; reorder the fields, or correct the predicate'. The Axis-1 inversion chain (36c0750e foundation + 63daab97 codegen wire-up + bf41498c 19-fixture migrate + 19b0fcf0 delete legacy RPF) tightened this grammar.

Round 121h-pre.decision_summary: R121h-pre bumps the SCE vendor pin from 7bada841 to e10619d3 to land the B5-ν variant-dispatch ownership invert (leaf-owned `tag="parent.X"` → parent-owned `<sce:variant-dispatch flag="X.Y"/>` at `<sce:import>`), updates wireexpr.scxml + 8 importer codecs accordingly, and retires the R121g `WireexprNonlocal` derive-suppression workaround in `build_declare_kexpr`.
changes_bullets: vendor/sce submodule pin 7bada841 → e10619d3 (SCE: feat: Invert B5-ν variant dispatch ownership from leaf to parent).
sources/codecs/wireexpr.scxml: drop `tag="parent.M"` from `<sce:variant>` (β leaf shape); RPF block retires the M flag (B5-ν purpose ended) and keeps N for B5-γ chain forwarding to the arm bodies.

sources/codecs/{push,request,response,decl_subscriber,decl_queryable,decl_token}.scxml (6 codecs): add `<sce:variant-dispatch flag="header.M"/>` as a child of the wireexpr `<sce:import>` to opt into parent-derived M-flag emission. Each rationale cites the corresponding zenoh-pico `_Z_FLAG_*_M = 0x40` definition.

sources/codecs/{decl_kexpr,interest_body}.scxml: deliberately omit `<sce:variant-dispatch>` — DeclKexpr.header has no bit-6 flag (declarations.h:25-26), interest_body's bit 6 = `_Z_FLAG_N_INTEREST_FUTURE` semantic-mismatch with wireexpr.M. Doc comment cites both spec sources.

wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs::build_declare_kexpr: restore inner keyexpr arm from `WireexprNonlocal` → `WireexprLocal` (semantically correct under the invert). Drop the `WireexprNonlocal` import. Comment retired as R121h-pre history note.

wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests: update build_declare_kexpr_wraps_decl_kexpr_with_literal_suffix assertion arm to `WireexprLocal`; byte-compare test (build_declare_kexpr_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes) wire bytes unchanged.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace: all suites green; 38 wz-runtime-tokio lib tests pass including the byte-compare gate that anchors `build_declare_kexpr` output to zenoh-pico's `_z_decl_kexpr_encode` reference (wire bytes identical pre- and post-invert).

bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E); Layer E foreign-interop tests (5 total — ap_demo_round_trip / wz_publisher_to_zsub / wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor / wz_initiator_to_zsub / wz_publisher_aliased_to_zsub) all green confirming the invert + workaround removal preserves wire compatibility against zenoh-pico 1.5.0.

Wire-byte regression-resistance: `build_declare_kexpr_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes` validates the bytes [0x1E, 0x20, 0x07, 0x00, 0x09, "demo/test"] byte-for-byte against zenoh-pico's reference at vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/declarations.c:52-63. The invert changes derive plumbing but not the emitted bytes — DeclKexpr's parent declines `<sce:variant-dispatch>` so no parent derive is emitted at bit 6.

SCE-side validator surface: parser.rs comment confirms `tag="parent.X"` form is removed and dispatch on parent flag is now declared at import; cross-doc validator (Q-D-3a per generator.rs comment) requires either a `<sce:variant-dispatch>` or a default arm on every variant import — wz's wireexpr_local arm carries `default="true"` so the no-dispatch parents (decl_kexpr / interest_body) construct cleanly.
carry_forward_bullets: R121i: 6 remaining DECLARE sub-variants (DeclSub / DeclToken / DeclQbl + Undecl* + DeclFinal). With B5-ν ownership inverted, each variant's parent codec can independently opt in/out of M-flag derive at the import site — no codec-level workaround needed. Pin each new outbound with a byte-compare unit test mirroring `build_declare_kexpr_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes`.

Round 121h-pre2.decision_summary: R121h-pre2 bumps SCE vendor pin e10619d3 to 54d513af to land the codec simple-path length-ref let-binding refactor (multi-byte plain-id length-field support), introduces sources/codecs/stream_envelope.scxml for the Zenoh streamed-link u16 LE length-prefix envelope, and registers it in wz-codecs; TcpDriver wire-up is deferred to R121h proper.
changes_bullets: vendor/sce submodule pin e10619d3 to 54d513af (6 commits: 7d910c03 fmt; 948f73d0 refactor: Lift codec simple-path fields into let-bindings landed the textbook fix that lets BitSize::LengthRef reference the just-bound sibling local instead of indexing raw[len_off] for the length value; 03fbd7a0 test: Lock multi-byte length-ref via 256-byte runtime round-trip; dadca3f3 feat: Validate plain-id length-field sibling bit-size shape adds parse-time validation that length-field siblings are Fixed bits in {8,16,24,32} or VLE; 45d0f188 fmt; f7939e3f fix: Resolve cpp len name collision; ff616921 chore: pre-push fmt hook; 54d513af fix: Rename cpp struct local from value to _decoded).

sources/codecs/stream_envelope.scxml: new codec_kind authoring the streamed-link wire envelope (uint16 LE payload_len at byte 0 + bytes length-ref payload at byte 2, max-size=65535 matching the u16 wire ceiling). Mirrors zenoh-pico _z_link_send_t_msg (vendor/zenoh-pico/src/transport/common/tx.c) + _z_link_recv_t_msg_cap_flow_stream (rx.c) with _Z_MSG_LEN_ENC_SIZE=2 (vendor/zenoh-pico/include/zenoh-pico/protocol/definitions/core.h:32).
crates/wz-codecs/build.rs::CODECS: stream_envelope registered in leaf-codecs section (no imports; single-field plus length-ref pair).
crates/wz-codecs/src/lib.rs: pub mod stream_envelope block added next to ext_zbuf, exposing the generated StreamEnvelope struct.

TcpDriver send/poll_event wire-up (crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs lines 160-181 + 191-224) deferred to R121h proper; this round only lands the codec catalog registration so the wire-up commit's diff stays focused on the inline-to-generated-call swap.
verification_bullets: cargo build --workspace: clean rebuild including vendor/sce/sce-build + sce-forge-runtime; no rustc warnings introduced.

cargo test --workspace: all suites green (38+ wz-runtime-tokio lib tests, 5 Layer E integration tests, wz-codecs smoke tests). Confirms the new vendor pin doesn't regress any existing codec; the let-binding refactor preserves wire bytes for codecs that used VLE length-field (ext_zbuf) or flags-accessor length-field (join.zid), since their generated decode paths were already let-binding-based.

bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). Layer B byte-compare against verify-codegen.sh Layer 2 oracle preserved; Layer E foreign interop with zenoh-pico 1.5.0 still green.

Generated crates/target/debug/build/wz-codecs-*/out/stream_envelope.rs decode body uses the new let-binding pattern: let payload_len = raw[0] as u16 | ((raw[1] as u16) << 8); let payload = raw[2..2 + payload_len as usize].to_vec(); the multi-byte payload_len reconstructs through the named local rather than truncating to raw[0]. Verified by direct read of the generated file post-rebuild.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_declare_subscriber(subscriber_id, keyexpr_mapping_id, keyexpr_suffix) builder added next to build_declare_kexpr. Inner DeclSubscriber.header carries author-supplied MID(0x02) + optional N(0x20) suffix gate; the M bit (0x40) is codegen-derived from the wireexpr import's variant-dispatch flag=header.M declaration in decl_subscriber.scxml under the R121h-pre B5-nu ownership invert. Wireexpr arm fixed to Local (default arm per wireexpr.scxml default=true); literal-vs-alias-vs-composite is driven by the (mapping_id, suffix) pair following the same convention as build_push_aliased and build_declare_kexpr.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs SessionLinkActions::send_declare_subscriber action added alongside send_declare_keyexpr. Hard-coded to the reliable channel for the same SN-window ordering reason as DECLARE-kexpr: the peer's subscriber table must be populated before any matching Push arrives, otherwise the peer drops it as unknown subscription.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests: two new unit tests. build_declare_subscriber_wraps_decl_subscriber_in_declare_envelope exercises the 3 semantic cases (pure alias, composite alias+suffix, literal id=0+suffix) at the struct-shape level. build_declare_subscriber_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes is the wire-byte regression gate — 3-vector byte-for-byte compare against zenoh-pico declarations.c:65-84 reference encoder. The literal case validates the wireexpr local-sentinel id=0 + VLE(len) + suffix bytes shape; the alias case validates the M-bit codegen derivation (header = 0x42 = MID|M); the composite case validates the N|M co-set header (0x62) + wireexpr id-prefix + suffix-tail emit.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_declare_subscriber: both new tests pass. The byte-compare test confirms the 3 emit shapes match zenoh-pico's _z_decl_subscriber_encode + _z_decl_commons_encode byte-for-byte; any future codegen drift on either header derivation, wireexpr arm selection, or the parent.M dispatch path surfaces immediately.

cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: all 40 lib tests green (38 prior + 2 new R121i-a), including the existing build_declare_kexpr_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes byte-compare gate from R121g (no regression on the established declare-kexpr path; the new declare-subscriber tests are additive).

bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). Layer C2 (clippy --deny warnings) caught a doc-lazy-continuation lint in the new test doc comment and was fixed before commit; the lint-clean state preserves the workspace's warnings=deny policy.
validate_workspace baseline: docs=12/12, T1 orphan=0 (no new), round-trip mandatory 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync; atomic ledger entries advance 162 -> 163, sections=214 unchanged.
carry_forward_bullets: R121i-b: next two DECLARE sub-variants (DeclQueryable, DeclToken). Identical _z_decl_commons shape with different MIDs (0x04 for Queryable, 0x06 for Token); the build helper is a near-copy of build_declare_subscriber with the MID literal swapped. Per-variant byte-compare unit tests mirror the R121i-a pattern.

R121i-c: the 3 Undecl_* variants (UndeclSubscriber, UndeclQueryable, UndeclToken) - shape [header, VLE(id), optional Z ext keyexpr] per zenoh-pico _z_undecl_encode. Simpler than the Decl_* variants (no wireexpr embed in the base shape). Plus UndeclKexpr (the simplest: just header + VLE(id)) and DeclFinal (single-byte marker). Estimate 1-2 rounds.
R121j: Query/Reply chain (wz-side encoder + dispatch). AP MVP's last large area. Estimate 4-6 rounds.
R123b-pre: deploy yaml flat to nested topology schema migration; resumes R123b once that lands.
R114: freshness gate negative test for scripts/run-ci.sh layer_b (sce-codegen mtime backward auto-rebuild verification). Low-priority defensive carry.
Optional SCE feature proposal carry from R121h: expose codec MIN_FRAME_BYTES as a generated associated const so runtime consumers can drop hard-coded prefix sizes.

Round 121i-b.decision_summary: R121i-b lands the second and third of the 7 remaining DECLARE sub-variants — Declare(DeclQueryable) and Declare(DeclToken) outbound — via build_declare_queryable / build_declare_token + matching SessionLinkActions send actions, each with a 3-vector wire-byte regression test (alias/composite/literal) anchored to zenoh-pico _z_decl_queryable_encode and _z_decl_token_encode references.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs imports: add wz_codecs::decl_queryable::DeclQueryable and wz_codecs::decl_token::DeclToken alongside the existing DeclSubscriber import.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_declare_queryable(queryable_id, keyexpr_mapping_id, keyexpr_suffix) builder added next to build_declare_subscriber. AP MVP scope: emits the has_info_ext=false shape (no _Z_MSG_EXT_ENC_ZINT|0x01 ExtQueryableInfo tail), matching the default _z_queryable_infos_t shipped by z_query_consolidation_default. A separate build_declare_queryable_with_info helper for complete=true / non-zero distance lands in R121j+. Inner DeclQueryable.header carries author-supplied MID(0x04) + optional N(0x20); the M bit (0x40) is codegen-derived from the wireexpr import's variant-dispatch flag=header.M declaration in decl_queryable.scxml.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_declare_token(token_id, keyexpr_mapping_id, keyexpr_suffix) builder added. DeclToken has no extension surface at all in zenoh-pico (_z_decl_token_encode is a pure _z_decl_commons_encode(has_ext=false) wrapper at declarations.c:123-126), so the builder's emit shape is byte-stable across every (id, mapping, suffix) input. Inner DeclToken.header carries MID(0x06) + optional N + codegen-derived M.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs SessionLinkActions::send_declare_queryable + SessionLinkActions::send_declare_token actions added alongside send_declare_subscriber. Both hard-coded to the reliable channel for the same SN-window ordering reason as the rest of the DECLARE outbound paths: the peer's queryable/token table must be populated before any matching Request(Query) or liveliness fan-out arrives.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests: four new unit tests. {build_declare_queryable, build_declare_token}_wraps_*_in_declare_envelope exercise the 3 semantic cases (pure alias, composite alias+suffix, literal id=0+suffix) at struct-shape level. {build_declare_queryable, build_declare_token}_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes are the wire-byte regression gates — 3-vector byte-for-byte compare against zenoh-pico declarations.c:105-126 reference encoders, pinning the MID-0x04 and MID-0x06 alias/composite/literal trios.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib -- build_declare_queryable build_declare_token: all 4 new tests pass. The byte-compare gates confirm the 6 emit shapes (2 builders x 3 vectors) match zenoh-pico's _z_decl_queryable_encode + _z_decl_token_encode byte-for-byte; any future codegen drift on header derivation, wireexpr arm selection, or the parent.M dispatch path on either DeclQueryable or DeclToken surfaces immediately.
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: all 44 lib tests green (40 prior + 4 new R121i-b); no regression on the existing R121g build_declare_kexpr + R121i-a build_declare_subscriber byte-compare gates.
bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). No new clippy warnings under Layer C2 (--deny warnings); warnings=deny workspace policy preserved.
validate_workspace baseline: docs=12/12, T1 orphan=0 (no new), round-trip mandatory 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync; atomic ledger entries advance 163 -> 164, sections=214 unchanged.

SCE drift verification: SCE upstream HEAD = 943786d6 (Seat AuthClassifier under sce/include/mesh/third_party — internal component refactor) is +1 commit past vendor pin 54d513af, but cargo check -p wz-codecs -p wz-runtime-tokio builds cleanly without bumping the pin. AuthClassifier is not referenced by any codec generation surface; the drift is wz-codegen-neutral and stays on the SCE side until a wz-actionable reason emerges.
carry_forward_bullets: R121i-c next: Undecl_* 4 variants + DeclFinal. UndeclKexpr (MID 0x01) is the simplest — header + VLE(id) per declarations.c:86-89, no wireexpr field. UndeclSubscriber (0x03) / UndeclQueryable (0x05) / UndeclToken (0x07) follow the _z_undecl_encode shape at declarations.c:90-101: [header, VLE(id), optional _Z_FLAG_Z_Z ext keyexpr]. DeclFinal (0x1A) is a single-byte marker (_z_decl_final_encode at declarations.c:131-135). Each variant gets a build_* helper + send_* action + byte-compare test (Undecl_* needs both Z-ext-present and Z-ext-absent vectors since the ext is optional).

R121h-perf zero-alloc encode debt unchanged: codec-generated StreamEnvelope::encode and the *.encode() bodies all do Vec::with_capacity(65537) (or 267 for declarations) per call, and TcpDriver::send adds a frame.bytes.to_vec()+extend pair on top. AP functional OK but production-perf debt. Repayment path: SCE upstream zero-alloc encode_into(&mut Vec<u8>) variant feature proposal (textbook per SCE-is-priority directive) OR wz fast-path that bypasses codec for the fits-in-u16 hot path (codec SSOT still locked by byte-compare tests).

DECLARE outbound Nonlocal arm silent gap: build_declare_{subscriber,queryable,token} are all fixed to WireexprLocal. Interest/Reply paths in R121j may need Nonlocal coverage when the referenced mapping is peer-declared rather than local-declared; audit + extend builders with a Nonlocal arm parameter at that point.

Round 121i-c.decision_summary: R121i-c closes the DECLARE sub-variant cluster — Undecl_* four variants (UndeclKexpr / UndeclSubscriber / UndeclQueryable / UndeclToken) plus DeclFinal terminator — via build_undeclare_* + build_declare_final builders, matching SessionLinkActions send actions, and 5 wire-byte byte-compare tests anchored to zenoh-pico declarations.c:86-103 + :131-135 references.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs imports: add wz_codecs::decl_final::DeclFinal, wz_codecs::undecl_kexpr::UndeclKexpr, wz_codecs::undecl_subscriber::UndeclSubscriber, wz_codecs::undecl_queryable::UndeclQueryable, wz_codecs::undecl_token::UndeclToken alongside the existing Decl_* imports.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_undeclare_kexpr(mapping_id) builder added: emits [header(_Z_UNDECL_KEXPR_MID=0x01), VLE(mapping_id)] mirroring zenoh-pico _z_undecl_kexpr_encode at declarations.c:86-89. No wireexpr body, no Z ext surface (unlike the other three Undecl_* variants); the retraction is purely id-based because the peer already has the (id -> keyexpr) entry from a prior DeclKexpr.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_undeclare_subscriber / build_undeclare_queryable / build_undeclare_token builders added: each emits [header(MID 0x03/0x05/0x07), VLE(id)] mirroring _z_undecl_subscriber_encode / _z_undecl_queryable_encode / _z_undecl_token_encode at declarations.c:90-130 with has_keyexpr_ext=false. AP MVP scope: the wz codec for these does not model the optional _z_decl_ext_keyexpr_encode tail (declarations.c:38-50) — SCXML stops at id. Future router cross-validation paths that need the ext_keyexpr surface extend sources/codecs/undecl_*.scxml + add _with_keyexpr builders; the no-ext contract here stays byte-stable.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_declare_final() builder added: single-byte marker (header=_Z_DECL_FINAL_MID=0x1A) per _z_decl_final_encode at declarations.c:131-135. No body, no id, no ext — the full wire is exactly 2 bytes (N_MID_DECLARE envelope + DeclFinal.header). Reserved for the future Interest/Reply path (R121j+) that needs to close multi-DECLARE reply batches; the unsolicited DECLARE outbound path the AP MVP uses today does not emit DeclFinal.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs SessionLinkActions: 5 new send_* actions added — send_undeclare_kexpr / send_undeclare_subscriber / send_undeclare_queryable / send_undeclare_token / send_declare_final. All hard-coded to the reliable channel for the same SN-window ordering reason as the DECLARE outbound path: the peer must observe the retraction before any later Push that still aliases the retracted id, otherwise the peer dispatches to the stale entry.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests: 5 new unit tests covering the 5 builders. build_undeclare_{kexpr,subscriber,queryable,token}_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes each lock 2 vectors: id=42 (single-byte VLE) + id=200 (multi-byte VLE boundary, encodes as 0xC8 0x01 — the 7-bit VLE boundary). build_declare_final_emits_two_byte_marker pins the stateless [0x1E, 0x1A] shape. Each test also includes an inner-arm sanity check so a future codegen drift on the DeclareVariant arm dispatch surfaces immediately.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib -- build_undeclare build_declare_final: all 5 new tests pass. The byte-compare gates confirm the 9 emit shapes (4 Undecl_* x 2 vectors + DeclFinal x 1) match zenoh-pico's reference encoders byte-for-byte; the multi-byte VLE boundary vector (id=200) protects against any future regression on the VLE writer's continuation-bit emit logic.
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: all 49 lib tests green (44 prior + 5 new R121i-c); no regression on the existing R121g/R121i-a/R121i-b byte-compare gates.
bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). No new clippy warnings under Layer C2 (--deny warnings); warnings=deny workspace policy preserved.
validate_workspace baseline: docs=12/12, T1 orphan=0 (no new), round-trip mandatory 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync; atomic ledger entries advance 164 -> 165, sections=214 unchanged.
carry_forward_bullets: R121i cluster fully closed: 7/7 DECLARE outbound variants land with builders + send actions + byte-compare gates. Wire-codec catalog: 88%+ (was 82% post-R121i-a). Coverage matrix: DeclKexpr (R121g), DeclSubscriber (R121i-a), DeclQueryable + DeclToken (R121i-b), UndeclKexpr + UndeclSubscriber + UndeclQueryable + UndeclToken + DeclFinal (R121i-c). All anchored to zenoh-pico declarations.c references.

Next-round candidates ranked by debt-priority: (1) R121h-perf zero-alloc encode debt repayment (1 round, Option A wz fast-path vs Option B SCE upstream encode_into proposal); (2) R121j Query/Reply chain (4-6 rounds, AP MVP's last large area, raises Query/Reply 10% -> 100%); (3) R123b-pre + R123b deploy yaml flat -> nested topology schema (2 rounds). Recommendation: R121h-perf first since it is short and unblocks production-perf gating; R121j second.

Undecl_* ext-keyexpr tail still uncovered: when zenoh router cross-validation paths surface (peer-of-peer scenarios where the retraction needs the keyexpr proof), extend sources/codecs/undecl_subscriber.scxml + undecl_queryable.scxml + undecl_token.scxml with the optional ext_keyexpr field, then add _with_keyexpr builder variants. Out of scope for AP MVP.

Nonlocal arm silent gap (R121i-a/b/c): all DECLARE builders are fixed to WireexprLocal default. Interest/Reply paths in R121j may need Nonlocal coverage when the referenced mapping is peer-declared rather than local-declared; audit + extend builders with a Nonlocal arm parameter at that point.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_undeclare_kexpr(mapping_id) builder added: emits [header(_Z_UNDECL_KEXPR_MID=0x01), VLE(mapping_id)] mirroring zenoh-pico _z_undecl_kexpr_encode at declarations.c:86-89. No wireexpr body, no Z ext surface (unlike the other three Undecl_* variants); the retraction is purely id-based because the peer already has the (id -> keyexpr) entry from a prior DeclKexpr.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_undeclare_subscriber / build_undeclare_queryable / build_undeclare_token builders added: each emits [header(MID 0x03/0x05/0x07), VLE(id)] mirroring _z_undecl_subscriber_encode / _z_undecl_queryable_encode / _z_undecl_token_encode at declarations.c:90-130 with has_keyexpr_ext=false. AP MVP scope: the wz codec for these does not model the optional _z_decl_ext_keyexpr_encode tail (declarations.c:38-50) — SCXML stops at id. Future router cross-validation paths that need the ext_keyexpr surface extend sources/codecs/undecl_*.scxml + add _with_keyexpr builders; the no-ext contract here stays byte-stable.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_declare_final() builder added: single-byte marker (header=_Z_DECL_FINAL_MID=0x1A) per _z_decl_final_encode at declarations.c:131-135. No body, no id, no ext — the full wire is exactly 2 bytes (N_MID_DECLARE envelope + DeclFinal.header). Reserved for the future Interest/Reply path (R121j+) that needs to close multi-DECLARE reply batches; the unsolicited DECLARE outbound path the AP MVP uses today does not emit DeclFinal.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs SessionLinkActions: 5 new send_* actions added — send_undeclare_kexpr / send_undeclare_subscriber / send_undeclare_queryable / send_undeclare_token / send_declare_final. All hard-coded to the reliable channel for the same SN-window ordering reason as the DECLARE outbound path: the peer must observe the retraction before any later Push that still aliases the retracted id, otherwise the peer dispatches to the stale entry.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests: 5 new unit tests covering the 5 builders. build_undeclare_{kexpr,subscriber,queryable,token}_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes each lock 2 vectors: id=42 (single-byte VLE) + id=200 (multi-byte VLE boundary, encodes as 0xC8 0x01 — the 7-bit VLE boundary). build_declare_final_emits_two_byte_marker pins the stateless [0x1E, 0x1A] shape. Each test also includes an inner-arm sanity check so a future codegen drift on the DeclareVariant arm dispatch surfaces immediately.
publishable_verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib -- build_undeclare build_declare_final: all 5 new tests pass. The byte-compare gates confirm the 9 emit shapes (4 Undecl_* x 2 vectors + DeclFinal x 1) match zenoh-pico's reference encoders byte-for-byte; the multi-byte VLE boundary vector (id=200) protects against any future regression on the VLE writer's continuation-bit emit logic.
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: all 49 lib tests green (44 prior + 5 new R121i-c); no regression on the existing R121g/R121i-a/R121i-b byte-compare gates.
bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). No new clippy warnings under Layer C2 (--deny warnings); warnings=deny workspace policy preserved.
validate_workspace baseline: docs=12/12, T1 orphan=0 (no new), round-trip mandatory 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync; atomic ledger entries advance 164 -> 165, sections=214 unchanged.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: R121i cluster fully closed: 7/7 DECLARE outbound variants land with builders + send actions + byte-compare gates. Wire-codec catalog: 88%+ (was 82% post-R121i-a). Coverage matrix: DeclKexpr (R121g), DeclSubscriber (R121i-a), DeclQueryable + DeclToken (R121i-b), UndeclKexpr + UndeclSubscriber + UndeclQueryable + UndeclToken + DeclFinal (R121i-c). All anchored to zenoh-pico declarations.c references.

Next-round candidates ranked by debt-priority: (1) R121h-perf zero-alloc encode debt repayment (1 round, Option A wz fast-path vs Option B SCE upstream encode_into proposal); (2) R121j Query/Reply chain (4-6 rounds, AP MVP's last large area, raises Query/Reply 10% -> 100%); (3) R123b-pre + R123b deploy yaml flat -> nested topology schema (2 rounds). Recommendation: R121h-perf first since it is short and unblocks production-perf gating; R121j second.

Undecl_* ext-keyexpr tail still uncovered: when zenoh router cross-validation paths surface (peer-of-peer scenarios where the retraction needs the keyexpr proof), extend sources/codecs/undecl_subscriber.scxml + undecl_queryable.scxml + undecl_token.scxml with the optional ext_keyexpr field, then add _with_keyexpr builder variants. Out of scope for AP MVP.

Nonlocal arm silent gap (R121i-a/b/c): all DECLARE builders are fixed to WireexprLocal default. Interest/Reply paths in R121j may need Nonlocal coverage when the referenced mapping is peer-declared rather than local-declared; audit + extend builders with a Nonlocal arm parameter at that point.

Round 121i-d.decision_summary: R121i-d closes the WireexprNonlocal-arm silent gap for DECLARE builders by adding three companion builders (subscriber / queryable / token), keeping the existing Local-arm builders untouched.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `use wz_codecs::wireexpr_nonlocal::WireexprNonlocal;` import.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `build_declare_subscriber_nonlocal` / `build_declare_queryable_nonlocal` / `build_declare_token_nonlocal` (post-R121i-b `build_declare_token`). Same `(id, mapping_id, suffix)` signature as the Local-arm builders; inner `Wireexpr` arm flipped to `WireexprNonlocal`; codegen-derived M bit (0x00 vs Local's 0x40 — verified at `wz-codecs-*/out/decl_subscriber.rs` line 134's `_derived_header` match). `build_declare_kexpr` deliberately has no Nonlocal companion — DeclKexpr's encoder-perspective locality is by construction Local (sender installs the (id, literal) pair into its OWN mapping table).

Each `_nonlocal` builder panics on `keyexpr_mapping_id == 0` mirroring `build_declare_kexpr_rejects_zero_mapping_id`. Rationale: zenoh-pico's `_Z_KEYEXPR_MAPPING_LOCAL` sentinel is `(uintptr_t)0` (core.h:151), so a remote-mapped id=0 refers to the peer's literal-sentinel slot which carries no table entry — the literal case is only representable in the Local arm via `build_declare_subscriber(_, 0, Some(s))`.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added six unit tests in the `tests` mod (one byte-compare + one panic-rejection per builder). Each byte-compare locks alias (mapping_id=7, no suffix) / composite (mapping_id=7 + Some("abc")) / multi-byte VLE boundary (mapping_id=200, no suffix) — three vectors per builder. Locked wire bytes: 0x02 / 0x22 / 0x02 (Nonlocal subscriber header), 0x04 / 0x24 / 0x04 (queryable), 0x06 / 0x26 / 0x06 (token) — all with the M bit clear (Local-arm counterparts emit 0x42 / 0x62, 0x44 / 0x64, 0x46 / 0x66).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — fixed three pre-existing `clippy::doc_overindented_list_items` errors in the R121j-1 `build_request_query_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes` doc comment (lines 5139, 5142, 5143) by realigning the bullet continuation lines from 7-space indent to 5-space content-aligned indent. These only surfaced once `--all-targets` was added to the clippy invocation locally; the prior session's `cargo clippy` runs did not include test docs.

Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` — 60 passed, 0 failed (up from 54; +6 R121i-d tests).
Layer C1 equivalent: `cargo test --workspace` — 6 suites ok, 0 failed.
Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean (after the R121j-1 doc reindent fix above).

Round 121j-1.decision_summary: R121j-1 opens the Query/Reply chain — Request(Query) outbound — with build_request_query + SessionLinkActions::send_request_query + encode_frame_with_request (PUSH/DECLARE envelope wrapper mirror), each anchored to zenoh-pico _z_request_encode + _z_query_encode references and gated by 3-vector wire-byte regression tests. AP MVP minimal shape only (no consolidation / params / Query-level exts / Request-level exts); layered helpers for consolidation / params / target / budget / timeout_ms land in follow-up R121j sub-rounds.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs imports: replace `use wz_codecs::request::Request;` with `use wz_codecs::request::{Request, RequestVariant};` (the variant enum is needed by the builder) and add `use wz_codecs::query::Query;` for the inner body type.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_request_query(rid, keyexpr_mapping_id, keyexpr_suffix) builder added next to build_declare_final. Emits the minimal AP MVP shape: Request.header = _Z_MID_N_REQUEST(0x1C) | optional N(0x20) suffix gate, with M(0x40) codegen-derived from the WireexprLocal arm via the same <sce:variant-dispatch flag=header.M> mechanism the DECLARE builders use. Inner Query.header = _Z_MID_Z_QUERY(0x03) only — no C(consolidation) / P(params) / Z(exts) flags. Doc comment enumerates the three layered follow-up helpers (build_request_query_with_consolidation / _with_parameters / _with_exts) that future rounds will add.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs encode_frame_with_request(sn, request, reliable) helper added: composes the one transport-envelope header byte that lives outside the body codec's scope (parent_flags | T_MID_FRAME) with Frame {sn, payload: request.encode()}.encode() body. Mirror of encode_frame_with_push (R121e) and encode_frame_with_declare (R121g); the reliable-channel default matches the DECLARE outbound contract (SN-window ordering ensures the peer sees the Query before any Response/ResponseFinal that depends on the rid).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs SessionLinkActions::send_request_query(rid, mapping_id, suffix) action added: build_request_query -> next_outbound_frame_sn -> encode_frame_with_request -> driver.send_blocking(Reliable). Same SN-window ordering reason as the DECLARE outbound path; unreliable Query is unsupported by this minimal builder because the local z_get future would hang on a dropped Query.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests: three new unit tests. build_request_query_wraps_query_in_request_envelope exercises the 3 semantic cases (alias / composite / literal) at struct-shape level, verifying the minimal-shape Query fields (consolidation/params/exts all None). build_request_query_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes is the wire-byte regression gate — 3-vector byte-for-byte compare against zenoh-pico network.c:114-169 + message.c:394-451, pinning the alias [0x5C 0x2A 0x07 0x03] / composite [0x7C 0x2A 0x07 0x03 'abc' 0x03] / literal [0x7C 0x2A 0x00 0x09 'demo/test' 0x03] shapes. encode_frame_with_request_wraps_request_in_frame_envelope validates the Frame envelope wrap mirrors encode_frame_with_push/declare for both reliable and best-effort parent-flag paths.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib -- build_request_query encode_frame_with_request: all 3 new tests pass. The byte-compare gate confirms the 3 emit shapes match zenoh-pico's _z_request_encode + _z_query_encode byte-for-byte; any future codegen drift on Request header derivation, wireexpr arm selection, Query MID emission, or the Z/M/N flag dispatch surfaces immediately.
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: all 52 lib tests green (49 prior + 3 new R121j-1); no regression on the R121g/R121i-a/b/c byte-compare gates.
bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). No new clippy warnings under Layer C2; warnings=deny workspace policy preserved.
validate_workspace baseline: docs=12/12, T1 orphan=0 (no new), round-trip mandatory 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync; atomic ledger entries advance 165 -> 166, sections=214 unchanged.
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-2 next: inbound Request(Query) parser + dispatch. zenoh-pico _z_request_decode (network.c:209-238) is the reference. The wz Request codec's generated decode() already handles the byte parse; R121j-2 wires the decoded Request into the on_query callback path on the receive side. Question to resolve: where does the on_query callback registration live in wz — SessionLinkActions or a new QueryableTable owned by the session?

R121j-3 next: Response(Reply) outbound. zenoh-pico _z_response_encode (network.c:241-321) + _z_reply_encode (message.c reply path). Should be symmetrical to Request(Query) but with the inner body being Reply instead of Query. Reply body carries the actual payload (z_value) so this round wires up the value codec too.

R121j-4 next: Response(Err) + ResponseFinal outbound. ResponseFinal terminates a multi-reply sequence so the local z_get future can resolve. Inbound dispatch for Reply / Err / ResponseFinal on rid follows R121j-5.

Layered Query helpers for full Z_FEATURE_QUERY support: build_request_query_with_consolidation(mode) + _with_parameters(params) + _with_exts(body/info/attachment). Each layered helper extends the byte-compare contract with its own vectors. Out of scope for R121j-1 minimal shape but needed for AP MVP closure.

Query exts (body/info/attachment) require the ExtEntry / Value codec wiring — verify these codec modules are already published by wz-codecs (lib.rs:98-103 lists ext_entry + ext_envelope; check Value too) before R121j-3 enters.

Round 121j-1a.decision_summary: R121j-1a adds the consolidation layered helper on top of R121j-1's minimal Request(Query) builder, paying down the first quarter of the Q-helpers carry without touching the inbound path.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `pub enum ConsolidationMode { None, Monotonic, Latest }` mirroring zenoh-pico's `z_consolidation_mode_t` (constants.h:185-187) for the three transmitted values. `Z_CONSOLIDATION_MODE_AUTO = -1` is intentionally NOT representable since zenoh-pico's encoder treats AUTO as the `do not transmit` sentinel — callers wanting that case use plain `build_request_query`.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `ConsolidationMode::wire_byte(self) -> u8` const fn returning 0/1/2 per the zenoh-pico enum integer values. Const-fn so the mapping is verifiable at compile time.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `build_request_query_with_consolidation(rid, mapping_id, suffix, consolidation)`. Built on top of `build_request_query` (calls it then mutates the inner `Query` body via the `RequestVariant::CodecZenohQuery` arm match): ORs Q_C(0x20) into `Query.header` and sets `Query.consolidation = Some(consolidation.wire_byte())`. The 1-byte consolidation field then emits at offset 4 of the Request wire shape (after the existing 4-byte minimal-shape prefix). No params, no exts — those are separate layered helpers (R121j-1b/c/d carry).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `ConsolidationMode::wire_byte(self) -> u8` const fn returning 0/1/2 per the zenoh-pico enum integer values. Const-fn so the mapping is verifiable at compile time.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `build_request_query_with_consolidation(rid, mapping_id, suffix, consolidation)`. Round 121j-1b.decision_summary: R121j-1b adds the parameters layered helper, paying down the second quarter of the Q-helpers carry (R121j-1a closed consolidation; this closes parameters).
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `pub const REQUEST_QUERY_PARAMETERS_MAX_LEN: usize = 256` mirroring sources/codecs/query.scxml's `sce:max-size="256"` on the parameters field.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `build_request_query_with_parameters(rid, mapping_id, suffix, params)`. Built on top of `build_request_query` (same one-shot composition pattern as R121j-1a): ORs Q_P(0x40) into `Query.header`, sets `parameters_len = Some(params.len() as u64)` and `parameters = Some(params.to_vec())`. The codec gates both `parameters_len` and `parameters` fields on `header.P` (query.scxml line 79 / 81), so the flag + paired Optional fields stay byte-coherent.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added two assertion gates. `assert!(!params.is_empty(), ...)` rejects the empty-slice case (zenoh-pico's `has_params` predicate at message.c:398 is `_z_slice_check && len > 0`, so an empty slice would silently clear Q_P and emit no params — the caller's intent is then `build_request_query` no-params). `assert!(params.len() <= REQUEST_QUERY_PARAMETERS_MAX_LEN, ...)` rejects oversize at the call site rather than at the peer's decoder.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added three unit tests in the `tests` mod. Byte-compare locks small-params (k=v) + multi-byte VLE boundary (params_len=128 → 0x80 0x01) + max-size (256-byte payload → VLE 0x80 0x02). Two `#[should_panic]` tests guard the empty-slice and over-max rejections.

Out of scope (audit-traced carry): `_implicit_anyke` selector convention (zenoh-pico message.c:414-425 prepends `_Z_QUERY_PARAMS_KEY_ANYKE` for `**` / `?` selectors). AP MVP simple `z_get` does not set it; a future helper `build_request_query_with_parameters_and_anyke` layers the anyke-prepend when needed.
verification_bullets: Targeted: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_request_query_with_parameters` — 3 passed, 0 failed (1 byte-compare + 2 panic-rejection).
Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` — 65 passed, 0 failed (up from 62 at R121j-1a closure; +3 R121j-1b tests).

Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean after a single doc-comment rewrite (line 2609 had `> [REQUEST_QUERY_PARAMETERS_MAX_LEN]` which clippy `doc_lazy_continuation` interpreted as a missing-marker quote; reworded to `above` prose form).
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-1c/d carry intact — layered helpers for Q-level exts (Q_Z + ext_entry chain for source_info ext_id=0x01 / value ext_id=0x03 / attachment ext_id=0x05; needs Value codec wiring investigation for the value ext) and Request-level exts (outer N_Z + qos / tstamp / target / budget / timeout_ms ext_ids).

Open compositional gap: R121j-1a (consolidation) + R121j-1b (parameters) are one-shot helpers that do not compose. A caller wanting both layered must mutate the Request body manually. If 1c or 1d also need composition with the existing layers, the textbook fix is to introduce a `RequestQueryExt` trait with `with_consolidation` / `with_parameters` / `with_*` fluent chain methods and refactor the existing one-shot helpers into thin wrappers over the trait. Defer the trait extraction until composition is genuinely needed (YAGNI — R121j-1c may reveal that exts can be set independently without inter-layer composition concerns).

changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `use wz_codecs::ext_zbuf::ExtZbuf;` import (joins existing ExtEntry / ExtEntryVariant / ExtZint imports).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `pub const QUERY_EXT_ZBUF_MAX_LEN: usize = 32` mirroring sources/codecs/ext_zbuf.scxml's `sce:max-size="32"` on the value field. The wz ExtZbuf is shared across all ZBuf-encoded Query exts (attachment / value-as-payload / source_info-as-payload); a future bump would need either an ExtZbufLarge arm or a codec max-size lift.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `build_request_query_with_attachment(rid, mapping_id, suffix, attachment)`. One-shot pattern mirroring R121j-1a/1b: calls `build_request_query` then mutates the Query body to set Q_Z flag (0x80, the network-message-layer extensions-present bit at `_Z_FLAG_Z_Z`) and append a single ExtEntry with header=`ENC_ZBUF(0x40)|id_attachment(0x05)=0x45` and ExtZbuf body. M=0 (attachment is informational, not mandatory), entry-Z=0 (single-ext chain, no successor).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added two assertion gates. Empty attachment rejected (zenoh-pico's `_z_msg_query_required_extensions` only sets attachment requirement on non-empty bytes — the empty case is functionally `build_request_query` with no ext). Oversize rejected at QUERY_EXT_ZBUF_MAX_LEN=32 to surface the wz codec bound at the call site rather than as a runtime decoder error.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added three unit tests in the `tests` mod. Byte-compare locks small attachment (b"hi") + at-max (32-byte sequence 0..32). Two `#[should_panic]` tests guard empty-slice and over-max rejections. Inner-arm sanity check verifies the ExtEntry.header byte + ExtZbuf body shape independently.

Audit-traced carry: source_info ext (ext_id=0x01, body=source_id zid+eid+source_sn struct) and body(Value) ext (ext_id=0x03, body=encoding+payload pair) are scope-deferred. source_info needs the source_info codec wired (currently wz has no SourceInfo codec module — SCXML authoring required); body(Value) ext needs the Value codec wired. AP MVP client-side z_get does not need either; both are router/queryable surfaces.

Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` — 68 passed, 0 failed (up from 65 at R121j-1b closure; +3 R121j-1c tests).

carry_forward_bullets: R121j-1d carry intact — Request-level exts (outer N_Z(0x80) + Request.extensions tlv-chain for qos / tstamp / target / budget / timeout_ms ext_ids). The wz Request codec already has the `extensions` Optional field generated from request.scxml; the helper pattern mirrors this round. Each ext sub-type (qos / tstamp / etc.) is its own ext_id + body-encoding combination — likely needs sub-helpers similar to how this round picked attachment-only.

Audit-traced gap: source_info (ext_id=0x01) and body(Value) (ext_id=0x03) Query exts. Routers and queryable-side need them; client AP MVP does not. If a future round adds them, the helper signature is `build_request_query_with_source_info(rid, mid, suf, source_info: SourceInfo)` and `_with_body_value(rid, mid, suf, value: Value)` respectively. Add the wz SourceInfo / Value codec authoring before the helper land.

If a caller needs e.g. params + attachment together they must mutate the Request manually. If R121j-1d's Request-level exts also need composition with the existing layers, introduce a `RequestQueryExt` trait at that point and refactor the four one-shot helpers into thin wrappers; document the refactor in the carry.

Round 121j-1d.decision_summary: R121j-1d adds the Request-level timeout extension layered helper, paying down the fourth and final Q-helpers carry quarter; qos / tstamp / target / budget are scope-deferred sub-helpers with the same pattern.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `build_request_query_with_timeout_ms(rid, mapping_id, suffix, timeout_ms)`. One-shot pattern mirroring R121j-1a/1b/1c: calls `build_request_query` then mutates Request to set the Request-level N_Z flag (0x80, the network-message-layer extensions chain present bit at request.scxml's `<sce:flag name="Z" bit="7"/>`) and append a single ExtEntry with header=`ENC_ZINT(0x20)|id_timeout(0x06)=0x26` and ExtZint body. M=0 (timeout is informational — the peer may ignore without breaking query semantics; matches zenoh-pico network.c:152 emitting no M, unlike target ext at line 140 which DOES set M).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — doc comment clarifies the two distinct Z bits at two layers (Request.header.Z gates Request-level tlv-chain; ExtEntry.header.Z is the per-entry chain-continuation marker). For single-ext, only the former is set; the latter stays clear. Same shape pattern applies to R121j-1c's Q-level ext chain (Query.header.Z + entry-Z).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — assertion gate: `timeout_ms != 0` rejected. zenoh-pico's encoder predicate at vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/definitions/network.c:29 is `ext_timeout_ms = msg->_ext_timeout_ms != 0`, so a zero value silently clears the ext from the wire — the helper surfaces this as a panic at the call site rather than letting the caller think their timeout is being honored.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added two unit tests. Byte-compare locks single-byte VLE (timeout=50ms → 0x32), multi-byte VLE boundary (timeout=1000ms → 0xE8 0x07), and large VLE (timeout=2^32 ms → 5-byte VLE `0x80 0x80 0x80 0x80 0x10`). One panic-rejection test guards the timeout_ms=0 case. Inner-arm sanity verifies the Request-level Extensions vec, the ExtEntry.header byte, and the ExtZint.value independently — Query body stays minimal (no Q_C / Q_P / Q_Z).

Scope-deferred (audit-traced carry): four other Request-level exts. QoS (ext_id=0x01, ZINT u64, no M): priority + congestion-control + reliability packed into one zint per zenoh-pico session-level helper. Timestamp (ext_id=0x02, ZBUF, no M): timestamp_t struct (zid+time pair). Target (ext_id=0x04, ZINT, M=1!): query target enum (BestMatching / All / AllComplete). Budget (ext_id=0x05, ZINT u64, no M): max replies count. Each follows R121j-1d's pattern with appropriate ENC and ext_id; target's M=1 means the peer MUST handle it or fail the frame.
verification_bullets: Targeted: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_request_query_with_timeout_ms` — 2 passed, 0 failed.
Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` — 70 passed, 0 failed (up from 68 at R121j-1c closure; +2 R121j-1d tests).
Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean.
carry_forward_bullets: Q-helpers carry now CLOSED across all 4 layered surfaces (consolidation R121j-1a / parameters R121j-1b / Q-level attachment R121j-1c / Request-level timeout R121j-1d). Each is a one-shot helper.

Open Request-level ext sub-helpers carry (lower priority, AP MVP client rarely needs them): `build_request_query_with_target(rid, mid, suf, target_enum)` (M=1!), `_with_qos(rid, mid, suf, qos_packed_u64)`, `_with_budget(rid, mid, suf, max_replies)`, `_with_timestamp(rid, mid, suf, timestamp_t)`. Each follows R121j-1d's pattern. Target is the only mandatory ext (M=1 at network.c:140) — routers may reject queries that omit it for cross-network forwarding.

Open Query-level ext sub-helpers carry: `build_request_query_with_body_value(rid, mid, suf, value)` (ext_id=0x03, needs wz Value codec authoring — currently unmapped), `_with_source_info(rid, mid, suf, source_info)` (ext_id=0x01, needs wz SourceInfo codec authoring). Both are router/queryable-side surfaces, not AP MVP client needs.

Composition gap reaffirmed: all 4 layered helpers (R121j-1a/b/c/d) are one-shot and do not compose. A caller wanting consolidation + params + timeout must mutate the Request manually. When the next layered helper need arises (any of the deferred sub-helpers above), introduce a `RequestQueryExt` trait with `with_consolidation` / `with_parameters` / `with_query_attachment` / `with_request_timeout_ms` / etc fluent chain methods and refactor the 4 existing helpers into thin wrappers.

R121h-perf-bump-2 carry intact: blocking on explicit user consent for the atomic 12-SCXML migration + vendor pin bump. Drift state unchanged this session (vendor pin 54d513af vs SCE main de5d5e9a, +15 commits).

R121j-3 (Response/Reply outbound) + R121j-4 (Response/Err outbound) + R121j-5 (inbound parser + QueryableTable) all carry intact — R121j-1 series closure moves Query/Reply outbound from ~36% (post-R121j-1a) to ~50% (Request layer functional; Response/Reply path still missing). Recommended next attack vector once perf-bump unblocks or before it.
Round 121j-1d.publishable_decision_summary: R121j-1d adds the Request-level timeout extension layered helper, paying down the fourth and final Q-helpers carry quarter; qos / tstamp / target / budget are scope-deferred sub-helpers with the same pattern.
publishable_changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `build_request_query_with_timeout_ms(rid, mapping_id, suffix, timeout_ms)`. One-shot pattern mirroring R121j-1a/1b/1c: calls `build_request_query` then mutates Request to set the Request-level N_Z flag (0x80, the network-message-layer extensions chain present bit at request.scxml's `<sce:flag name="Z" bit="7"/>`) and append a single ExtEntry with header=`ENC_ZINT(0x20)|id_timeout(0x06)=0x26` and ExtZint body. M=0 (timeout is informational — the peer may ignore without breaking query semantics; matches zenoh-pico network.c:152 emitting no M, unlike target ext at line 140 which DOES set M).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — doc comment clarifies the two distinct Z bits at two layers (Request.header.Z gates Request-level tlv-chain; ExtEntry.header.Z is the per-entry chain-continuation marker). For single-ext, only the former is set; the latter stays clear. Same shape pattern applies to R121j-1c's Q-level ext chain (Query.header.Z + entry-Z).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — assertion gate: `timeout_ms != 0` rejected. zenoh-pico's encoder predicate at vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/definitions/network.c:29 is `ext_timeout_ms = msg->_ext_timeout_ms != 0`, so a zero value silently clears the ext from the wire — the helper surfaces this as a panic at the call site rather than letting the caller think their timeout is being honored.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added two unit tests. Byte-compare locks single-byte VLE (timeout=50ms → 0x32), multi-byte VLE boundary (timeout=1000ms → 0xE8 0x07), and large VLE (timeout=2^32 ms → 5-byte VLE `0x80 0x80 0x80 0x80 0x10`). One panic-rejection test guards the timeout_ms=0 case. Inner-arm sanity verifies the Request-level Extensions vec, the ExtEntry.header byte, and the ExtZint.value independently — Query body stays minimal (no Q_C / Q_P / Q_Z).

Scope-deferred (audit-traced carry): four other Request-level exts. QoS (ext_id=0x01, ZINT u64, no M): priority + congestion-control + reliability packed into one zint per zenoh-pico session-level helper. Timestamp (ext_id=0x02, ZBUF, no M): timestamp_t struct (zid+time pair). Target (ext_id=0x04, ZINT, M=1!): query target enum (BestMatching / All / AllComplete). Budget (ext_id=0x05, ZINT u64, no M): max replies count. Each follows R121j-1d's pattern with appropriate ENC and ext_id; target's M=1 means the peer MUST handle it or fail the frame.
publishable_verification_bullets: Targeted: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_request_query_with_timeout_ms` — 2 passed, 0 failed.
Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` — 70 passed, 0 failed (up from 68 at R121j-1c closure; +2 R121j-1d tests).
Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: Q-helpers carry now CLOSED across all 4 layered surfaces (consolidation R121j-1a / parameters R121j-1b / Q-level attachment R121j-1c / Request-level timeout R121j-1d). Each is a one-shot helper.

Open Request-level ext sub-helpers carry (lower priority, AP MVP client rarely needs them): `build_request_query_with_target(rid, mid, suf, target_enum)` (M=1!), `_with_qos(rid, mid, suf, qos_packed_u64)`, `_with_budget(rid, mid, suf, max_replies)`, `_with_timestamp(rid, mid, suf, timestamp_t)`. Each follows R121j-1d's pattern. Target is the only mandatory ext (M=1 at network.c:140) — routers may reject queries that omit it for cross-network forwarding.

Open Query-level ext sub-helpers carry: `build_request_query_with_body_value(rid, mid, suf, value)` (ext_id=0x03, needs wz Value codec authoring — currently unmapped), `_with_source_info(rid, mid, suf, source_info)` (ext_id=0x01, needs wz SourceInfo codec authoring). Both are router/queryable-side surfaces, not AP MVP client needs.

Composition gap reaffirmed: all 4 layered helpers (R121j-1a/b/c/d) are one-shot and do not compose. A caller wanting consolidation + params + timeout must mutate the Request manually. When the next layered helper need arises (any of the deferred sub-helpers above), introduce a `RequestQueryExt` trait with `with_consolidation` / `with_parameters` / `with_query_attachment` / `with_request_timeout_ms` / etc fluent chain methods and refactor the 4 existing helpers into thin wrappers.

R121h-perf-bump-2 carry intact: blocking on explicit user consent for the atomic 12-SCXML migration + vendor pin bump. Drift state unchanged this session (vendor pin 54d513af vs SCE main de5d5e9a, +15 commits).

R121j-3 (Response/Reply outbound) + R121j-4 (Response/Err outbound) + R121j-5 (inbound parser + QueryableTable) all carry intact — R121j-1 series closure moves Query/Reply outbound from ~36% (post-R121j-1a) to ~50% (Request layer functional; Response/Reply path still missing). Recommended next attack vector once perf-bump unblocks or before it.

Round 121j-1e.decision_summary: R121j-1e adds the Request-level target extension layered helper; target is the only Request-level ext with M=1 mandatory marker, distinguishing it from timeout/qos/budget which leave M clear.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `pub enum QueryTarget { All, AllComplete }` mirroring zenoh-pico's `z_query_target_t` for the two transmitted values. `Z_QUERY_TARGET_BEST_MATCHING (0)` is intentionally NOT representable since zenoh-pico's encoder predicate `ext_target != BEST_MATCHING` clears the ext on the default — callers wanting BEST_MATCHING use plain `build_request_query`.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `QueryTarget::wire_byte(self) -> u8` const fn returning 1/2 per the enum integer values (mirroring the ConsolidationMode pattern from R121j-1a). const fn so the mapping is verifiable at compile time.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `build_request_query_with_target(rid, mid, suf, target)`. One-shot pattern mirroring R121j-1d: calls `build_request_query` then sets Request.header.Z and appends a single ExtEntry with header=`ENC_ZINT(0x20)|M(0x10)|id_target(0x04)=0x34` and ExtZint body carrying the target enum value. M=1 is the textbook-distinguishing feature: target is the only Request-level ext where zenoh-pico's encoder unconditionally sets the mandatory marker (network.c:140 `extheader |= _Z_MSG_EXT_FLAG_M` is target-specific; qos/tstamp/budget/timeout omit this OR).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added two unit tests. Wire-byte regression locks both target values (All → 0x01, AllComplete → 0x02) and verifies the M-bit is set on the ExtEntry.header (`assert!(header & 0x10 != 0)` with explicit docstring about peer-reject semantics on unknown-M-bit exts per ext_entry codec contract). Enum-to-byte mapping invariant tested independently (`query_target_wire_byte_matches_zenoh_pico_enum_values`).
verification_bullets: Targeted: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_request_query_with_target query_target_wire_byte` — 2 passed, 0 failed.
Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` — 72 passed, 0 failed (up from 70 at R121j-1d closure; +2 R121j-1e tests).
Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean.
carry_forward_bullets: Request-level ext sub-helpers remaining (carry intact, low priority for AP MVP client): `build_request_query_with_qos(rid, mid, suf, qos_packed_u64)` (ext_id=0x01, ZINT, no M; priority + congestion-control + reliability packed per zenoh-pico session helper), `build_request_query_with_budget(rid, mid, suf, max_replies)` (ext_id=0x05, ZINT, no M), `build_request_query_with_timestamp(rid, mid, suf, timestamp_t)` (ext_id=0x02, ZBUF, no M; needs Timestamp codec authoring — currently unmapped). Each follows R121j-1d/1e's pattern.

Composition refactor carry GROWING (was 4 helpers in R121j-1d, now 5 in R121j-1e): all one-shot helpers reset extensions vec; cannot compose. If R121j-1f adds qos/budget/timestamp (3 more), function count hits 8 — the textbook RequestQueryBuilder pattern becomes mandatory. Builder structure: per-layer Option fields + .with_*() chained setters + single .build() materializer that orders Request-level exts (qos → tstamp → target → budget → timeout per network.c:122-167) and threads Z chain-continuation bits. Defer the refactor until R121j-1f attempt or actual multi-ext caller surfaces.

R121j-3 (Response/Reply outbound) carry intact — next major AP MVP closure round. Reply codec inspection shows it wraps a push_body (Put/Del) variant similar to Push, so the Reply outbound builder pattern can reuse `build_push_literal` / `build_push_aliased`-shaped helpers for the inner body. Reply-level header has C(consolidation) and Z(exts) flags; the wz codec's `_z_reply_encode` upstream never sets Z (no reply-level exts defined yet) so the wire shape is approximately: [Response.header | N | M | Z=ext] + VLE(request_id) + wireexpr + [Z-gated Response.extensions] + [Reply.header | C] + [C-gated consolidation] + push_body. Estimated 1-2 rounds.

R121j-3 dependency: ext_responder Response-level ext (network.c:268, ext_id=0x01, ZINT) needs investigation — zenoh-pico responses CAN carry responder zid for reply attribution. AP MVP may or may not need it depending on whether routers expect it on the wire. Audit-traced for the R121j-3 entry round.
R121h-perf-bump-2 carry intact: blocking on explicit user consent for the atomic 12-SCXML migration + vendor pin bump.

Round 121j-1f.decision_summary: RequestQueryBuilder.request_qos(packed) setter -- emits Request-level qos ext (ENC_ZINT|id=1, no M) at the head of the ext chain per zenoh-pico encode order qos to tstamp to target to budget to timeout.
changes_bullets: RequestQueryBuilder in crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs gains the request_qos: Option<u8> field + the .request_qos(packed: u8) setter. Setter takes the pre-packed byte per zenoh-pico _z_n_qos_create at vendor/zenoh-pico/include/zenoh-pico/protocol/definitions/network.h (bits 0-2 priority, bit 3 nodrop, bit 4 express, bits 5-7 reserved); the wz setter is intentionally low-level so callers integrating directly with zenoh-pico-defined constants can pass _z_n_qos_create's output verbatim

RequestQueryBuilder.build() pushes the qos ExtEntry FIRST in request_exts (before target + timeout), matching zenoh-pico _z_request_encode chain order at vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/network.c (qos -> tstamp -> target -> budget -> timeout). The Z chain-continuation loop already in build() sets bit 0x80 on every ext except the last, so the emitted chain shape stays consistent as new sub-setters layer in

qos ExtEntry header = ENC_ZINT(0x20) | id_qos(0x01); NO M(0x10) flag because qos is an informational hint, not mandatory per the ext_qos M=0 convention in zenoh-pico's _z_request_encode. Body is ExtZint { value: packed as u64 }

2 new lib tests pin the wire shape: single-setter case (header = 0x21, value = packed verbatim, N_Z(0x80) on Request.header), three-layer composition (qos + target + timeout with Z continuation bit on indices 0 and 1, no Z on index 2; qos at index 0 confirms encode-order)

R121j-1h ergonomic typed wrapper .request_qos_typed(priority, congestion_drop, express) layering over .request_qos(packed) so callers do not need to memorise the bit layout. Low priority; the packed form is already production-correct

Q-side Request-level ext-chain coverage now complete for the 4 actually-implemented setters: qos / target / budget / timeout. The 5th zenoh-pico-defined Request ext (tstamp at id_tstamp=0x02 ENC_ZBUF) is intentionally deferred -- the wz codec does not currently expose a Timestamp sub-codec on the Request ext path; layering it requires extending the wz_codecs ext_zbuf variant set
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: R121j-1h ergonomic typed wrapper .request_qos_typed(priority, congestion_drop, express) over .request_qos(packed). Low priority; the packed form is production-correct

R121j-tstamp setter (last unimplemented Request ext, id_tstamp=0x02 ENC_ZBUF) needs the wz_codecs ext_zbuf Timestamp sub-codec wiring before it can land. Defer until z_timestamp is needed by an AP MVP path
R121j-3b Reply.source_info setter, R121j-3c Reply.responder setter, R121j-3d Reply.MsgDel body variant still pending on ResponseReplyBuilder
R121j-4b Err.source_info setter, R121j-4c Err.attachment setter still pending on ResponseErrBuilder

R121h-perf-bump-4: 4 secondary encoders (InitBody/OpenBody/DeclFinal-batch/KeepAlive at session_glue.rs lines 1543/1596/1620/1645) still use inner-Vec + extend_from_slice. Apply encode_frame_envelope-style shape for symmetry. Lower priority than R121j-5
R121j-5 inbound Request(Query) parser + on_query dispatch + QueryableTable struct -- largest single AP MVP weighted jump (+5-7%p); 2-3 sub-rounds = 1 session
R121k inbound DECLARE parser silent gap (outbound 9/9 vs inbound 0/8); 4-6 sub-rounds = 1-2 sessions
SliceSink-based zero-alloc hot path deferred to a runtime API design round

Round 121j-2.decision_summary: R121j-2 adds ResponseFinal outbound to close the Query/Reply sequence — build_response_final + encode_frame_with_response_final + SessionLinkActions::send_response_final — with 2-vector wire-byte byte-compare tests against zenoh-pico _z_response_final_encode (network.c:368-376) covering both single-byte and multi-byte VLE rid cases.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_response_final(request_id) builder added next to build_request_query. Emits the minimal AP MVP shape: ResponseFinal.header = _Z_MID_N_RESPONSE_FINAL(0x1A), Z bit-7 clear (no RF-level exts), extensions = None. zenoh-pico defines no ResponseFinal-level extensions today but the wire format reserves bit 7 for them via _Z_FLAG_Z_Z; future rounds extend with an exts-present variant if a use case emerges.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs encode_frame_with_response_final(sn, response_final, reliable) helper added: Frame envelope wrap mirror of encode_frame_with_push / encode_frame_with_declare / encode_frame_with_request. ResponseFinal is unconditionally reliable in zenoh-pico's model (losing it would leave the requesting peer's z_get future hung), so the production action layer below hard-codes reliable=true; the helper still accepts the flag for fuzz / negative-test paths.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs SessionLinkActions::send_response_final(request_id) action added: build_response_final -> next_outbound_frame_sn -> encode_frame_with_response_final -> driver.send_blocking(Reliable). request_id MUST equal the rid from the matching Request(Query); callers are trusted to thread the rid through.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests: two new unit tests. build_response_final_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes locks 2 vectors (rid=42 single-byte VLE + rid=200 multi-byte VLE boundary that protects against VLE writer continuation-bit regression). encode_frame_with_response_final_wraps_in_frame_envelope validates the Frame envelope wrap behaves identically to the other three encode_frame_with_* helpers for both reliable and best-effort parent-flag paths.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib -- response_final: both new tests pass. The byte-compare gate confirms the 2 emit shapes match zenoh-pico's _z_response_final_encode byte-for-byte; any future codegen drift on header derivation, VLE writer, or the Z-flag dispatch surfaces immediately.
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: all 54 lib tests green (52 prior + 2 new R121j-2); no regression on the R121g/R121i-a/b/c/R121j-1 byte-compare gates.
bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). No new clippy warnings; warnings=deny workspace policy preserved.
validate_workspace baseline: docs=12/12, T1 orphan=0 (no new), round-trip mandatory 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync; atomic ledger entries advance 166 -> 167, sections=214 unchanged.
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-3 next: Response(Reply) outbound. zenoh-pico _z_response_encode (network.c:241-321) + _z_reply_encode (message.c:507+) reference. Response envelope mirrors Request structurally (header / rid / keyexpr / exts) but uses _Z_MID_N_RESPONSE(0x1B) and carries the inner Reply body (MID 0x04). Reply body in turn carries a value payload similar to MsgPut — needs the Value codec wiring (verify Value module is published by wz-codecs).

Response Exts surface non-trivial: zenoh-pico encodes ext_qos / ext_timestamp / ext_responder for Response (network.c:266-292). AP MVP Reply outbound likely needs ext_responder at minimum because zenoh-pico clients dispatch reply callbacks per-responder; minimal shape may be too sparse for real interop. Verify against zenoh-pico's z_query / on_reply behaviour during R121j-3 entry.

R121j-4: inbound Query parser + dispatch (on_query callback + QueryableTable). Bigger engineering change — introduces the queryable registration surface. Best entered after R121j-3 lands the Reply outbound so the queryable callback has a place to emit replies.

R121h-perf zero-alloc encode debt still on the carry list — production-perf gating, parked as SCE-upstream feature proposal (textbook path per SCE-is-priority directive). Not a wz-session deliverable.

Round 121j-2a.decision_summary: R121j-2a introduces the RequestQueryBuilder fluent composition pattern that unifies the 5 one-shot Query helpers (R121j-1a/1b/1c/1d/1e) and unlocks multi-layer composition the one-shots could not express; existing helper signatures unchanged.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added `pub struct RequestQueryBuilder` with per-layer Option fields (consolidation / parameters / query_attachment / request_target / request_timeout_ms). Fluent setters validate per-call (panic on empty / oversize / zero per the corresponding zenoh-pico encoder predicates). `.build()` materialises the Request, applying Query-layer settings to the inner Query body and assembling Request-level extensions in zenoh-pico's emit order (target before timeout per network.c:122-167) with Z chain-continuation bits set on every entry except the last.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — refactored 5 one-shot helpers into thin builder wrappers: `build_request_query_with_consolidation` / `_with_parameters` / `_with_attachment` / `_with_timeout_ms` / `_with_target`. Each is now a 3-line `RequestQueryBuilder::new(…).<setter>(…).build()` chain. Public signatures unchanged — callers see the same surface; internal implementation now goes through the builder, eliminating ~150 lines of duplicated Query mutation boilerplate.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — panic message body moved from the helper to the builder setter (e.g. `RequestQueryBuilder::parameters requires a non-empty params slice` replaces `build_request_query_with_parameters requires a non-empty params slice`). The three pre-existing `#[should_panic]` rejection tests were updated to match the new substring; behaviour (panic-on-bad-input) is identical and the max-size tests' panic strings stayed verbatim (`exceeds wz Query codec's max-size (256)` etc.) so those tests required no edits.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs — added four new tests in the `tests` mod. `request_query_builder_composes_consolidation_and_parameters` verifies two Query-layer settings combined (the multi-layer case one-shot helpers cannot express). `request_query_builder_composes_all_five_layers` is the full-stack regression: it locks the wire shape with consolidation+parameters+attachment+target+timeout combined, verifies Request-level ext ordering (target → timeout), Z chain bit on the target ext, all three Q-flag bits set together, and the Q-attachment ext positioning. Two `#[should_panic]` tests guard the builder's per-setter validation directly.
verification_bullets: Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` — 85 passed, 0 failed (up from 81 at R121j-4 closure; +4 R121j-2a tests). All pre-existing byte-compare tests for the 5 one-shot helpers pass unchanged, proving the refactor produces byte-identical wire bytes.
Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean.
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-2a closes the composition gap noted in every prior Q-helper entry (R121j-1b/1c/1d/1e all carried it forward). Future Request-level ext sub-helpers (qos / budget / timestamp) add as `.request_qos(...)` / `.request_budget(...)` / `.request_timestamp(...)` setters on the builder + matching one-shot helpers as thin wrappers. The textbook function-count growth is now bounded — each new layer is one setter, not one helper duplication.

Audit-traced carry: parallel ResponseReplyBuilder for the R121j-3/R121j-4 helpers. Currently Response/Reply has only literal/aliased variants (no layered exts), so the function count there is still 4 helpers — below the builder threshold. The carry triggers when Reply consolidation / responder / del-body / Err encoding sub-helpers land.

R121j-5 (inbound Request(Query) parser + on_query callback + QueryableTable) remains the next major AP MVP closure target. Now that ALL outbound paths (Query + Reply + Err) are functional and the layered composition pattern is established, the queryable-side request handling is the only major gap. Scope: 2-3 rounds. Recommended next session focus once perf-bump unblocks or independently.
R121h-perf-bump-2 carry intact: blocking on explicit user consent for atomic 12-SCXML migration + vendor pin bump.

Round 121j-2b.decision_summary: ResponseReplyBuilder + ResponseErrBuilder added mirroring R121j-2a RequestQueryBuilder, with Reply.consolidation setter (R121j-3a squeeze) + Err.encoding(id, schema) setter (R121j-4a squeeze).
changes_bullets: ResponseReplyBuilder struct in crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs (request_id + keyexpr_mapping_id + suffix + payload + consolidation). new + .consolidation(ConsolidationMode) + build(); build() delegates to build_response_reply_literal (mapping_id=0+Some) or build_response_reply_aliased (mapping_id!=0) for the minimal-shape baseline, then applies Reply.header.C(0x20) + Reply.consolidation byte if the setter fired

ResponseErrBuilder struct mirroring the Reply side (request_id + keyexpr_mapping_id + suffix + payload + encoding(id + optional schema)). new + .encoding(id, schema) + build(); build() delegates to build_response_err_literal/aliased then applies Err.header.E(0x40) + Encoding { packed_id = (id << 1) | has_schema_bit, schema_len = schema.len(), schema = schema bytes } per zenoh-pico _z_encoding_encode at vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/core.c
+ use wz_codecs::encoding::Encoding to session_glue.rs imports (Encoding now constructed in this file via ResponseErrBuilder)

Both builders enforce literal-path invariant: (mapping_id=0, suffix=None) panics at build() time with the diagnostic 'ResponseXxxBuilder literal path (mapping_id=0) requires a non-empty keyexpr_suffix; use mapping_id != 0 for aliased' so the caller cannot silently produce a zero-id zero-suffix illegal wire shape

7 new lib tests pin the wire-byte invariants: ReplyBuilder no-setters byte-identical to baseline aliased helper; .consolidation(Latest) sets R_C(0x20) on Reply.header + emits 0x02 at offset 4; ErrBuilder no-setters byte-identical to baseline aliased helper; .encoding(4, None) packs to 0x08 (4<<1|0) + omits schema_len/schema; .encoding(4, Some(schema_v1)) packs to 0x09 (4<<1|1) + emits VLE schema_len=9 + 9 schema bytes; both literal-path validation panics caught via should_panic
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-runtime-tokio: clean
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: 92/92 pass (85 baseline + 7 new builder tests)
cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets: clean
scripts/run-ci.sh: 8/8 lanes pass on first attempt (no flake observed this round)

Byte-identical baseline guarantee: ResponseReplyBuilder.new(...).build() and ResponseErrBuilder.new(...).build() produce the exact wire bytes of build_response_reply_aliased / build_response_err_aliased respectively when no setters fire (pinned by the no_setters_matches_aliased_baseline tests on both builders)
carry_forward_bullets: Reply sub-setters still pending and layer onto ResponseReplyBuilder via the same idiom consolidation introduced this round: R121j-3b source_info (Reply.header.Z + Responder ext on Response.header.Z chain ?? naming TBD by zenoh-pico ext id mapping), R121j-3c responder (Response.header.Z + zid + eid ZBUF ext), R121j-3d MsgDel body variant (swap MsgPut for MsgDel in the inner ReplyVariant arm so a delete-reply is expressible without dropping the builder)
Err sub-setters still pending: R121j-4b source_info (Err.header.Z + ext chain), R121j-4c attachment (Err.header.Z + ExtZbuf payload ext)
R121j-1f Request-level qos setter on RequestQueryBuilder (ext_id=0x01 ZINT no M; zenoh-pico packs priority+congestion+reliability into u8); R121j-1g Request-level budget setter (ext_id=0x05 ZINT)

R121h-perf-bump-4: 4 secondary encoder sites in session_glue.rs (lines 1543/1596/1620/1645 -- InitBody/OpenBody/DeclFinal-batch/KeepAlive) still use the inner-Vec + extend_from_slice pattern; apply the same encode_frame_envelope-style shape for symmetry. Lower priority than R121j-5 on the AP MVP weighted axis
R121j-5 inbound Request(Query) parser + on_query dispatch + QueryableTable struct (largest single AP MVP weighted jump +5-7%p; 2-3 sub-rounds = 1 session)
R121k inbound DECLARE parser silent gap (outbound 9/9 vs inbound 0/8; DeclKexpr inbound first for AP MVP alias-aware DECLARE-then-Push when wz is receiver side; 4-6 sub-rounds = 1-2 sessions)
